## 2026-05-25 PM-394 ŌĆö Premium-feel tab-flicker architectural decision: ship Option 1 (snapshot-first paint) + Option 4 (View Transitions API) over Option 3 (persistent-shell SPA) ŌĆö brain-only architectural lock

**Context.** Dean raised two pre-binary concerns this session: (1) debug surfaces currently shipping to members (Settings Reset Achievements button, Settings Force Refresh App button, PM-310 always-on live-tracker debug strip on the 8 `*-live.html` shells, `?debug=tracker` mind-video strip), and (2) the quarter-to-half-second flicker on tab-in to Home (and by extension the other hubs) where the skeleton paints for one frame before `paintCacheEarly()` swaps in real content.

**Question Dean raised.** Would bundling the app to a new iOS/Android binary fix the home tab-in flicker? Answer: no. Bundling moves HTML/CSS/JS delivery from network/SW-cache to bundled-asset (saves ~50-200ms on first byte), but the flicker is caused by the rebuild-from-scratch step every tab tap triggers ŌĆö the page parses, the skeleton element is `display:block` by default in HTML, the synchronous `paintCacheEarly()` IIFE runs, and only then does `reveal()` swap skeleton for real content. Even on bundled assets that paint-cycle is the same; the skeleton flashes at least one frame before the swap.

**Four architectural options laid out.**

1. **Snapshot-first paint.** Save a complete `vyve_home_snapshot_<email>` (and sibling keys for `body`/`mind`/`connect`) to localStorage on every state change. Inline `<script>` at top of `<body>` reads the snapshot synchronously and writes real content into the DOM **before the first browser paint frame**. No skeleton in HTML at all ŌĆö honest empty state if no snapshot exists yet (truly first launch). 99% of return visits paint complete content in one frame.

2. **`visibility:hidden` until paint.** Hide `<body>` until `paintCacheEarly()` resolves, then reveal in one go. Cheaper but worse UX in the slow case (blank screen reads as broken; skeleton at least signals "loading"). Rejected.

3. **Persistent-shell SPA.** Convert the four hub pages (Home/Body/Mind/Connect) into mounted views inside a single `app.html` shell. Bottom-nav taps swap which view is `display:block`, no page navigation, all state stays alive (Dexie connections, bus subscribers, scroll position, video players). The architecturally-correct answer. Scoped at 3 chats / 8-11 hours Claude-assisted, with real risks: blast radius of any boot-path bug becomes whole-app, four hub pages of ~2000+ lines each need ref-grabbing patterns moved from script-parse to mount-time, iOS WKWebView memory pressure with 4 mounted views + active video.

4. **View Transitions API.** Wire `document.startViewTransition` into nav.js's bottom-nav tap path so tab swaps animate slide/fade. Doesn't fix the underlying paint mechanic ŌĆö disguises it visually inside the transition. iOS 18+ only; older devices degrade silently to current behaviour. ~30 minutes of work, sits alongside any other change.

**Decision: ship Option 1 + Option 4 combined. Option 3 parked post-trial.**

**Rationale.**

- Dean's weekly Pro 20x usage at ~75% ŌĆö Option 3 burns through remaining headroom on a campaign that can't be safely partial-shipped (half-migrated nav has to handle both shell-routing AND real-navigation modes simultaneously, blast-radius all-or-nothing).
- Trial cohort is 15-20 testers, not paying consumers expecting Instagram polish ŌĆö the perceived premium-feel gap between Option 1+4 and Option 3 is roughly 80/20 (Option 1 eliminates flicker on cache-hit, the 99% case; Option 4 disguises the rare cache-miss with smooth animation; the 20% Option 3 adds is the stuff users don't consciously notice but feel ŌĆö preserved scroll position, persistent video players, animations don't reset).
- Per-chat blast radius for Option 1 is exactly one file per hub ŌĆö Home regression doesn't take down Body. Option 3 boot-path bug takes down the whole app.
- Option 3 is the right long-term answer for the product but should be done with a clear week ahead, not under rate-limit pressure pre-binary.

**The chat sequence Dean committed to.**

1. **Chat 1 (next):** Cheap-stuff sweep ŌĆö keystore + password into 1Password (Dean's 30s manual action per PM-116 backlog), `vyve-capacitor` initialised as git repo + dirty tree committed + push to `VYVEHealth/vyve-capacitor` (per PM-115 backlog item L3458 ŌĆö third occurrence escalation, do not touch Capacitor project for non-trivial work until landed), debug-strip gating across the 8 `*-live.html` shells + mind-video tracker behind `localStorage.vyve_debug_tracker === '1'`. One atomic vyve-site commit for the gating. Brain entry at end.

2. **Chat 2:** Snapshot design + Home page migration. Map every DOM element on `index.html` that needs first-paint data, define the `vyve_home_snapshot_<email>` JSON shape, add writer hooks at every state-changing publisher (bus subscribers + EF response handlers + Dexie write paths), add synchronous reader at script parse before any frame paints, remove `display:block` skeleton from HTML, honest empty state on no-snapshot. Device walk evidence required.

3. **Chat 3:** Body + Mind migrations in one chat. Both lighter than Home; patterns established in Chat 2 carry across.

4. **Chat 4:** Connect migration + Option 4 View Transitions API wired into nav.js bottom-nav tap path + final device walk polish + brain close.

**The ┬¦23.7.7 trap to watch.** Cache-first surfaces must paint complete first-paint data from local; any element sourced from a non-snapshot path (separate cache, Dexie read, network fetch) sits on placeholder text and we ship a worse flicker than the skeleton one. Each chat's design phase must enumerate every visible element on first paint and confirm the snapshot covers it. Strict discipline required.

**Open question for Chat 1.** Whether Option 4 View Transitions wire ships in Chat 1 alongside the cheap-stuff sweep (so it's helping during Chat 2/3 device walks) or in Chat 4 (so the heavy snapshot work lands first). Trade-off: earlier ships means easier device walks but introduces an unrelated change into the cheap-stuff sweep. Deferred to Chat 1 framing.

**Banked (NOT codifying solo):** ┬¦23 candidate around chat-budget-aware architectural sequencing ŌĆö when token-rate-limited, prefer surgical patterns with per-commit blast radius over architectural refactors with all-or-nothing semantics, even when the refactor is the correct long-term answer. Single occurrence so far; promotes on second recurrence.

**No vyve-site changes this chat ŌĆö brain-only decision lock.** ┬¦23.74 cross-repo PM scan at compose time: vyve-site max-PM 393 (`184e7bd3` Connect tab-in flicker real fix), brain max-PM 393.b (`7deed506` PM-393.b brain close). Claim PM-394 uncontested. ┬¦23.70 fired loud at compose: PM-393 was taken mid-compose by parallel session ŌĆö re-fetched all 3 brain files at new HEAD, replayed patches with PM-393ŌåÆPM-394 sed sweep across all entries, no content drift in the patched lines since neither my original PM-393 entry nor the parallel PM-393 ship touched the same lines (brain-only ship per ┬¦23.74's exception clause: brain-only ships claim own integer, not `.b` suffix). ┬¦23.58 fresh-HEAD re-fetch of all 3 brain files fired at compose time ŌĆö PM-391/392 already in changelog, no drift.

## 2026-05-25 PM-393.b brain close ŌĆö Real fix to Connect tab-in flicker via inverted cache-first paint ordering (vyve-site PM-393 `184e7bd3`)

**Scope.** Closes the visible flicker Dean reported on device at Update 275: tab into Connect shows 3 cached check-ins, flashes to 1 (own-only), then back to 3. Same shape on reaction tap. PM-392 had skipped the EF call on cache hit but left the own-only paint at the top of paintAll's Promise.all .then UNCONDITIONALLY firing first ŌĆö two renders per paintAll regardless of cache state.

**Root cause.** paintAll's structure was:

```js
Promise.all([dbT.allFor(memberEmail), rxPromise]).then(function (results) {
  renderRecentCheckins(rows);   // ŌåÉ FIRST PAINT, own-only, always fires
  // then nested cache check + EF branch
  kvP.then(cached => {
    if (fresh) renderRecentCheckins(cached.checkins);  // ŌåÉ SECOND PAINT from cache
    else EF fetch ŌåÆ renderRecentCheckins(data.checkins);
  });
});
```

The first render is always the own-only Dexie paint with member's check-in only ŌĆö 1 row. The second render replaces it with the community feed ŌĆö 3 rows. Visible 1ŌåÆ3 flicker on every paintAll invocation, regardless of cache freshness. PM-392's fix only addressed the EF round-trip; the own-only paint outside the cache branch still fired.

**PM-393 fix ŌĆö invert order.** Read cache FIRST, before any renderRecentCheckins call. Branch on cache freshness:

- **Path A (cache hit, fresh, no bypass).** Load Dexie reactions in parallel to overlay own-row reaction state on top of cache's server-side counts, but call `renderRecentCheckins` exactly once ŌĆö with the cache's community check-ins. No own-only paint. No flicker.

- **Path B (cache miss / stale / bypass).** Own-only Dexie paint as fallback (preserves cold-boot UX where cache is empty), then EF fetch upgrades to community feed when network returns and populates cache for next tab-in. This is the original PM-242 behaviour, retained for cold-boot and after-own-action paths.

After this ship: every tab-in within 90s of the last paintAll is Path A (single instant render from cache, no flicker). Reaction taps go Path B because `__cacheBypassOnce` is true after a member action ŌĆö so the own-only-then-EF flicker is bounded to the reaction-tap itself, not every tab-in.

**Known-remaining: reaction-tap own-only flicker.** PM-393 doesn't fully eliminate the 1ŌåÆ3 sequence on reaction tap because the bypass forces Path B which still does the own-only fallback render before the EF lands. Possible follow-on: instead of bypassing cache on reaction-tap, apply the reaction delta directly to the cached row and keep Path A. Mutate `cached.checkins[idx].reaction_counts[reaction]` += or ŌĆō=, write the mutated cache back, then renderRecentCheckins(cached.checkins). No EF round-trip needed on tap. Banked if Dean still sees flicker on tap after PM-393.

**Ship details.** vyve-site `184e7bd3`. 4 files atomic. connect.html (paintAll Promise.all .then rewritten ŌĆö old block ~80 lines, new block ~100 lines, structurally inverted), sw.js (cache pm392-connect-feed-prefetch-cache-a ŌåÆ pm393-cache-first-paint-ordering-a), index.html (vbb 275 ŌåÆ 276), settings.html (settings-vbb 275 ŌåÆ 276). 3 inline JS blocks in connect.html + sw.js standalone node --check clean. ┬¦23.41 byte-perfect verify clean on all 4 files at commit SHA. ┬¦23.74 cross-repo PM scan: vyve-site max=392, brain max=392.b, claim PM-393 uncontested. PAT-direct via Vault (Composio still down).

**Lesson banked (┬¦23 candidate, NOT codified solo).** When refactoring a render path to add a fast path that skips work, audit EVERY render call site in the path ŌĆö not just the one closest to the work being skipped. PM-392 audited the EF call and the EF-result render but missed the own-only fallback render sitting two indent levels up. Same bug shape as "you fixed the slow path but the fast path still triggers all the slow path's side effects." Two ships to land the fix when one careful audit would have done it. Banked as candidate; promote to hard rule on second occurrence.

**Verification expected on device.** (1) Force-quit + reopen, look for Update 276 in Settings. (2) Tab from home ŌåÆ Connect should paint 3 community check-ins instantly with no 1-flash. (3) Reaction tap will still show a brief flicker (1 then 3) because Path B forces own-only fallback ŌĆö that's the known-remaining and the candidate follow-on. (4) Lewis's check-ins still take up to 90s to surface on Path A; tap anything or wait for TTL to refresh.

## 2026-05-25 PM-391 + PM-392.b brain close ŌĆö Connect tab-in flicker eliminated: reverted PM-390 reaction subscriber double-mutation (PM-391, vyve-site `e98e67fe`), then added boot-time prefetch + cache-first paint for connect-feed-preview (PM-392, vyve-site `624552ce`)

**Scope.** Two surgical ships in one session closing two distinct flicker bugs on connect.html that Dean reported after PM-390 landed.

**Bug 1 (PM-390 regression ŌĆö fixed in PM-391): Reaction-tap +2 flicker.** PM-390's envelope-trusted upgrade applied an optimistic +1 to `reactionsByCheckin[checkin_id].reaction_counts[reaction]` in the `connect:reaction:logged` subscriber. But `toggleReaction()` at connect.html L1661 already mutates `reactionsByCheckin` synchronously BEFORE publishing ŌĆö increments the new reaction count, decrements the previous, sets my_reaction, then calls renderRecentCheckins. So my subscriber's +1 was a second +1 on top of toggleReaction's already-applied +1, leaving the counter at +2 for the ~150ms until repaintDebounced rebuilt reactionsByCheckin from Dexie scratch and snapped back to +1. Dean saw the flicker on every reaction tap on device.

**Root-cause classification.** ┬¦23.65 misapplication. The envelope-trusted pattern is for CROSS-PAGE subscribers ŌĆö surfaces that did NOT do the optimistic mutation themselves and need a synchronous in-memory render before the publisher's fire-and-forget Dexie write commits. On connect.html, the publisher (toggleReaction) and the subscriber are on the same page. Same-surface subscribers must trust the local optimistic mutation and just call repaintDebounced; applying the envelope delta a second time double-mutates.

**PM-391 fix.** Surgical revert of both reaction subscribers to bare `function () { repaintDebounced(); }`. Inline comment documents the ┬¦23.65-vs-same-surface distinction so future Claudes don't re-apply the same wrong pattern. Other 4 PM-390 connect.html subscribers (connect:checkin:logged + :failed envelope flag, connect:challenge:progress, connect:hydrated, vyve-localdb-hydrated, mind:logged, body:logged) untouched ŌĆö none of those have the same in-page double-mutation pattern. Mind.html / engagement-v2.html / events-rp.html PM-390 changes unaffected by revert.

**Bug 2 (pre-existing UX ŌĆö fixed in PM-392): Two-paint flicker on Connect tab-in.** Dean confirmed this had been visible "the entire time" ŌĆö not a PM-390 regression. paintAll() in connect.html is architected to paint own-only check-ins from Dexie first (instant), then re-paint with full community feed when the EF response arrives 200-500ms later. On a fast connection the gap is small but visible; on slow/cold it's the 500ms+ flicker Dean was seeing in his screenshots (frame 1: only "You" check-in visible, frame 2: "You" + "TEST TWO" + "TEST SEEDED" + others). Pre-existing since the community-feed feature shipped; got more visible after PM-381 unblocked Connect cross-page bus traffic generally.

**Existing infrastructure surveyed.** The cache slot was already there: `VYVELocalDB._kv` key `'connect_feed_preview_v1'` (constant `FEED_PREVIEW_KV_KEY`), populated by `prewarmFeedPreview()` from PM-201, used by connect-checkin.html for posted-state preview. The cache existed; nothing on connect.html was actually reading it for the immediate render. paintAll always fetched the EF regardless of cache.

**PM-392 fix shape.** Two changes that hook into existing infrastructure rather than adding new cache layers:

(a) **Boot-time prefetch on index.html.** Right after `moodShowPanel(email)` at L2403, behind a 1200ms setTimeout to keep first paint uncontested, fire the connect-feed-preview EF in the background and write the response to the existing `_kv` cache slot. Skipped if cache is already fresh (idempotent across home re-renders). Uses existing `SUPA_URL` and `jwtHeaders` from auth.js scope ŌĆö both globally available on every authenticated page. Failure modes (no auth, no Dexie, network down) all silent ŌĆö best-effort prefetch, never blocks anything.

(b) **Cache-first paint on connect.html paintAll.** Community-feed block rewritten to read `_kv` cache before EF. On cache hit (<90s fresh, has checkins): paints from cache directly via `cacheIdentitiesFromResponse` + reactionsByCheckin merge + renderRecentCheckins(cached.checkins), no EF call, no flicker. On cache miss or stale: existing EF fetch path, plus a new `_kv.set` write so the response populates cache for next tab-in. The own-only Dexie paint that fired earlier in paintAll still runs (paints the member's own check-in instantly) ŌĆö but now the community feed paint either lands from cache instantly (so no perceived second paint) or fetches in the same flow as before.

**Cache invalidation via `__cacheBypassOnce`.** Module-scoped one-shot flag declared at L1025 alongside `reactionsByCheckin`. Set true by the three same-page subscribers when the member's own action means the cached snapshot is stale to other members' eyes: `connect:checkin:logged`, `connect:reaction:logged`, `connect:reaction:cleared`. Consumed once per paintAll then cleared, so the bypass forces a fresh fetch on the next paint after a member action while passive cross-member updates continue serving from cache until the 90s TTL expires.

**Trade-off accepted.** Cross-member updates (Lewis posts a check-in you haven't seen) may take up to 90s to surface, until cache TTL expires or the member takes any action that flips `__cacheBypassOnce`. Cohort is small (~15 members) and check-in cadence is roughly daily/weekly per member, so a 90s lag for cross-member feed updates is acceptable. Dean's call.

**Ship details ŌĆö PM-391** (`e98e67fe`). 4 files atomic: connect.html (subscriber revert), sw.js (cache pm390-section65-sweep-events-rp-unify-a ŌåÆ pm391-revert-reaction-double-mutation-a), index.html (vbb 273 ŌåÆ 274), settings.html (settings-vbb 273 ŌåÆ 274). 16 inline JS blocks + sw.js standalone node --check clean. ┬¦23.41 byte-perfect verify clean.

**Ship details ŌĆö PM-392** (`624552ce`). 4 files atomic: connect.html (paintAll cache-first rewrite + __cacheBypassOnce wiring on 3 subscribers), index.html (boot prefetch added at L2403 behind 1200ms defer), sw.js (cache pm391-revert-reaction-double-mutation-a ŌåÆ pm392-connect-feed-prefetch-cache-a), settings.html (settings-vbb 274 ŌåÆ 275). 16 inline JS blocks + sw.js standalone node --check clean. ┬¦23.41 byte-perfect verify clean. ┬¦23.74 cross-repo PM scan immediately before commit: vyve-site max=391, brain max=390.b, claim PM-392 uncontested.

**┬¦23 candidates banked (NOT codified solo).**

1. **┬¦23.65 in-page-vs-cross-page distinction.** PM-391 is the first occurrence where ┬¦23.65's envelope-trusted pattern was applied to an in-page same-surface subscriber and produced a double-mutation flicker. The rule body in master.md ┬¦23.65 says "*cross-page* bus subscribers MUST NOT trust Dexie as the sole source of truth" ŌĆö the cross-page qualifier was there from the start, but the audit signal (grep for `VYVEBus.subscribe('<event>:logged'` + bare `loadX()` helper) doesn't distinguish in-page from cross-page. Future audits should add a check: does the same file ALSO contain a `VYVEBus.publish(<event>:logged'` for the matching event? If yes, the subscriber is in-page and ┬¦23.65 does NOT apply ŌĆö bare repaint is correct. Banked, not codifying solo. Promotion criterion: any future audit-driven envelope-trusted upgrade that produces a regression.

2. **Cache infrastructure exists; readers are the missing piece.** PM-201 shipped `_kv` + `FEED_PREVIEW_KV_KEY` + `prewarmFeedPreview` but only one consumer (connect-checkin.html) actually reads the cache. PM-392 added connect.html as a second consumer. Worth a periodic audit: for every `_kv.set(KEY, ...)` call site, is there at least one `_kv.get(KEY)` site that actually consumes the cached value? Orphan caches are silent waste ŌĆö populate disk for no benefit. Banked, not codifying solo.

3. **Boot prefetch as a pattern.** PM-392 is the first ship where the home page proactively warms a downstream page's cache. Same shape could help other pages with EF-bound paints (achievements, certificates, leaderboard). Bank as a candidate pattern; promote to a playbook entry once a second boot-prefetch ships.

**Verification expected on device.** (1) Force-quit + reopen, look for Update 275 in Settings. (2) Reaction tap on a Connect post should now be smooth ŌĆö no +2 flicker before settling on +1. (3) Connect tab-in from home should paint instantly with full community feed; no own-only flash before the full feed lands. (4) After posting a check-in or tapping a reaction, next paintAll should refetch fresh (bypass flag), so own actions are immediately reflected. (5) Cross-member updates (Lewis posts a check-in) will appear after the cache 90s TTL expires or after any tap on the page.

**Next.** Out of scope for this session: connect-feed.html and connect-checkin.html could read the same `connect_feed_preview_v1` cache slot ŌĆö both are downstream of the prefetch, same shape, free win on top of existing prefetch. Single-session sweep when next touching either page. Not pressing ŌĆö the visible-on-device flicker was specifically on connect.html tab-in, that's now closed.

## 2026-05-25 PM-390.b brain close ŌĆö ┬¦23.65 envelope-trusted subscriber sweep on connect.html + mind.html, engagement-v2 L1682 dead subscribe converted, events-rp.html unified onto shared session-rp shell (vyve-site PM-390 `f2f07b99`)

**Scope.** Four atomic surgical patches in one 7-file vyve-site commit, closing the Audit PM-379 queue's last two remaining items (┬¦23.65 sweep for Connect + Mind, plus P0-5 events-rp out-of-band shell). All four patches share a single underlying contract ŌĆö *publishers carry enough envelope payload that subscribers don't need to re-read storage to know what changed* ŌĆö but each manifests in a different topology.

**connect.html ŌĆö 4 of 6 bus subscribers upgraded.** The page's bus subscribers all called `repaintDebounced()` (150ms ŌåÆ `paintAll()` which re-reads Dexie for postedState + reactionsByCheckin + streak + challenge tiles), pure Dexie re-read pattern, classic ┬¦23.65 race surface against the 6 Connect publishers that all use ┬¦23.39 fire-and-forget Dexie writes.

Upgrades applied per envelope decode-ability:

- `connect:checkin:logged` envelope is `{client_id, source, focus_tag}` ŌĆö module-scoped `__optimisticCheckinClientId` is set to envelope.client_id synchronously before repaintDebounced fires. Future renderPostedState hook can use this as a hint that the posted-today state should hold even when Dexie races against the IndexedDB commit window.
- `connect:checkin:failed` envelope is `{client_id, http_status, reason}` ŌĆö clears `__optimisticCheckinClientId` when it matches, then repaintDebounced.
- `connect:reaction:logged` envelope is `{checkin_id, reaction}` ŌĆö synchronously increments `reactionsByCheckin[checkin_id].reaction_counts[reaction]` and sets `my_reaction = envelope.reaction` on the bucket. Next render reads the updated bucket from in-memory state, not Dexie. The 150ms repaintDebounced still reconciles fully from `checkin_reactions` Dexie table on the tail.
- `connect:reaction:cleared` envelope is `{checkin_id}` ŌĆö decrements `reaction_counts[my_reaction]` and clears `my_reaction` synchronously, then repaintDebounced.

Left as pure Dexie re-read by design:
- `connect:challenge:progress` ŌĆö signal/aggregate publish, no per-row counter to apply.
- `connect:hydrated` ŌĆö hydration completion signal, repaint is the entire point.
- `mind:logged` + `body:logged` ŌĆö cross-pillar aggregate signals on connect.html (drive challenge metric + Elite progress aggregates). No per-row counter local to connect.html to mutate; full Dexie re-read after debounce is the correct shape.

**mind.html ŌĆö all 3 bus subscribers upgraded with a module-scoped delta map.** Mind subscribers are interesting because `readMindActivities` does **both** Dexie + a REST round-trip and takes `max(Dexie, REST)`, so the race surface extends beyond the local IndexedDB commit window ŌĆö the REST GET has its own 100-300ms latency during which the just-published activity may not have hit the server-side snapshot yet.

Pattern: a module-scoped `__mindDeltas` map keyed by `client_id` holds `{kind, activity_date, sign}` overlays. Three new helpers ŌĆö `applyMindDelta(d)`, `revertMindDelta(client_id)`, `mergeMindDeltasInto(rows)`. The merger overlays envelope-confirmed +1 rows that aren't yet visible in Dexie/REST; it's idempotent ŌĆö once the real row lands in either source with the same client_id, `existingIds` dedupes the delta to a no-op (the delta entry stays in the map but adds zero rows to the merged result). One-line touch at the terminal `.then(...)` of `readMindActivities` swaps `return baseRows` for `return mergeMindDeltasInto(baseRows)`. All 6 Mind publishers (breathwork / meditation / sleep / visualisation / journal / affirmations) emit `{kind, client_id, ref_id, ...}` envelopes ŌĆö the kind + client_id are sufficient to apply the delta.

`mind:failed` calls revertMindDelta with envelope.client_id ŌĆö publisher already revoked the Dexie row on failure, so dropping the cached delta restores parity.

`mind:unlogged` is handled in two paths: if envelope carries client_id we target the specific delta entry; otherwise we apply a generic `sign:-1` delta. Generic decrements can't dedupe by client_id so they may linger as a -1 in the cache until Dexie reconcile catches up, but renderProgress's todayCount/streak computation tolerates that gracefully (the count never goes negative because both branches feed into the same `rows.reduce` over activity_date).

**engagement-v2.html ŌĆö single-line dead subscribe conversion (Audit PM-379 Flag 14).** L1682 was `VYVEBus.subscribe('vyve-localdb-table-pulled', recompute)`. The event name `vyve-localdb-table-pulled` has no colon ŌĆö fails bus.js EVENT_NAME_RE both pre-PM-381 (1-segment-only) and post-PM-381 (2+-segment, requires at least one colon). Subscriber was never registered, recompute never fired from this signal since the page was built. The signal is actually dispatched by sync.js + focus-shell as a window CustomEvent (every other consumer uses `window.addEventListener`). Converted in line, four-line comment block documents why.

**events-rp.html ŌĆö full replacement of standalone 17,618-byte self-rendered page with canonical session-rp shell (2,473 bytes).** Audit PM-379 P0-5 claimed events-rp was "missing session-rp.js + theme.js + session-rp.css" but inspection showed events-rp wasn't on the shared shell at all ŌĆö it was a fully self-contained page rendering its own grid layout, embedded CSS, hand-rolled topbar/sidebar, with an exposed YouTube API key in inline JS. The other 7 replay-category pages (yoga / mindfulness / workouts / therapy / podcast / education / checkin) are all 2KB shells loading `session-rp.js` + `session-rp.css` and exposing `window.VYVE_SESSION = {name, playlistId, liveLink, description}` for the shell to consume. events-rp had drifted out of band ŌĆö probably never migrated when the shared shell was introduced.

Brief's instruction to "add the 3 missing tags" would have broken the page (no `<div id="session-app">` for `session-rp.js` to mount on, layout conflict between inline CSS and `session-rp.css`). Pushed back on Dean's brief and proposed full replacement with the canonical shell template. Mockup approved, ship landed.

Four side fixes baked into the migration:

1. **Layout drift closed.** events-rp.html is now byte-equivalent in structure to yoga-rp.html and mindfulness-rp.html. Any future bug fix or feature on `session-rp.js` updates all 8 replay-category pages at once ŌĆö one less surface for future drift.
2. **Stale playlist ID fixed.** Old standalone page pointed at `PLIQ-snig8bSm7qQ2a1GFhN9U58wmkSEjm` which is not in `public.replay_playlists`. The canonical playlist ID for the `events` slug per `replay_playlists.youtube_playlist_id` is `PLyaCafiXVssiPt5whqWDiK0EVTMYxbCyh` (matches the `PLyaCafiXVssŌĆ”` family used by all other slugs). New shell uses the canonical ID.
3. **Theme support gained.** Old page had hand-rolled `:root` CSS variables and never loaded `theme.js`, so the global dark/light toggle didn't reach it. New shell inherits via `theme.css` + `theme.js`.
4. **Embedded YouTube API key removed from this surface.** The key is still in `session-rp.js` (shared across all 8) so this isn't a credential rotation ŌĆö just one less file containing it.

Description copy reconciled to the canonical text from `events-live.html`'s `VYVE_SESSION.description`. Live link points at `events-live.html`. Title preserved as "VYVE ŌĆö Events & Run Club (Replays)".

**Engagement-v2 recompute fan-in subscriber NOT upgraded (parked low-urgency).** The L1679 `recompute()` is a fan-in over 14 events (`habit:logged` / `workout:logged` / `set:logged` / `cardio:logged` / `movement:logged` / `mind:logged` / `mind:unlogged` / `connect:checkin:logged` / `food:logged` / `food:deleted` / `wellbeing:logged` / `session:viewed` / `monthly_checkin:submitted` / `live_checkin:logged`) all calling a single `refreshScore()` which is a pure Dexie re-read via `computeHomeStateFromDexie`. BUT recompute is already wrapped in a 50ms `setTimeout` debounce that serves as the ┬¦23.65 pass-2 mechanism (the fire-and-forget Dexie write typically completes within 50ms). Brief said "upgrade if pure Dexie re-read, skip if already safe" ŌĆö I read this as safe-enough-by-design. Sibling case to the `index.html` `_rerenderHome` fan-in retrofit that PM-381.b backlog explicitly parked as "low urgency, wait for user-visible glitch." Same shape, same disposition.

**Audit PM-379 queue status post-PM-390:** P0-1 / P0-2 (PM-382) / P0-3 (PM-383) / P0-4 (PM-383) / P0-5 (this ship) / P1-3 (PM-384.b) / P1-4 live-side (PM-385) / Tier 2.3 (PM-384) / ┬¦23.65 sweep on Connect + Mind (this ship) ŌĆö all CLOSED. Remaining open from the audit: P1-4 rp-side (gated on per-replay attribution doctrine clarification, low urgency at trial scale), and the two fan-in retrofits noted above (low urgency, wait-for-glitch).

**Ship details.** vyve-site `f2f07b99`. 7 files atomic via Git Data API (Composio still down per memory #8 fallback, PAT-direct via Vault per memory #20). Files: `connect.html` (envelope-trusted subscriber rewrite + `__optimisticCheckinClientId` module-scoped state), `mind.html` (subscriber rewrite + 3 delta helpers + `readMindActivities` overlay), `engagement-v2.html` (L1682 single-line addEventListener conversion), `events-rp.html` (full replacement with shared shell), `sw.js` (cache key `pm389-breakdown-tiles-dexie-direct-a` ŌåÆ `pm390-section65-sweep-events-rp-unify-a`), `index.html` (vbb-marker 272 ŌåÆ 273), `settings.html` (settings-vbb-marker 272 ŌåÆ 273).

PM-386/387/388/389 covered the parallel debug arc but none of them touched the subscribers/lines being patched here (connect.html bus subscribers L2055-2071, mind.html L1227-1229, engagement-v2.html L1682, events-rp.html as a whole). ## 2026-05-25 PM-389.b brain close ŌĆö Activity Breakdown tile counts on engagement-v2.html rewritten as Dexie-direct (vyve-site PM-389 `09c8f7d7`)

**Scope.** Dean's second device walk on test3 after PM-387 surfaced two surviving symptoms on engagement-v2.html's Activity Breakdown grid: (1) Body tile shows 1 when server has 0 workouts + 1 cardio + 1 movement (expected 2); (2) Connect tile shows 0 when server has 1 connect_checkin + 1 replay_video_view (expected 2). Score panel ("Where your score came from") rendering correctly post-PM-387 ŌĆö Mind 4pts, Connect 2pts, Check-ins 8pts ŌĆö so the JS twin score compute is fixed. The bug surviving PM-387 was that the **tile renders** in Activity Breakdown still ran on a 3-stage compose that depended on v1 home_state columns.

**Two layered problems in PM-387's loadMindCount.**

**Problem A ŌĆö Tile counts depending on v1 home_state columns that are stale immediately post-write.** PM-387's `renderScore` set the tiles from `v2.workouts_total`, `v2.cardio_total`, `v2.sessions_total` first. Then loadMindCount async-patched Mind directly + Body additively (`prev = parseInt(bel.textContent, 10) + movement.length`) + Connect by overwrite. But `workouts_total`/`cardio_total`/`sessions_total` come from member_home_state v1 columns that lag the optimistic Dexie write ŌĆö they only update after a server-side `refresh_member_home_state` call lands. On test3 right after a cardio log, `cardio_total` was still 0; movement_activities.length was 1; Body tile rendered `0 + 1 = 1` not the expected 2. Same shape on Connect: `sessions_total` was 0 (legacy column counts session_views which aren't being written anymore ŌĆö PM-294's `replay_video_views` and PM-304's `session_live_views` are where the actual writes go), and the Connect overwrite read `connectN` from Dexie which was empty for some tables, so the tile got 0.

**Problem B ŌĆö Chained-await with outer-catch swallows.** loadMindCount did 8 sequential `await VYVELocalDB.<table>.allFor(email)` calls wrapped in a single try/catch (_). If any single allFor() threw (table not yet registered in Dexie, or table missing on a partial-hydrate state), the catch silently swallowed and the rest of the tile reads never ran. The Connect tile in particular needs 4 separate tables; if any of them hiccuped, the whole block bailed.

**Fix shape.** Mirrors `renderProgressTab` (which is verified working ŌĆö drives the home page progress pills correctly across the same period). loadMindCount rewritten as a single parallel Promise.all across the 8 source tables via a safeAllFor helper that catches per-table and returns [] on miss. Body, Mind, Connect tiles each set independently from row counts:

- Body = workouts.length + cardio.length + movement.length
- Mind = mind_activities.length
- Connect = connect_checkins.length + session_views.length + replay_video_views.length + session_live_views.length

Habits + Check-ins tiles still set from v1 columns at the renderScore call site ŌĆö server-side per-week unique semantics on check-ins, no clean Dexie-direct equivalent, and v1 lag is acceptable on that surface (weekly cadence dwarfs the refresh lag).

**┬¦23.70 collision rebase fired.** Initial commit attempt as PM-388 hit a fresh CACHE_NAME of `pm388-mood-faces-lucide-a` in staged sw.js after the marker bump ŌĆö a parallel session shipped PM-388 (mood check-in faces swapped to canonical Lucide glyphs, vyve-site `4882baec` + brain `4c46df0c`) during this build. ┬¦23.70 PM-claim recompute at commit time picked up the new HEAD, re-staged engagement-v2.html (untouched by parallel ship), sw.js (cache key from parallel), index.html (vbb 270 ŌåÆ 271 from parallel), settings.html (settings-vbb 270 ŌåÆ 271 from parallel). Re-applied my patch onto the post-PM-388 content. PM claim 388 ŌåÆ 389. Final cache key `pm388-mood-faces-lucide-a` ŌåÆ `pm389-breakdown-tiles-dexie-direct-a`. Marker 271 ŌåÆ 272 on both files. Clean rebase, no patch conflicts (engagement-v2 wasn't in either ship's file set on opposing sides).

**Ship details.** vyve-site `09c8f7d7`. 4 files atomic via Git Data API (Composio still down, PAT-direct via Vault per memory #20). Files: engagement-v2.html (loadMindCount + breakdown header comment), sw.js, index.html, settings.html. node --check clean on both inline JS blocks. Byte-perfect verify on all 4 files at commit SHA via Contents API. ┬¦23.69 collision scan at session start: clean ŌĆö last 5 vyve-site commits had no breakdown-tile or engagement-v2-render keywords. ┬¦23.41 fresh-HEAD recheck immediately before vyve-site commit: parent `4882baece8732f08d99e12ab2a9b9a1e955ef890` (PM-388 mood faces). ┬¦23.74 cross-repo: vyve-site max-PM=388, VYVEBrain max-PM=388 (`4c46df0c` PM-388.b), cross-repo max=388, claim PM-389 / PM-389.b. ┬¦23.58 brain pre-commit re-fetch: all 3 brain files MD5-match the immediate-pre-commit fetch.

**┬¦23.77 candidate gets its third reinforcement and is now promotable.** PM-386.b banked the rule as "any new activity table requires touching compute_engagement_components_v2 + v_active_days + dirty-mark triggers" (first occurrence). PM-387.b extended it to "...AND the JS mirror computeEngagementComponentsV2 AND the activity-breakdown tile renderers" (second observation, but follow-up of same root cause ŌĆö not promoted). PM-389 is the third surface of the same root cause: even after PM-387 made the Body/Mind/Connect tiles aware of movement_activities and connect_checkins, the read path was still v1-column-additive and broke on stale state. **Promotion criterion met if Dean wants it codified.** Suggested final wording: "When a new activity table goes live, every read surface that displays counts derived from it must be audited ŌĆö score functions (SQL + JS twin), tile renderers, home pills, and any v1-column-additive reads. Default to Dexie-direct parallel reads over v1-column dependencies for any surface that displays post-write state." Banked here, not auto-codifying ŌĆö flagging for Dean's call.

**Verification expected on device.** After Dean force-quit + reopens app on test3, engagement-v2 Activity Breakdown should show Body 2 (0+1+1), Mind 3 (mind_activities row count), Connect 2 (1 connect_checkin + 0 session_views + 1 replay_video_view + 0 session_live_views). Numbers will update on every bus event because loadMindCount runs via wireBus ŌåÆ recompute ŌåÆ refreshScore ŌåÆ loadMindCount chain on `cardio:logged`, `movement:logged`, `connect:checkin:logged`, `session:viewed`, etc. ŌĆö with 50ms debounce.

## 2026-05-25 PM-388.b brain close ŌĆö Mood check-in faces swapped to canonical Lucide glyphs (vyve-site PM-388 `4882baec`)

**Scope.** 5 mood buttons on `index.html` (home mood check-in panel surfaced via `moodShowPanel` after first paint) ŌĆö face SVGs swapped from PM-258's hand-rolled line-drawn faces to canonical Lucide icons. Mapping (Dean's call):
- 1 / "Not great" ŌåÆ `frown`
- 2 / "Meh" ŌåÆ `annoyed`
- 3 / "Good" ŌåÆ `smile`
- 4 / "Great" ŌåÆ `smile-plus`
- 5 / "Amazing" ŌåÆ `laugh`

viewBox bumped `22 22` ŌåÆ `24 24` to host Lucide canonical geometry; `stroke-width` retained at 1.5 (not Lucide-canonical 2) to match the surrounding glyph weight Dean tuned at 30px container size in PM-258. `stroke="currentColor"` + `fill="none"` inheritance preserved so the existing `.mood-icon` CSS palette (dim-white default, bright teal on `.selected` with glow shadow) carries through unchanged.

**Zero behavioural delta.** Tap interaction, `.selected` visual hold, 350ms-before-thanks-swap arc, `localStorage` `vyve_mood_checked_in_<email>_<date>` gate, optimistic-first ┬¦23.39 write to `daily_mood_checkins` via `?on_conflict=member_email,mood_date` with `Prefer: resolution=merge-duplicates,return=minimal` ŌĆö all unchanged. This is a single visual swap, no JS touched. Closes the last bespoke-SVG island on home: every other glyph on the page is already Lucide-canonical (habit icons PM-286, bottom nav PM-376, progress pills, etc.).

**┬¦23.70 fired loud.** First commit attempt (`011be99f`) created with parent `f5d8401294` (PM-386) and message "PM-387 ŌĆö Mood check-in facesŌĆ”". Ref PATCH returned 422 Unprocessable Entity because a parallel session shipped their own PM-387 (`bf5109bf`, JS twins of PM-386 SQL fix + Activity Breakdown tile parity for Body/Connect) in the gap between my ┬¦23.41 HEAD recheck and the `PATCH /git/refs/heads/main` call. Orphan commit `011be99f` exists in the dangling-blob space but isn't reachable from any ref.

Recovery path:
1. Re-fetched all 3 staged files at new HEAD `bf5109bf` ŌĆö parallel session had bumped vbb-marker 269 ŌåÆ 270 + cache key `pm386-id-handshake-and-reentrancy-a` ŌåÆ `pm387-js-twins-pm386-body-connect-tile-a`. Mood block was untouched (their surface was `home-state-local.js` + engagement-v2 tiles).
2. Re-applied mood SVG swap on fresh content.
3. Bumped vbb 270 ŌåÆ 271 + cache key ŌåÆ `pm388-mood-faces-lucide-a`.
4. Re-claimed PM-388 per ┬¦23.74 cross-repo PM scan at commit time (vyve-site max-PM=387, brain max-PM=386.b, cross-repo max=387, claim 388).
5. Re-issued blobs (new SHAs because Git's content-addressed ŌĆö same logical content but new parent tree means new tree which means new commit which means new blob refs in the tree object, though blob SHAs themselves are content-only and would be identical to the orphan's blobs).
6. New commit `4882baec` against parent `bf5109bf`, ref PATCH clean.

Total wall-clock cost of the collision: ~90 seconds. ┬¦23.70 mechanic worked exactly as codified ŌĆö fresh-HEAD recheck IMMEDIATELY before commit caught it; the recheck-to-PATCH window is the irreducible race surface and the recovery is deterministic. No new ┬¦23 rule earned.

**Verification.** ┬¦23.41 byte-perfect post-commit verify on all 3 files at commit SHA via Contents API with `Accept: application/vnd.github.raw`:
- `index.html` 130766 bytes, sha256 `516932cbe841ŌĆ”`
- `settings.html` 134417 bytes, sha256 `66ed67ccbd32ŌĆ”`
- `sw.js` 19240 bytes, sha256 `7c88936f41edŌĆ”`

All three matched local staged versions byte-for-byte. First-100-byte comparison via Python (`urlopen` + `read()` against `open('rb').read()`) ŌĆö no text-mode normalisation hazard per the post-commit verification lesson from PM-384.

**JS sanity.** 9 inline `<script>` blocks in index.html node --check clean. 4 inline blocks in settings.html clean. sw.js standalone node --check clean.

**Tooling.** Composio still down per memory #8 fallback ŌĆö direct PAT via `vault.decrypted_secrets` ŌåÆ `bash_tool` curl. Git Data API path: `POST /git/blobs` (├Ś3) ŌåÆ `POST /git/trees` with `base_tree` from parent commit's tree SHA ŌåÆ `POST /git/commits` with parent array ŌåÆ `PATCH /git/refs/heads/main` with `force:false`. The 422 on first ref PATCH is the documented behaviour for non-fast-forward without `force:true` ŌĆö confirming the orphan commit's parent was no longer reachable from main.

**Files atomic at vyve-site `4882baec`** (3): `index.html`, `settings.html`, `sw.js`.

## 2026-05-25 PM-387.b brain close ŌĆö JS twins of PM-386 SQL fix + Activity Breakdown tile parity for Body/Connect (vyve-site PM-387 `bf5109bf`)

**Scope.** Dean's device walk on test3 after PM-386 surfaced two surviving symptoms on engagement-v2.html: (1) "Where your score came from" ŌåÆ Body shows 0pts with the copy "No workouts or cardio logged this week" even though Body is shown 1/30 on the home pill and the walk is in `movement_activities`; (2) Activity Breakdown ŌåÆ Connect tile reads 0 in last 7 days even though a Connect check-in is in `connect_checkins`. Server data was correct in both cases (the PM-386 SQL function patch is doing its job). Bug was on the CLIENT side: PM-386 patched the SQL function but missed two of its twin JS surfaces.

**Bug A ŌĆö `home-state-local.js` `computeEngagementComponentsV2` is the JavaScript mirror of `compute_engagement_components_v2`** and had the same blind-spots PM-386's SQL fix repaired. Body UNION was workouts + cardio(focus_slug IS NULL) ŌĆö movement entirely absent. Focus UNION omitted movement. active_days_7 set omitted movement. `loadV2Tables` didn't even pull `movement_activities` from Dexie. Every walk Dean logged contributed zero to the locally-computed Body score on engagement-v2 (the page reads `v2.engagement_body_points` from the cached home-state pre-loaded by this JS function, NOT the server's `member_home_state.engagement_body_points` directly ŌĆö so the SQL fix landing didn't help engagement-v2). Same surgical shape applied: `loadV2Tables` now pulls movement_activities as a 5th parallel safeLoad; both call-sites thread `movement: (v2Extra && v2Extra.movement) || []` into `computeEngagementComponentsV2`; the function reads `movementAll` from `tables.movement`; Focus block adds `v2FilterFocus(movementAll, true)` to focusByDate; Body block adds `v2FilterFocus(movementAll, false)` to bodyByDate; active_days_7 set adds `addDates(movementAll, 'activity_date')`. Mirror of the SQL pm386 patch on every line that needed it.

**Bug B ŌĆö Activity Breakdown tiles in engagement-v2.html were reading wrong sources.** Line 1418 had `$('bkBodyCount').textContent = ((v2.workouts_total ?? 0) + (v2.cardio_total ?? 0))` ŌĆö no movement count. Line 1419 had `$('bkConnectCount').textContent = (v2.sessions_total ?? 0)` ŌĆö that's the v1 column that tallies session_views + replay_views only. **connect_checkins entirely missed from the Connect tile.** session_live_views (PM-294 live broadcast attribution) and replay_video_views (PM-294 replay attribution) also absent. Fix: extended `loadMindCount` into a Mind+Body+Connect async loader. Body tile now does `parseInt(currentText) + movement.length` (additive to the existing v1 workouts+cardio total). Connect tile builds the count from scratch as the union of connect_checkins + session_views + replay_video_views + session_live_views. Mirrors the canonical Connect pillar source set per ┬¦11C in master.

**Why this missed PM-386 audit.** PM-386 brain entry mentioned `home-state-local.js computeHomeStateFromDexie extension confirmed already done on index.html L2120` ŌĆö but that confirmation was about the home pill on index.html, which has its own Body compute path (the one Dean's screenshot showed reading 1/30 correctly). engagement-v2.html reads via the SQL function (when network OK) OR the local-state JS function (when offline / first paint). Dean's walk happened to hit the local-state JS path, which had the same bug. ┬¦23.77 candidate (banked at PM-386.b) now has its first reinforcement: any new activity table requires touching BOTH the SQL `compute_engagement_components_v2` AND the JS mirror `computeEngagementComponentsV2` AND the activity-breakdown tile renderers across every page that displays them. The SQL fix isn't enough on its own. Promote ┬¦23.77 to a hard rule on next recurrence ŌĆö this is the second observation of the pattern but is technically a follow-up patch of the same root cause, so not promoting yet.

**Ship details.** vyve-site `bf5109bf`. 5 files atomic via Git Data API: `home-state-local.js` (7 surgical edits ŌĆö loadV2Tables movement load + 2 call-site threads + tables.movement read + Focus UNION + Body UNION + active_days addDates), `engagement-v2.html` (Mind/Body/Connect tile async loader extension), `sw.js` (cache key `pm386-id-handshake-and-reentrancy-a` ŌåÆ `pm387-js-twins-pm386-body-connect-tile-a`), `index.html` (vbb-marker 269 ŌåÆ 270), `settings.html` (settings-vbb-marker 269 ŌåÆ 270). PAT-direct via Vault per memory #20 (Composio still down). All inline JS blocks `node --check` clean. ┬¦23.41 first-100/byte-perfect verify via Contents API: MATCH on all 5 files at commit SHA `bf5109bf`.

**┬¦23.69 collision scan clean at session start** ŌĆö last 5 vyve-site commits scanned for `engagement-v2`, `compute`, `body`, `connect`, `movement` keywords, no parallel session. ┬¦23.41 fresh-HEAD recheck at vyve-site commit time: parent `f5d8401294be87c2baa5196e5dec1ebe5a877fe9` (PM-386), no drift on any of the 5 staged files. ┬¦23.70 PM-claim at commit time: vyve-site max-PM=386 ŌåÆ claim PM-387. ┬¦23.74 cross-repo PM scan: vyve-site max-PM=386, VYVEBrain max-PM=386 (`4537c03e` PM-386.b), cross-repo max=386, claim PM-387 / PM-387.b.

**Test3 walk evidence.** Dean ran a 4-pillar walk on test3 (1 habit, 1 walk, 1 mind journal, 1 connect check-in). Home pills all read 1/30 cleanly (the PM-386 fix verified). Engagement-v2 "Where your score came from" showed Habits 1pt / Mind 2pts / Connect 2pts / Body 0pts (the JS Body bug); Activity Breakdown showed Habits 1 / Mind 1 / Body 0 / Connect 0 (the tile bug for Body + Connect). After PM-387 device refresh, Body should show 2pts on the score panel + 1 on the Body tile; Connect tile should show the count of `connect_checkins + session_views + replay_video_views + session_live_views` rows for test3 (currently 1 connect_checkin ŌåÆ tile reads 1).

**Production cleanup status.** Server data on test2 / test3 is clean post-PM-386. Pre-PM-386 ghost rows in Dexie clear on app-data-wipe + fresh hydrate. The 2 stale `connect_checkins` backfill rows (deanonbrown@hotmail.com, 2026-05-24 20:45:31) still banked for next housekeeping.

## 2026-05-25 PM-386.b brain close ŌĆö Mind/Body double-counting class fixed at root: server scorer extended to include movement_activities (Body + Focus + active_days UNIONs), 6 missing zzz_mark_home_state_dirty triggers added to mind_activities + connect_checkins, client-side writer payloads send `id=clientId` so server keeps client UUID as canonical (vyve-site PM-386 `f5d84012` + migration `pm386_engagement_v2_movement_in_body_focus_and_active_days_plus_mind_connect_dirty_triggers`)

**Scope.** Three independent bugs surfaced via a clean test2@test.com account walk. The doubling Dean reported on home pills (Mind 2/30, Body 2/30, Recent Movement listed twice) was NOT a render bug or a `body:logged` double-publish ŌĆö it was a Dexie primary-key duality where the optimistic write keyed Dexie rows on `client_id` and the sync hydrate brought server rows back with a freshly-generated server `id`. Two rows in Dexie for every logical activity. Counter reads see 2; server has 1.

**Layered diagnosis.**

**Bug 1 ŌĆö server scorer blind to movement_activities (`compute_engagement_components_v2`).** Body UNION was workouts + cardio(focus_slug IS NULL). Movement entirely absent. Focus UNION omitted movement too. `v_active_days` UNION omitted movement. Net effect: every walk Dean's members had ever logged contributed exactly zero to engagement scoring AND a walk-only day was counted as inactive. PM-307 follow-up backlog item at PM-309 era (backlog L5630) flagged this nine days ago but never closed. PM-386 closes it.

**Bug 2 ŌĆö `zzz_mark_home_state_dirty_*` triggers missing on `mind_activities` + `connect_checkins`.** Statement-level dirty-mark triggers exist on the canonical 8 source tables (daily_habits / workouts / cardio / session_views / replay_views / wellbeing_checkins / weekly_goals / weekly_scores). Movement was added when the table shipped at PM-307. Mind (PM-173) and Connect (PM-289 era) never got them. In practice the `member-dashboard` EF or another refresh path was incidentally catching test2 (the dirty queue was empty for test2 when audited, but the home_state row had been refreshed at 21:46:42), so the symptom was inconsistent rather than always-broken. Bug 2 alone wouldn't show as the visible "Mind 2/30" ŌĆö server values for test2 were correct (mind_points=2.00, connect_points=2.00) ŌĆö but the triggers belong to the wider home-state coherence contract and were a known gap.

**Bug 3 ŌĆö Dexie primary-key duality on the optimistic write path.** Schema on movement_activities, mind_activities, cardio (and connect_checkins less commonly): `id uuid` PK with `default gen_random_uuid()`, plus a `client_id uuid` column. Writer optimistic path constructs Dexie row with `{ id: _mvClientId, ... client_id: _mvClientId }` but the POST payload sends only `client_id` (no `id`), so server's `gen_random_uuid()` fires and the row lands with `id != client_id`. Sync hydrate brings server row back into Dexie keyed on server `id`. Dexie now has both rows. Render reads count two. Census across recent rows in production: **33/33 movement_activities, 38/42 mind_activities, 13/13 cardio rows had id != client_id** ŌĆö the bug has been live for the entire lifetime of these tables. connect_checkins has only 2/8 mismatched and both pre-date the canonical writer (looks like a backfill); its writer at `connect-checkin.html:550` already passes `id: clientId` and is clean ŌĆö explicitly NOT touched in this ship.

**Bug 4 (parallel finding, separate fix) ŌĆö genuine double-server-rows from iOS double-tap on movement + cardio + affirmations.** SQL hunt for near-duplicate rows (same member, same kind, within 30s) surfaced test1@test.com with: 2 movement rows 3s apart (mobility, different client_ids); 2 cardio rows ~25-28s apart; 8 affirmation rows over 12s; multiple journal/breathwork pairs 4-8s apart. Different client_ids means the writer function ran from scratch twice ŌĆö not a network retry, two genuine button taps each generating a fresh UUID. Root cause: `btn.disabled = true` is unreliable in iOS WKWebView ŌĆö the disabled paint can lag the next event by enough frames to admit a second tap. Existing guards (`if (sessionCompleted) return` in movement.html `markDone`) protect a *subsequent* tap minutes later but not a 100-300ms double-fire. Affirmations has a separate per-day-cap design issue and is deliberately not in scope this ship.

**Code surfaces in scope this ship.**

Server (migration `pm386_engagement_v2_movement_in_body_focus_and_active_days_plus_mind_connect_dirty_triggers`):
- `compute_engagement_components_v2(p_member_email text)` rewritten ŌĆö Body UNION adds `movement_activities WHERE focus_slug IS NULL`. Focus UNION adds `movement_activities WHERE focus_slug IS NOT NULL` (closes the focus-tagged walk = 0pts gap). `v_active_days` UNION adds `movement_activities` (regardless of focus_slug) so walk-only days count toward consistency multiplier.
- New triggers on `mind_activities`: `zzz_mark_home_state_dirty_ins` (AFTER INSERT, REFERENCING NEW TABLE AS new_table, FOR EACH STATEMENT), `zzz_mark_home_state_dirty_upd` (AFTER UPDATE, NEW + OLD tables), `zzz_mark_home_state_dirty_del` (AFTER DELETE, OLD table). Identical shape on `connect_checkins`. All point to existing functions `tg_mark_home_state_dirty_{ins,upd,del}` in public.

Client (vyve-site PM-386 `f5d84012`, 11 files atomic via Git Data API):
- **`id: clientId` added to POST payload** on journal.html (line ~778), meditation.html (~484), visualisation.html (~484), sleep.html (~484), breathwork.html (~1704), affirmations.html (~799), movement.html `markDone` (~622) + `logMovement` (~902), cardio.html (~882). Eliminates Dexie row duality permanently.
- **Module-level reentrancy flag** added on movement.html `markDone` (`_markDoneInFlight`, cleared after the "Session Complete" UI flip), movement.html `logMovement` (`_logMovementInFlight`, cleared in the 2200ms button-reset setTimeout), cardio.html `logCardio` (`_logCardioInFlight`, cleared in its button-reset setTimeout). Synchronous flag check at function entry, before any async work ŌĆö closes the WKWebView disabled-paint race.
- Affirmations payload also gets `id: clientId` (Dexie doubling fix) ŌĆö its per-day-cap design issue stays parked for separate follow-up. Connect-checkin not modified ŌĆö already clean.

**Verification.**

Server: `SELECT public.refresh_member_home_state('test2@test.com');` followed by `SELECT * FROM member_home_state WHERE member_email='test2@test.com'`. Pre-ship: `engagement_body_points=0.00, engagement_score_v2=53, engagement_pillars_touched_7=3`. Post-ship: `engagement_body_points=2.00, engagement_mind_points=2.00, engagement_connect_points=2.00, engagement_habits_points=4.00, engagement_score_v2=54, engagement_pillars_touched_7=4`. The movement walk now credits Body correctly. Mind + Connect untouched (server values were already correct ŌĆö Bug 3 was the symptom for those on the home pill, not Bug 1).

Triggers: `SELECT c.relname, t.tgname FROM pg_trigger t JOIN pg_class c ON c.oid=t.tgrelid WHERE c.relname IN ('mind_activities','connect_checkins') AND t.tgname LIKE 'zzz_mark_home_state_dirty_%'` returns 6 rows (3 per table). Confirmed.

Client byte-perfect verify at commit SHA `f5d84012` via Contents API on all 11 files: MATCH on every file. `node --check` clean on every modified inline JS block across the 8 writer HTMLs (2/4/2/4/4/2/2/3 blocks).

**┬¦23 candidates earned (3) ŌĆö NOT codifying solo, all banked pending recurrence.**

**┬¦23.75 candidate** (first occurrence): server tables with client-side optimistic-write paths should accept client-supplied `id` rather than generating their own when `client_id` is also present. The dual-column shape (id PK + client_id column) is an anti-pattern unless the writer explicitly POSTs `id=client_id` ŌĆö otherwise Dexie's PK and server's PK diverge and every optimistic-first surface accumulates ghost rows on hydrate. Codify as a hard rule after the next independent occurrence (most likely the next new activity table with the same shape).

**┬¦23.76 candidate** (first occurrence): `btn.disabled = true` is not sufficient reentrancy protection in iOS WKWebView. Every writer function with a primary action button needs a module-level boolean in-flight flag checked synchronously at function entry, before any DOM or async work. Codify after second writer hits the same double-tap-different-UUIDs pattern in production.

**┬¦23.77 candidate** (first occurrence): when adding a new activity table to the platform, the migration must touch `compute_engagement_components_v2` (Body / Focus / Mind / Connect / Check-ins UNION as appropriate) AND `v_active_days` UNION AND the `zzz_mark_home_state_dirty_{ins,upd,del}` triggers (statement-level shape, REFERENCING NEW/OLD TABLE pattern, calling existing public.tg_mark_home_state_dirty_* functions). PM-307 movement_activities ship missed all three; PM-173 mind_activities + PM-289 connect_checkins each missed the triggers. Three independent occurrences makes this the strongest ┬¦23.77 candidate ŌĆö promote to hard rule on the next new activity table that goes live without the migration touching these surfaces.

**Tooling discipline.** ┬¦23.69 collision scan at session start: clean (last 8 vyve-site commits showed no parallel work on engagement scorer, mind/connect/movement triggers, or any of the 8 writer surfaces). ┬¦23.41 fresh-HEAD recheck immediately before vyve-site `POST /git/commits`: HEAD `abd2faddda1f71c42f459978eb95d8d8d951bd63` (PM-385) unchanged since session start. ┬¦23.66 content rebase: zero drift on any of the 11 staged files between session start and commit time. ┬¦23.70 PM-claim recompute at commit time: vyve-site max-PM 385, claim PM-386. ┬¦23.74 cross-repo PM-claim: vyve-site max-PM 385, VYVEBrain max-PM 385 ŌĆö cross-repo max 385 ŌåÆ claim PM-386 in vyve-site + PM-386.b in brain. ┬¦23.58 pre-brain-commit re-fetch: all 3 brain files MD5-match session-start fetch, no parallel brain commits during this session.

**Production cleanup deferred.** Existing duplicate Dexie rows on Dean's iPhone and other devices need to be cleared once via app data wipe + fresh hydrate ŌĆö only the in-app side has the duplicates, server is clean. SQL cleanup of the 2 stale connect_checkins backfill rows (id != client_id, both deanonbrown@hotmail.com from 2026-05-24 20:45:31.517458) banked for next housekeeping pass ŌĆö they don't drive any visible bug, only show up in `id != client_id` audits.

**Backlog item closed.** PM-309 follow-up at backlog L5630 ("movement_activities table integration into streak/engagement") ŌĆö partially closed: the server-side scorer extension piece is done (Bug 1 fix above), and the dirty-mark trigger piece is done. The `home-state-local.js` `computeHomeStateFromDexie` extension to include movement_activities (referenced in that backlog item) is NOT touched here ŌĆö confirmed separately that the client-side Body pill compute on index.html L2120 already includes movement_activities in the Dexie-first read path, so the gap is closed on every consumer.

**Next.** Dean clears app data on test1 + test2 iPhones (force-quit, settings ŌåÆ clear), reopens app, fresh hydrate brings one row per activity into Dexie. Walk-test verification: log a fresh walk on test2, confirm home pill shows Body 1/30 (not 2/30), confirm Recent Movement list shows one entry, confirm Mind 1/30 if a fresh mind activity is logged. Then the affirmations per-day-cap issue gets its own session (single-page diagnose + fix, ~30 min). And the ┬¦23.65 follow-on sweeps for mind.html + connect.html (banked at PM-381.b L17 and PM-384.b respectively) remain on the audit queue.

## 2026-05-25 PM-385.b brain close ŌĆö tracking.js retired from 8 *-live.html shells (vyve-site PM-385 `abd2fadd`) [Audit P1-4 closed]

**Scope.** Closes Audit PM-379 P1-4. PM-251 brain entry claimed tracking.js was retired; live state pre-this-ship showed it loaded on all 8 `*-live.html` shells (yoga / mindfulness / workouts / therapy / events / podcast / education / checkin) AND all 8 `*-rp.html` shells AND sessions.html ŌĆö 17 loads total, contra the brain's PM-251 claim. Every live session was running two trackers in parallel: legacy `tracking.js` writing `session_views` with 30s heartbeats, and PM-304's unified `player-tracker.js` writing `session_live_views` with 30s heartbeats. Aggregator reads handled the overlap via dedupe-by-(activity_date, category) per PM-294/PM-304 + the wellbeing-checkin recap card, so members saw no broken state ŌĆö pure battery / data / write-volume waste on every live page. PM-385 retires tracking.js from the 8 live shells; rp shells unchanged per audit guidance.

**Scope decision ŌĆö 8 live shells only (not rp).** Audit P1-4 explicitly carved rp shells out: per-replay attribution doctrine is not finalised. PM-294 introduced `replay_video_views` as the engagement-grade per-video table, but the legacy `replay_views` table is still referenced in `refresh_member_home_state` UNION semantics. Replay-side retirement is a sequencing call (which table is canonical for replay attribution, what happens to UNION semantics, what migrates the historical `replay_views` data if anything) that this PM doesn't make. Live retirement is clean because `session_live_views` is unambiguously the engagement-grade canonical table for live sessions ŌĆö PM-294 design called it out as the live-side replacement, no legacy `session_views` UNION dependency. Rp retirement banked on the backlog as a follow-on PM gated on doctrine clarification.

**Per-shell change.** Single-line strip on every live shell, identical line 42 across all 8:

```html
<script src="/tracking.js" defer></script>
```

removed. `player-tracker.js` (line 42 in 7/8 shells, line 43 in checkin-live where ordering differs by one) remains in place and continues to write `session_live_views` as the sole tracker. No JS, CSS, or markup changes required ŌĆö player-tracker.js covers everything tracking.js did on live shells with a better data shape (per-video attribution via `session_live_views.broadcast_id` column from PM-294, accurate join semantics).

**Pre-strip audit (mandatory per ┬¦23.67-reverse).** Confirmed all 8 shells:
- Load tracking.js at line 42 (identical script tag across all 8 ŌĆö `  <script src="/tracking.js" defer></script>`, 45 bytes inc. indent + newline)
- Load player-tracker.js (precondition for safe retirement ŌĆö if any shell loaded tracking.js but NOT player-tracker.js, stripping tracking.js would leave that shell with zero tracker coverage; none of the 8 are in that state)

**Post-strip verification.** All 8 shells: 0 tracking.js references remaining, player-tracker.js still loaded. Byte deltas exactly 45b on each shell (yoga 3607ŌåÆ3562, mindfulness 3632ŌåÆ3587, workouts 3545ŌåÆ3500, therapy 3618ŌåÆ3573, events 3616ŌåÆ3571, podcast 3607ŌåÆ3562, education 3640ŌåÆ3595, checkin 3590ŌåÆ3545). Identical edit pattern = clean atomic.

## 2026-05-25 PM-384.b brain close ŌĆö checkin_questions catalogue (Tier 2.3 from PM-379 audit): schema + Dexie sync + read path (vyve-site PM-384 `aa43aa49`) [P1-3 closed negative]

**Scope.** Closes Audit PM-379 Tier 2.3 (the last unblocked content-surface audit queue item from PM-372) AND closes P1-3 with a clean negative result. Two distinct strands batched into one code commit because they're independent surfaces with no overlap and would otherwise need duplicate marker/cache bumps.

**Tier 2.3 ŌĆö checkin_questions catalogue.** Versioned slider questions for weekly + monthly check-ins. Lewis (or eventually the Command Centre) authors rows in Supabase Studio; bump `version` to roll wording without breaking historical answers (check-in submissions reference question_id from the version they were written against, not the current one ŌĆö versioning is the contract that lets wording evolve without invalidating historical analysis).

**Schema (`public.checkin_questions`, Supabase migration `pm383_create_checkin_questions`).** Migration name retained for migration immutability ŌĆö once `apply_migration` lands a name in the migration history, renaming requires a new migration. The canonical PM at commit time is 384 per cross-repo ┬¦23.74 claim (parallel session shipped PM-383 movement dual-write retire during this build). Schema:

```
id                BIGINT IDENTITY PK
slug              TEXT NOT NULL
survey_type       TEXT CHECK IN ('weekly','monthly')
version           INTEGER DEFAULT 1
display_order     INTEGER DEFAULT 100
question_text     TEXT NOT NULL
question_subtitle TEXT
min_label / max_label TEXT
min_value / max_value INTEGER (CHECK min_value < max_value)
default_value     INTEGER (CHECK NULL OR in [min_value, max_value])
active            BOOLEAN DEFAULT true
active_from / active_until TIMESTAMPTZ
created_at / updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ + trigger
```

UNIQUE (survey_type, slug, version) at the server lets Lewis roll versions without collisions. CHECK constraints on slug + question_text non-empty after trim refuse Studio rows someone half-filled. RLS enabled, single `SELECT TO authenticated WHERE active=true` policy ŌĆö no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policies, table is service-role-curated. Partial read index on `(survey_type, display_order, version DESC) WHERE active=true` for the catalogue pull ordering (latest live version of each slot floats to the top during transition windows when multiple versions are active at once).

**Sync wiring (db.js + sync.js ŌĆö eighth application of ┬¦23.49 + ┬¦23.50 catalogue pattern after service_catalogue PM-190, replay_playlists PM-235, replay_videos PM-245, mind_videos PM-367, persona_welcome_copy PM-372, how_to_resources PM-377, podcast_platforms PM-378).**
- `db.js` SCHEMA_V21 adds `checkin_questions: '&id, survey_type, slug, version, display_order, active'`
- `db.version(21).stores(SCHEMA_V21)` registered
- Consumer registered via `makeCatalogueTable('checkin_questions')`
- `sync.js` catalogue plan entry hitting `/checkin_questions?active=eq.true&select=*&order=survey_type.asc,display_order.asc,version.desc` (server-side composite sort so page-side filter is single `.filter()` with no resort)
- `CATALOGUE_FRESH_TABLES` extended (5-min stale window)
- `CATALOGUE_INVALIDATION_KEY` bumped `pm383-movement-dual-write-retire` ŌåÆ `pm384-checkin-questions` per ┬¦23.50

**Surfacing ŌĆö explicitly out of scope this PM.** wellbeing-checkin.html + monthly-checkin.html continue to render their existing hardcoded slider questions. Surfacing the catalogue is a follow-on touch on those two pages ŌĆö needs Lewis to populate at least one row first (otherwise the page would render an empty slider, worse UX than the existing hardcoded fallback). The rails are in place: as soon as Lewis adds a row marked `active=true`, devices pick it up within the 5-min CATALOGUE_FRESH_TABLES window. Surfacing PM lands wellbeing-checkin.html + monthly-checkin.html reading from `VYVELocalDB.checkin_questions.allFor()` with the existing hardcoded slider as `FALLBACK_QUESTIONS` const for cold paint, mirroring PM-378 podcast_platforms shape.

**Operational implication for Lewis.** Add a weekly slider: `INSERT INTO public.checkin_questions (slug, survey_type, version, display_order, question_text, min_label, max_label, default_value) VALUES ('overall-wellbeing', 'weekly', 1, 10, 'How are you feeling this week?', 'Struggling', 'Thriving', 7);`. Roll wording without breaking history: `INSERT` a new row with same slug + bumped version + `active=true`, then `UPDATE ŌĆ” SET active=false WHERE slug='overall-wellbeing' AND version=1`. Historical check-in submissions still resolve question text by question_id (referencing the v1 row, still in the table, just not in the live catalogue pull). Devices pick up new active version within 5 min.

**P1-3 audit ŌĆö closed negative.** Audit P1-3 asked for a ┬¦23.65 envelope-trusted subscriber sweep on workouts-session.js's publish surface. workouts-session.js publishes `set:logged` (L425), `workout:logged` (L676), `body:logged` (L692, PM-382 ship), `workout:failed` (L826), `workout:shared` (L911, L961). Only own internal subscriber is `workout:failed` at L1023 ŌĆö pure envelope-trusted by design (failure-revert path, calls `VYVEHomeState.revertPatch('workouts', { loggedAt: envelope.logged_at })` synchronously from envelope; no Dexie re-read). Cross-page subscribers for `set:logged` (6 sites via GitHub code search):

- `workouts.html` L571 ŌĆö calls only `VYVEAchievements.evaluate()` (debounced 1.5s, per inline comment NEVER re-reads exerciseHistory mid-session because nudges compare against previous sessions not today's just-logged sets). **┬¦23.65 SAFE by explicit design.**
- `index.html` L2664 ŌĆö `_rerenderHome` recomputes from Dexie. **┬¦23.65 surface ŌĆö but this is the home-state coherence pattern shared across every `_rerenderHome` subscriber, not workouts-session-specific.** Belongs to a separate home-state sweep, out of P1-3 scope.
- `engagement-v2.html` L1626 / L2641 ŌĆö both lists end in `setTimeout(repaint, 150)`. **┬¦23.65 workaround pattern (debounce instead of envelope) ŌĆö the 150ms wait absorbs the fire-and-forget Dexie commit window** for the engagement-v2 score ring where 150ms of stale paint is invisible. SAFE.
- `achievements-evaluator.js` L1301 ŌĆö server-side-style metric eval registration (not a render subscriber, reads aggregates not the new row). **Not ┬¦23.65 surface.**
- `auth.js` L155-167 ŌĆö Realtime bridge (publishes from server-side rows, fires `deliverLocal` bypassing the race). **Not ┬¦23.65 surface.**

**Conclusion.** Zero envelope-trusted retrofits needed on workouts-session.js's publish surface specifically. The only real ┬¦23.65 surface in the set (index.html `_rerenderHome` on set:logged) is part of the broader home-state sweep, not workouts-session-scoped. P1-3 closed clean.

**┬¦23.65 follow-on still open.** mind.html (6 publishers all using ┬¦23.39 fire-and-forget Dexie writes, subscribers re-read Dexie) and connect.html (3 publishers, headline ┬¦23.65 risk per audit Flag 15) remain on the ┬¦23.65 sweep backlog. Both are larger sweeps that warrant their own session per file. Per audit, connect.html sweep is gated on Chat 9 / P0-2 having landed (now done ŌĆö PM-382), so the connect.html ┬¦23.65 sweep is unblocked as the next audit item if Dean wants it.

## 2026-05-25 PM-382.b brain close ŌĆö body:logged aggregator publish from the three Body publishers (vyve-site PM-382 `124f78f9`) [Audit P0-2 closed]

**Scope.** Closes Audit PM-379 P0-2. Three Connect surfaces (`connect.html:2071`, `connect-feed.html:944`, `connect-challenge.html:586`) have subscribed to `body:logged` for months with zero publishers ŌĆö Connect tile counters stayed stale after a Body activity log until a page nav, `sync.js` hydrate, or auth-ready re-fire triggered a repaint. Now they refresh in-session the moment a workout / cardio / movement log lands.

**What shipped on vyve-site (PM-382 `124f78f9`).** `VYVEBus.publish('body:logged', { kind, ... })` added immediately after each existing per-table publish at the three Body writer surfaces:

- `workouts-session.js` L676 ŌĆö `kind:'workout'` alongside `workout:logged` (envelope carries `workout_id`, `duration_min`, `source` (programme/custom), `logged_at`)
- `cardio.html` L921 ŌĆö `kind:'cardio'` alongside `cardio:logged` (envelope carries `cardio_id`, `cardio_type`, `duration_min`, `distance_km`, `logged_at`)
- `movement.html` L910 ŌĆö `kind:'movement'` alongside `movement:logged` (envelope carries `movement_id`, `movement_kind`, `duration_min`, `distance_km`, `logged_at`)

**Critical placement detail.** The `body:logged` publish on `movement.html` is intentionally next to the `movement:logged` site at L910 only ŌĆö NOT next to the legacy `workout:logged` publish at L637 (the dual-write into the `workouts` table that PM-379 P0-3 will retire next). Adding `body:logged` to both publish sites would double-publish per movement tap, doubling-up the Connect repaint cost. The L637 path is going away in P0-3; this ship leaves it alone. Inline comment at the L910 publish site documents the choice explicitly so a future cleanup session doesn't symmetrically add `body:logged` to the legacy site.

**Discriminator shape.** All three subscribers today are unconditional ŌĆö they call `repaintDebounced()` / `computeEliteProgress()` / `rePaint` regardless of envelope content. So the `{kind, ...}` discriminator is informational, not gating. But future subscribers can filter per-strand without needing a new event name (e.g. an achievements metric that only counts cardio sessions could subscribe to `body:logged` and gate on `envelope.kind === 'cardio'`). Precedent: `workouts-builder.js` L118 publishes `workout:logged` with `source:'builder'` discriminator for exactly this pattern (audit Flag 12). Aligns with ┬¦23.65's envelope-carries-enough-payload doctrine ŌĆö subscribers should be able to decode the change from the envelope alone where possible.

**Subscriber audit before code.** Read all three subscriber handlers before locking the payload shape. `connect.html:2071` ŌåÆ `VYVEBus.subscribe('body:logged', function () { repaintDebounced(); })` ŌĆö payload unused. `connect-feed.html:944` ŌåÆ `VYVEBus.subscribe('body:logged', function () { computeEliteProgress().then(...) })` ŌĆö payload unused. `connect-challenge.html:586` ŌåÆ `VYVEBus.subscribe('body:logged', rePaint)` ŌĆö payload unused. Decision: keep the discriminator informational and ship.

**┬¦23.65 race surface acknowledged but not patched this ship.** Now that `body:logged` actually flows, the ┬¦23.65 race (publisher fire-and-forget Dexie write, subscriber re-read Dexie before commit lands) is potentially live on every Connect subscriber. The three handlers call `repaintDebounced()` / `computeEliteProgress()` / `rePaint`, all of which re-read Dexie. PM-379 audit Flag 15 explicitly named connect.html as the headline ┬¦23.65 risk. **Backlog item kept open** (was already banked under PM-381.b L17) ŌĆö extension scope unchanged by P0-2 landing; if anything more urgent now that traffic flows. Surface separately, not in this ship.

**Tooling discipline.** ┬¦23.69 (collision scan at session start): clean ŌĆö PM-381 was the latest, no parallel ship on Body publishers. ┬¦23.41 fresh-HEAD recheck at vyve-site commit time: parent `29a8760f79c82d3fc2a54e5884fe46b832a0552d` (PM-381), no drift on any of the six staged files. ┬¦23.66 (content rebase): all 6 files MD5-matched live HEAD at session-start fetch ŌĆö no parallel sessions touched any of them. ┬¦23.70 (PM-claim-at-commit-time in target repo): vyve-site max-PM 381, claim 382. ┬¦23.74 (cross-repo PM scan, hard rule from PM-381): vyve-site max-PM 381, VYVEBrain max-PM 381 (`f6ea0fc8` PM-381.b brain close, `dfd611ff` PM-379.b, `ed70eedf` PM-380), cross-repo max 381 ŌåÆ **claim PM-382 in vyve-site, PM-382.b in brain**. Novel in both repos. First clean application of ┬¦23.74 since codification.

**Atomic 6-file commit via Git Data API.** Composio incident day 4, PAT-direct via Supabase Vault per memory #20. Files: `workouts-session.js`, `cardio.html`, `movement.html`, `sw.js` (cache key `pm381-bus-regex-3-segment-a` ŌåÆ `pm382-body-logged-aggregator-a`), `index.html` (vbb-marker 264 ŌåÆ 265), `settings.html` (settings-vbb-marker 264 ŌåÆ 265). Memory #10 dual-marker invariant held. All 19 inline JS blocks across the 4 touched HTML files passed `node --check`. ┬¦23.41 first-100-char post-commit verification via Contents API at commit SHA `124f78f9ba003692e9bf929956567f27806204de`: MATCH on all 6 files. Body-logged occurrence count verified at SHA: 2/2/3 across workouts-session/cardio/movement (publish + comment + movement's L894 legacy-context docstring mention).

**Curl arg-list trap surfaced (PM-374 ┬¦23.79 candidate, second occurrence ŌĆö promotable next).** First curl attempt at `POST /git/blobs` for `index.html` (130K blob) failed with `OSError: [Errno 7] Argument list too long`. Switched to `--data-binary @file` via tempfile, retried ŌĆö clean. PM-374 brain entry banked the `>1MB brain reads need Git Blob API` candidate (┬¦23.79); this is a sibling rule on the WRITE side: `POST /git/blobs` for blobs >~100KB needs `--data-binary @file` not `-d "$json"`. Two independent occurrences (PM-374 read side, PM-382 write side) ŌĆö promotable to ┬¦23 hard rule at next ship that hits the same trap, or could codify pre-emptively as ┬¦23.75 covering both sides. Banked here, not codifying solo.

Reverted both back to `PM-XXX` so this ship doesn't mis-attribute prior work to PM-382. Two-line follow-up: forensic the git log for which PM actually shipped that haptic block, sweep accordingly. Not pressing; banked in backlog.
