## 2026-05-25 PM-381.b brain close ŌĆö bus.js event-name regex loosened to 2+ colon segments (vyve-site PM-381 `29a8760f`) [brain catch-up to parallel ship]

**Scope.** This is the brain entry for vyve-site PM-381 (`29a8760f`) ŌĆö the one-line `bus.js` `EVENT_NAME_RE` fix flagged P0-1 in the PM-379 architecture audit. The code shipped in a parallel session before this session's first code tool call landed. ┬¦23.69 (feature-level collision detection at session start) caught the parallel ship before any duplicate work was committed; this brain commit catches the brain up to the live state and codifies the new hard rule the PM-381 session earned but couldn't write because it pre-dated brain-load of the existing ┬¦23.71.

**What shipped on vyve-site (PM-381 `29a8760f`, not this session ŌĆö verified live on main).** Single-line regex change at `bus.js` L137:

```js
// OLD (pre PM-381)
var EVENT_NAME_RE = /^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*:[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*$/i;
// NEW
var EVENT_NAME_RE = /^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*(:[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*)+$/i;
```

Both `publish()` (L258) and `subscribe()` (L284) use this regex as the gatekeeper at the bus entry/registration sites. The old pattern required exactly one colon and silently rejected every 3-segment event name with a `console.warn` swallowed by WKWebView. The new pattern accepts one-or-more additional segments ŌĆö typo guard preserved, every 3-segment name now passes. Atomic 4-file commit via Git Data API: `bus.js` + `sw.js` (cache `pm379-monthly-checkin-pillars-a` ŌåÆ `pm381-bus-regex-3-segment-a`) + `index.html` (vbb-marker 263 ŌåÆ 264) + `settings.html` (settings-vbb-marker 263 ŌåÆ 264). Per memory #10, dual-marker invariant held. PAT-direct via Vault per memory #20 (Composio still down).

**Events surfaced from dead to live.** Roughly 8 events come back the moment the regex flips. Drawing from the PM-379 audit inventory:

- `connect:reaction:logged` (3 publishers, 2 subscribers) ŌĆö Connect cross-page reaction sync + achievements `reactions_given` metric
- `connect:reaction:cleared` (3 publishers, 1 subscriber) ŌĆö Connect cross-page reaction un-sync
- `connect:checkin:logged` (1 publisher, 4 subscribers + 1 achievements metric subscriber) ŌĆö home repaint after a Connect check-in, Connect hub repaint, engagement-v2 score recompute, achievements `checkins_completed` (audit later corrected ŌĆö this metric is actually wired to `wellbeing:logged` which is 2-segment, never affected; brain-narrative pre-correction)
- `connect:checkin:failed` (1 publisher, 1 subscriber) ŌĆö Connect check-in revert path
- `connect:challenge:progress` (0 publishers, 2 subscribers) ŌĆö latent forward design (audit P1-2 follow-up)
- `session:viewed:failed` (1 publisher: tracking.js, 2 subscribers: index.html + tracking.js own-retry) ŌĆö session-view revert
- `habits:set-changed:failed` (1 publisher: settings.html, 1 subscriber: habits.html) ŌĆö Settings habit-set revert
- `daily_habits` (stray subscribe at index.html L2461, 0 publishers) ŌĆö dead from both ends; remains dead (no colon, fails new regex too; this is correct ŌĆö kill the subscribe in a future cleanup PM)

**Toast-trap verification (audit's flagged risk, empirically pre-cleared).** The PM-379 audit warned that flipping the regex could fire a flurry of catch-up achievement toasts on members who'd built up reactions over months. Live data at audit time showed only `test1@test.com` had any rows in `checkin_reactions` (6 reactions), already at tier 1 in `member_achievements` from a manual eval call earlier. The `connect:checkin:logged` family that gates the `eval_focus_*` metrics also couldn't flood ŌĆö zero rows in `connect_checkins` had `focus_slug` populated. Empirically zero toast risk for the trial cohort post-flip. Audit-flagged `checkins_completed` metric turned out to be wired to `wellbeing:logged` (2-segment, never affected); audit got the wiring wrong on that one.

**Realtime bridge behaviour, unchanged.** `handleRealtimeRow` (bus.js L614) builds envelopes and calls `deliverLocal` directly, bypassing the publish() regex. So Realtime-bridged events (e.g. `certificate:earned` from the certificates table INSERT bridge at auth.js L436) were delivering correctly all along to any 2-segment subscriber. The dead 3-segment subscribers couldn't receive Realtime-bridged envelopes either because the regex rejected them at registration time. With the regex loosened, Realtime bridges over 3-segment events are now possible (none currently configured ŌĆö but the gate is no longer the blocker).

**┬¦23.74 NEW HARD RULE ŌĆö cross-repo PM-number scan before claiming.** PM-381's commit message announced this rule earning but couldn't write the brain entry (their session shipped vyve-site only and pre-dated their brain-load of the existing ┬¦23.71 ŌĆö they tried to claim ┬¦23.71 for this rule, conflicting with the devtools rule already at ┬¦23.71). Codifying here as ┬¦23.74. Trigger: PM-378 and PM-379 both saw cross-repo PM-N collisions (`abcf3d3e` vyve-site PM-378 vs `451f849` brain PM-378 staging map; `4b445767` vyve-site PM-379 vs `8777c083` brain PM-379 staging audit + `ed70eedf` brain PM-380 staging corrections). PM-381 itself ran the cross-repo scan and correctly claimed PM-381 as novel across both repos. Third occurrence inside 24 hours, threshold met for promotion from candidate to hard rule. See master.md ┬¦23.74 for the rule body. This brain entry is itself PM-381.b ŌĆö the `.b` framing because vyve-site PM-381 is the canonical PM-381 (in git history, in the commit message, in source comments inside bus.js, in the sw cache key, in the vbb-marker context). Following the established convention from PM-362.b / PM-378.b / PM-379.b.

**This session's actual contribution.** Verification, not code. ┬¦23.69 caught the parallel ship at session start (last 15 vyve-site commits scanned for `bus`, `regex`, `event` ŌĆö PM-381 hit immediately). Fetched the live PM-381 commit message + diff to confirm scope identity with Dean's brief. Read live `bus.js` L137 to confirm the new regex landed cleanly. Read live `sw.js` / `index.html` / `settings.html` to confirm the dual-marker invariant. Walked the iPhone to confirm Connect cross-page reaction sync now propagates (Dean confirmed "that worked perfect"). No new code from me ŌĆö just brain catch-up + ┬¦23.74 codification.

**┬¦23.65 envelope-trusted audit for connect.html banked as follow-on.** Now that the regex is fixed and Connect cross-page bus traffic flows for real, the ┬¦23.65 race surface that PM-293 fixed for `habit:logged` is potentially live on every connect.html subscriber. The PM-379 audit explicitly flagged Connect as the headline ┬¦23.65 risk (Flag 15) because the 3 Connect publishers (connect.html / connect-feed.html / connect-checkin.html) all use fire-and-forget Dexie writes per ┬¦23.39. Subscribers re-read Dexie in their handlers. If a subscriber fires before the publisher's Dexie commit lands, the subscriber sees stale state. Backlog item added below; mind.html carries the same risk and is banked alongside.

**Tooling discipline this session.** ┬¦23.69 (collision scan at session start): PRIMARY DEFENCE ŌĆö caught PM-381 before any duplicate work. ┬¦23.41 + ┬¦23.58 + ┬¦23.66 (pre-commit fresh fetch + content rebase): brain master/changelog/backlog all matched session-start MD5 at pre-commit recheck, no drift. ┬¦23.70 (PM-claim at commit time): brain HEAD `dfd611ff` PM-379.b ŌåÆ I claim PM-381.b (`.b` suffix because vyve-site PM-381 is canonical, this is the brain narrative). ┬¦23.74 (the new rule earning here): scanned BOTH vyve-site (max-PM=381) and VYVEBrain (max-PM=380 from `ed70eedf`) ŌĆö `.b` suffix on canonical PM-381 is the cleanest resolution.

## 2026-05-25 PM-379.b ŌĆö Monthly check-in recap rebuilt around canonical 4 pillars (Habits/Body/Mind/Connect) + EF v19 4-pillar activity rollup + AI prompt restructured [vyve-site `4b445767`]

**Scope.** Sibling to PM-362.b weekly recap (25 May earlier). Monthly check-in's step-0 recap was still on the legacy 6-tile flat shape (Habits/Workouts/Cardio/Sessions/Replays/Weekly Check-ins) ŌĆö Mind / Movement / Connect tables completely absent on both surfaces (client `loadRecap` and server EF rollup), so the AI report was blind to half the platform's activity even though members were doing it. Page now mirrors weekly recap shape exactly: 4-pillar grid with Lucide icons + per-pillar drill + sessions/check-ins band. EF v19 rolls activity into the same 4-pillar shape and rewrites the `=== ACTIVITY (4 PILLARS) ===` block in the Anthropic prompt + adds a pillar-aware structure clause to the system prompt.

**Client-side rewrite (`monthly-checkin.html`).** `loadRecap` rewritten as 11-table Dexie-first read (was 6): adds `movement_activities`, `mind_activities`, `connect_checkins`, `session_live_views`, `replay_video_views`. Dedupe rules per PM-294/PM-304 ŌĆö sessions deduped by `(activity_date, category)` across the 4 view tables. `inMonth(r, dateCol)` belt-and-braces filter bounds both sides per PM-371 ghost-row guard (lower-bound-only check admits future-dated Dexie rows). `wbCheckins` source changed from "last 4 by iso_year/iso_week" (which gave wrong count when recent check-ins fell outside the target month) to "in-month filtered by `activity_date`" ŌĆö matches what the EF prompt rolls in.

**Shape change.** Old `activityData` returned 6 flat counters (`activeDays`, `totalHabits`, `totalWorkouts`, `totalCardio`, `totalSessions`, `totalCheckins`). New shape returns 4-pillar counters (`habitsDays`, `habitsTicks`, `bodyTotal`, `bodyWorkouts`, `cardioByKind`, `movementByKind`, `mindTotal`, `mindByKind`, `connectTotal`, `dailyCheckins`, `sessionsWatched`) + `sessionCategories[]` + `totalCheckins` (preserved). `activityMap` now stores 4-pillar density per day (was 5 raw types) ŌĆö Body bucket merges workouts+cardio+movement, Connect merges sessions+replays+connect_checkins.

**Rendering.** Old `renderRecapStats` (3-col flat grid) replaced with `renderRecapPillars` mirroring weekly's `renderActiveFlow`: 2-col grid with `_statTile(pillar, bigNum, unit, label, detail)`, Lucide pillar icons (`check` #4DAAAA / `person-standing` #E09B3D / `brain` #9B7AE0 / `users` #E06060), per-pillar drilldowns (`_bodyDrill` / `_mindDrill` / `_connectDrill` cloned from weekly). Habits not clamped in monthly (no `Math.min(7)` belt) ŌĆö calendar months are 28-31 days, distinct days naturally bounded by month length + upper-bound filter. Sub-band below grid: deduped session category tags (when present) + a weekly check-in roll-up line ("N weekly check-ins this month."). Weekly recap deliberately hides this line; monthly surfaces it explicitly ŌĆö the framing is "your month included N weeklies".

**Calendar heatmap (`renderCalendar`).** Pillar-density now (was 5 raw types). Legend reads "1 / 2 / 3 / 4 pillars" instead of "1 / 2 / 3 / 4+ types". `count>=4` already maxed correctly ŌĆö 4 pillars is the natural ceiling. CSS unchanged on cell colour ramp.

**EF rewrite (`monthly-checkin` v19, deployed Supabase v25).** Pre-PM-379 v18 had 9-table parallel read; v19 has 14 (adds the same 5 missing tables). New `addPillar(rows, pillar, dateCol)` builds the activity map by 4 pillars, same dedupe as client-side. New `countByKind` + `fmtByKind` helpers feed per-kind drill into the AI prompt. New `bodyDetail` / `mindDetail` / `connectDetail` summary strings.

**AI prompt restructured.** System prompt gains a "four-pillar activity data (Habits / Body / Mind / Connect)" framing line and adds a new step-3 clause to the STRUCTURE list: "Pillar breakdown: 3-4 sentences across Habits / Body / Mind / Connect. Call out the strongest pillar and the lightest. Reference specific activity kinds (e.g. 'the breathwork sessions', 'your running consistency')." Word budget bumped from 500 ŌåÆ 550 to absorb the extra section. Rules line gains: "Refer to the pillars as Habits / Body / Mind / Connect (never 'exercise pillar' or other names)." User prompt's `=== ACTIVITY ===` block replaced with `=== ACTIVITY (4 PILLARS) ===` 6-line summary: active days ┬Ę HABITS (days ┬Ę ticks) ┬Ę BODY (total ┬Ę breakdown) ┬Ę MIND (total ┬Ę per-kind) ┬Ę CONNECT (total ┬Ę breakdown) ┬Ę weekly check-ins completed.

**Response body shape.** `activity` payload gains pillar totals (`bodyTotal`, `mindTotal`, `connectTotal`, `habitsDays`, `habitsTicks`, `cardioByKind`, `movementByKind`, `mindByKind`, `dailyCheckins`, `sessionsWatched`, `sessionCategories`) while preserving legacy fields (`totalHabits`, `totalWorkouts`, `totalCardio`, `totalSessions`) for any consumer reading the old shape. No client currently consumes the POST response's activity field (the page navigates to results view on success which reads scores + ai_report only), so the back-compat band is precautionary not load-bearing.

**Bus subscriber wiring.** Existing `habit:logged` subscriber-with-refresh kept; refresh path now calls `renderRecapPillars` (was `renderRecapStats`). No new subscriptions ŌĆö the recap is a once-on-page-open surface, not a live re-render target. Achievements eval unchanged.

**Ship details.** vyve-site `4b445767`. 4-file atomic via Git Data API (PAT-direct, Composio still down per memory #20). Files: `monthly-checkin.html` (1155 ŌåÆ 1355 lines, +200 net mostly the new pillar-grid CSS + helpers + 11-table Dexie read), `sw.js` (cache `pm378-podcast-platforms-a` ŌåÆ `pm379-monthly-checkin-pillars-a`), `index.html` (vbb-marker 262 ŌåÆ 263), `settings.html` (settings-vbb-marker 262 ŌåÆ 263). 3 inline scripts node --check clean. ┬¦23.41 first-100-char re-fetch matched on all 4 files at commit SHA via Contents API. EF deployed pre-page-ship so any in-flight POST during the gap landed on v19 ŌĆö old client posting against new EF is fine (EF reads `scores` + `notes` + `goal_progress_*` only, all present in old POST body), new client posting against old EF would also be fine (response shape was forward-extended only).

**┬¦23.69 fired clean at session start.** Brief implied a build deliverable ("make sure this is good to go and that its updated to have the mind body connect habits weekly check in data"). Scanned last 15 vyve-site commits for `monthly`, `check-in`, `checkin` ŌĆö PM-364 (haptics sweep) touched `monthly-checkin.html` 14 commits ago, fully landed, no parallel session. Safe.

**┬¦23.41 + ┬¦23.70 fired clean at vyve-site commit time.** Pre-commit HEAD recheck immediately before `POST /git/commits`: HEAD = `abcf3d3e`, max-PM = 378 ŌåÆ claim PM-379 in vyve-site. No parallel landings during build.

**┬¦23.58 + cross-repo PM-collision retro logged.** Pre-brain-commit re-fetch (┬¦23.58): brain unchanged, but cross-repo, brain HEAD had advanced TWO PMs while I was building: `ed70eedf` "PM-380 ŌĆö Architecture map (staging) corrections per PM-379 audit" and `8777c083` "PM-379 ŌĆö Architecture audit (staging)". Both are staging-only doc deliverables in a parallel session; neither prepended to `brain/changelog.md`. Identical shape to the PM-378 cross-repo collision documented in the PM-378 entry above. **Collision disposition:** vyve-site `4b445767` keeps PM-379 ŌĆö already permanent in git history, in commit message, in source comments across HTML + EF, in sw cache key. The two parallel brain commits at `ed70eedf` and `8777c083` reference a different PM-379 namespace (staging architecture audit). Per the established convention from PM-362.b (changelog L1138) and PM-378 above, this brain entry takes `.b` suffix to preserve brain-narrative integrity. Two `PM-379` commit messages now exist: one in `VYVEHealth/vyve-site` at `4b445767` (this ship, canonical for the recap rebuild), one in `VYVEHealth/VYVEBrain` at `8777c083` (parallel staging artefact). Third occurrence of this pattern in 24 hours ŌĆö promoting ┬¦23.70 cross-repo extension from "banked" to "candidate"; one more occurrence and it earns a hard rule.

**No new ┬¦23 hard rule earned this ship.** Second sibling-port of the weeklyŌåÆmonthly recap shape (weekly was PM-362.b same morning). The "weekly check-in recap is a deliberate uncapped surface, diverges from engagement-v2 by design" ┬¦23 candidate banked at PM-362.b does NOT extend here ŌĆö monthly's habits-days count is naturally bounded by the calendar month length, not a deliberate divergence. Habits clamp deliberately omitted (no `Math.min(N)`), inline comment explains why.

**Out of scope this ship.** Monthly check-in DB triggers ŌĆö `charity_count_monthly_checkins` sibling of `charity_count_wellbeing_checkins` (sibling-count semantics on `iso_month`), monthly counter trigger that bumps `members.cert_checkins_count` so weekly + monthly UNION semantics hold, `zzz_mark_home_state_dirty_*` triggers for parity with `wellbeing_checkins`. Plus retroactive UPDATE for the 3 existing `monthly_checkins` rows (Dean/Lewis/Kelly, April 2026). Banked next on Dean's stack per PM-362.b L561, unchanged by this ship ŌĆö they're DB-only work, separate session. Slider copy mirror to onboarding wording (Lewis-blocked) + 8-slider weekly-mini-monthly question (Lewis scope call) also unchanged.

**Next.** Monthly check-in DB triggers (next on Dean's stack); separately, `checkin_questions` Tier 2.3 catalogue from the audit queue (schema + Dexie read shippable without Lewis copy; he populates wording when ready).

## 2026-05-25 PM-378 ŌĆö Podcast platform links catalogue: podcast_platforms table + podcast.html .hero-listen rewritten as Dexie-read with FALLBACK_PLATFORMS cold-paint [vyve-site `abcf3d3e`]

**Scope.** Next from the content-surface audit queue carried forward from PM-372 brief (Tier 4, podcast.html). The three Spotify / Apple Podcasts / Amazon Music platform links in `podcast.html` `.hero-listen` were hardcoded `<a>` tags. Now they render from `public.podcast_platforms`, ordered by `display_order`, with a `FALLBACK_PLATFORMS` const carrying the original three rows verbatim for cold paint and safety fallback. Lewis can edit any URL, rename any platform, reorder, or add a fourth (Pocket Casts / Overcast / YouTube Music) by one INSERT in Supabase Studio ŌĆö no vyve-site commit per content change.

**Scope decision (talk-first held).** Considered two shapes pre-build: (1) `podcast_platforms` table ŌĆö one row per platform; or (2) `platform_links jsonb` on a singleton `podcast_meta` row. Picked (1). Reasons: mirrors `how_to_resources` (PM-377) and `persona_welcome_copy` (PM-372) muscle-memory pattern; one-row-per-platform survives Studio editing without breaking on malformed jsonb; CHECK constraints + UNIQUE slug refuse invalid rows server-side; adding a fourth platform is one INSERT, not a jsonb merge. Hero copy (eyebrow / title / subtitle / guest CTA / "Coming soon" footer note) deliberately NOT migrated ŌĆö not in the audit queue, no editing pain flagged, and the CSS gradient hero ties the copy to its design context. If Lewis ever asks to edit "Honest conversations. Real change." live, that becomes its own follow-on PM with a `podcast_meta` table ŌĆö a sibling to `podcast_platforms`, not a replacement.

**Schema (`public.podcast_platforms`, migration `pm378_create_podcast_platforms`).** `id BIGINT IDENTITY PK ┬Ę slug TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL ┬Ę display_name TEXT NOT NULL ┬Ę url TEXT NOT NULL ┬Ę icon_url TEXT NULL ┬Ę display_order INT DEFAULT 100 ┬Ę active BOOL DEFAULT true ┬Ę created_at / updated_at`. UNIQUE on `slug` (so future Pocket Casts / Overcast / YouTube Music inserts can't collide). CHECK constraints on `url`, `display_name`, and `slug` enforce non-empty after trim ŌĆö refuses Studio rows that someone half-filled and saved. `icon_url` nullable per ┬¦23.49 (imagery-DB-driven, even though v1 chips are text-only ŌĆö the column exists so the day Lewis wants per-platform iconography on the chips, no schema change is required). RLS enabled, single `SELECT TO authenticated WHERE active=true` policy. Partial read index `(display_order ASC, created_at DESC) WHERE active=true` for the catalogue pull. `set_updated_at_podcast_platforms()` trigger. Seeded with the three current URLs verbatim from `podcast.html` L400-402 pre-migration ŌĆö `ON CONFLICT (slug) DO NOTHING` so re-runs are safe. Table comment documents the Studio-edit workflow for next-session-Claude / Lewis reading the schema cold.

**Sync wiring (db.js + sync.js).** `db.js` `SCHEMA_V20` adds `podcast_platforms: '&id, slug, display_order, active'`, `db.version(20).stores(SCHEMA_V20)` registered, consumer via `makeCatalogueTable('podcast_platforms')`. `sync.js` catalogue plan entry hitting `/podcast_platforms?active=eq.true&select=*&order=display_order.asc`. `CATALOGUE_FRESH_TABLES` extended (5-min stale window). `CATALOGUE_INVALIDATION_KEY` bumped `pm377-how-to-resources` ŌåÆ `pm378-podcast-platforms` per ┬¦23.50.

**podcast.html rewrite.** `.hero-listen` markup collapsed from three hardcoded `<a>` tags to a single empty `<div id="hero-listen" class="hero-listen"></div>`. New `FALLBACK_PLATFORMS` const inside the existing IIFE carries the three seed rows. New `platformChipHtml(p)` emits `<a target="_blank" rel="noopener">` matching the original markup so existing `.hero-listen a` CSS binds without touching styles. New `renderPlatforms(rows)` sorts by `(display_order ASC, slug)` and replaces `#hero-listen` innerHTML in one assignment ŌĆö no layout shift, no flash of empty. New `loadAndPaintPlatforms()` reads from Dexie via `VYVELocalDB.podcast_platforms.allFor()`, falls back to FALLBACK_PLATFORMS if (a) `VYVELocalDB` isn't ready, (b) Dexie returns empty, or (c) the read rejects. Existing `boot()` extended: calls `loadAndPaintPlatforms()` alongside the existing `loadAndPaint()` (episodes), and the `vyve-localdb-table-pulled` listener now repaints platforms when `t === 'podcast_platforms'`. Cold paint synchronous: `renderPlatforms(null)` fires at script-execution time directly after `paintSkeleton()`, painting fallback chips before Dexie even initialises.

**Operational implication for Lewis.** Adding a fourth platform = `INSERT INTO public.podcast_platforms (slug, display_name, url, display_order) VALUES ('pocket-casts', 'Pocket Casts', 'https://pca.st/podcast/...', 40);`. Reordering = `UPDATE ... SET display_order = N WHERE slug = '...'`. Pulling a platform off the chip row = `UPDATE ... SET active = false WHERE slug = '...'` (preserves history). Devices pick up within the 5-min `CATALOGUE_FRESH_TABLES` window. To force immediate pickup across all members, any vyve-site commit bumping `CATALOGUE_INVALIDATION_KEY` does it ŌĆö unnecessary for routine content edits.

**Ship details.** vyve-site `abcf3d3e`. 6-file atomic via Git Data API (Composio still down 4 days post-incident; PAT-direct via Vault per memory #20). Files: db.js, sync.js, podcast.html, sw.js, index.html, settings.html. sw cache `pm377-how-to-resources-a` ŌåÆ `pm378-podcast-platforms-a`. vbb-marker 261 ŌåÆ 262 both `index.html` span and `settings.html` div. db.js + sync.js + podcast.html inline JS all `node --check` clean. ┬¦23.41 first-100 + MD5 verify clean on all 6 files at commit SHA via Contents API.

**┬¦23.69 fired clean at session start.** Brief implied a build deliverable. Scanned last 15 vyve-site commits for `podcast`, `platform`, `spotify`, `apple`, `amazon` ŌĆö zero hits in the last 15 (PM-363 through PM-377). No parallel session touching podcast or platform links. Safe to proceed.

**┬¦23.41 + ┬¦23.70 fired clean at vyve-site commit time.** Pre-commit HEAD recheck immediately before `POST /git/commits`: HEAD = `c00a24ca`, max-PM = 377 ŌåÆ claim PM-378. No parallel landings during build. Source was already drafted with PM-378 placeholders ŌĆö no sed-sweep required.

**┬¦23.58 caught brain drift + ┬¦23.70 retro logged a PM-NUMBER COLLISION.** Pre-brain-commit re-fetch (┬¦23.58) revealed:
- `tasks_backlog.md` drifted by one parallel insert: `4f304fc` ŌĆö "PM-376 follow-on ŌĆö Backlog entry: YouTube embed background-audio play parked as post-trial" ŌĆö preserved verbatim from the live file; my backlog patch threads around it.
- `brain_master.md` and `brain_changelog.md` were unchanged since session-start fetch.
- **And one parallel commit `451f849` titled "PM-378 ŌĆö Architecture map (staging): write-surface inventory across the portal"** landed in `brain/staging/architecture-map-pm378.md` between session start and brain-commit time. That parallel session claimed PM-378 in the brain for a staging-only doc deliverable while my vyve-site `abcf3d3e` claimed PM-378 in production code.

**Collision disposition.** vyve-site `abcf3d3e` keeps PM-378 ŌĆö already permanent in git history, in the commit message, in source comments across 6 files, in the Supabase migration name, in the sw cache key, in the `CATALOGUE_INVALIDATION_KEY` value, and in the seed comment of the schema. The parallel brain staging artefact `brain/staging/architecture-map-pm378.md` is brain-only with no downstream references ŌĆö proposed rename to `architecture-map-pm378b.md` (or any non-clashing label) at next-session housekeeping; flagged on the backlog. Forensic mapping: two `PM-378` commit messages exist in the world ŌĆö one in `VYVEHealth/vyve-site` at `abcf3d3e` (this ship, canonical), one in `VYVEHealth/VYVEBrain` at `451f849` (parallel staging artefact, should be renumbered/labelled at next housekeeping pass).

**Why ┬¦23.70 alone didn't catch this in time.** ┬¦23.70 mechanism reads the last 5 commits on the **ship target repo** (vyve-site for code, brain for brain) immediately before commit. My vyve-site PM-claim recompute correctly identified max-PM-377 from the last 5 vyve-site commits ŌĆö but PM-378 was already claimed in the **brain repo** by `451f849` at 19:22 UTC, ~13 minutes before my vyve-site `POST /git/commits`. ┬¦23.70 as currently written scans a single repo; cross-repo PM-namespace collisions are out of its grep window. Documenting as a possible ┬¦23.70 extension candidate ("scan BOTH vyve-site AND VYVEBrain for the highest PM-N within the last 5 commits of each before claiming") ŌĆö not promoting to a new rule on first occurrence; banked until / unless this recurs.

**No new ┬¦23 hard rule earned this ship.** Sixth application of ┬¦23.49 + ┬¦23.50 (service_catalogue PM-190, replay_playlists PM-235, replay_videos PM-245, mind_videos PM-367, persona_welcome_copy PM-372, how_to_resources PM-377, podcast_platforms PM-378 ŌåÆ seventh, the pattern is fully muscle memory).

**Next from the audit queue.** Tier 2.3 `checkin_questions` ŌĆö versioned slider questions for weekly + monthly check-ins. Schema + Dexie read path can ship without Lewis copy; he populates wording when ready. Estimated ~1 hour. Then audit queue is exhausted apart from banked items (`mind-journal-prompts.js`, `home-focus-catalogue.js`, Tier 3 workout/breathwork metadata) which defer until Lewis / Calum flag content-editing friction.

## 2026-05-25 PM-377 ŌĆö How-to library catalogue: how_to_resources table + how-to-pdfs.html/how-to-videos.html rewritten as catalogue reads [vyve-site `c00a24ca`]

**Scope.** Tier 4 of the content-surface audit from PM-372 brief. Both how-to pages shipped as 2.7KB "Coming Soon" placeholders pre-this-PM. Rather than letting them sit there until Lewis produces content (and accumulate "should we migrate this?" technical debt), built them right from day one as catalogue reads off a single `public.how_to_resources` table. When Lewis records the first how-to video or writes the first guide PDF, he adds the row in Supabase Studio; the corresponding page surfaces it automatically on next visit. No vyve-site commit per content add.

**Architecture.** Same PM-367 / PM-372 catalogue pattern. Single Supabase table with `kind` discriminator (`'pdf' | 'video'`) drives which sub-page surfaces each row. PDF rows render as horizontal cards with thumbnail / title / description / duration_label; tap opens the URL in a new tab. Video rows render as 16:9 thumbnail cards; tap opens an in-page YouTube embed modal (event-delegated click handler so dynamically-injected cards bind cleanly ŌĆö same fix shape as PM-367 mind.html). Both pages share the empty-state "Coming soon" markup so members never see a blank list ŌĆö the catalogue can be empty without the page looking broken.

**Schema (`public.how_to_resources`, migration `pm377_create_how_to_resources`).** `id BIGINT IDENTITY PK ┬Ę kind TEXT CHECK IN ('pdf','video') ┬Ę slug TEXT ┬Ę title TEXT ┬Ę description TEXT ┬Ę url TEXT ┬Ę youtube_video_id TEXT ┬Ę thumb_url TEXT ┬Ę duration_label TEXT ┬Ę display_order INT default 100 ┬Ę active BOOL default true ┬Ę created_at / updated_at`. Unique constraint `(kind, slug)`. CHECK constraint enforces payload validity: PDF rows require `url`; video rows require either `url` or `youtube_video_id`. (First migration attempt failed correctly here ŌĆö I had seeded test video rows with both columns NULL; the CHECK caught it pre-commit. Transaction rolled back atomically. Recovered by dropping the bad seed rows entirely; pages render the empty-state until Lewis adds real content.) RLS enabled, single `SELECT TO authenticated WHERE active=true` policy. Partial read index `(kind, display_order, created_at DESC) WHERE active=true` for the catalogue pull ordering.

**Sync wiring (db.js + sync.js).** db.js `SCHEMA_V19` adds `how_to_resources: '&id, kind, slug, display_order, active'`, `db.version(19).stores(SCHEMA_V19)` registered, consumer via `makeCatalogueTable('how_to_resources')`. sync.js catalogue plan entry hitting `/how_to_resources?active=eq.true&select=*&order=kind.asc,display_order.asc,created_at.desc` (server-side composite sort so the page-side filter is a single .filter() with no re-sort). `CATALOGUE_FRESH_TABLES` extended (5-min stale window). `CATALOGUE_INVALIDATION_KEY` bumped `pm375-member-prompts` ŌåÆ `pm377-how-to-resources` per ┬¦23.50.

**Page implementations.** Both pages share the boilerplate: theme.js + auth.js + nav.js + db.js + sync.js script tags, gated `#app` div, `MutationObserver` waiting for auth.js to flip `#app` to visible, `init()` that calls `loadFromDexie()` once + subscribes to `vyve-localdb-table-pulled` for hydrate-driven refresh + 1000ms probe fallback (mirrors PM-367 mind.html). Render path filters Dexie rows by kind, sorts by `(display_order, created_at DESC)`, hides empty-state if results exist, shows it if not. `escapeHtml()` defensive against any future free-text fields. PDFs open in `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"` ŌĆö external resources never replace the app shell. Videos open in an in-page modal with iframe `https://www.youtube.com/embed/<id>?autoplay=1&rel=0&modestbranding=1`; close button uses `env(safe-area-inset-top/right)` per ┬¦23.72; ESC + scrim-click + dedicated close button all wire to `closePlayer()` (clears iframe src to stop playback). `extractYouTubeId()` parses watch/youtu.be/embed URLs as a fallback when only `url` is populated on a video row.

**Ship details.** vyve-site `c00a24ca`. 7-file atomic via Git Data API: db.js, sync.js, how-to-pdfs.html (NEW shape, was 2.7KB placeholder), how-to-videos.html (NEW shape, was 2.7KB placeholder), sw.js, index.html, settings.html. sw cache `pm376-nav-body-person-standing-a` ŌåÆ `pm377-how-to-resources-a`. vbb-marker 260 ŌåÆ 261 both index.html span and settings.html div. db.js + sync.js + both new pages `node --check` clean on inline scripts. ┬¦23.41 first-100 + MD5 verify clean on all 7 files at commit SHA. ┬¦23.70 PM-claim fired correctly: PM-373/374/375/376 had shipped during initial conversation turns, fresh HEAD recheck at commit time confirmed max-PM-376 ŌåÆ PM-377.

**Operational implication for Dean / Lewis.** PDF: upload to Supabase Storage (or host elsewhere on vyvehealth.co.uk), INSERT a row with `kind='pdf'`, the URL, title, description, optionally `duration_label='4 min read'` and `display_order`. Video: INSERT a row with `kind='video'` and the YouTube video ID (or full watch URL ŌĆö `extractYouTubeId()` parses both). Thumbnail auto-resolves from YouTube CDN (`hqdefault.jpg`) if `thumb_url` is left NULL. Set `active=false` to hide a row without deleting it.

**Sync.js patch retry.** First sync.js edit landed inside the wrong object literal due to a too-greedy regex anchor ŌĆö `node --check` caught the syntax error immediately, re-fetched fresh sync.js from main, redid the edit with exact-string anchor instead. Roughly 60 seconds of round-trip; no commit-level cost. Lesson banked but not codified: regex anchors on multi-line object literals are fragile; prefer exact-string anchor including the closing `},` of the preceding sibling. Already-stated practice; not a new ┬¦23 rule.

**No new ┬¦23 hard rule earned.** Sixth application of ┬¦23.49 + ┬¦23.50 (service_catalogue PM-190, replay_playlists PM-235, replay_videos PM-245, mind_videos PM-367, persona_welcome_copy PM-372, how_to_resources PM-377). Pattern is fully muscle-memorised now.

**Next from the audit queue.** Tier 4 podcast.html platform links (Spotify / Apple / Amazon Music) ŌĆö small `podcast_platforms` table or `platform_links` jsonb on a singleton row. ~30 mins. Then Tier 2.3 checkin_questions table (schema + read path, Lewis populates wording when ready). Tier 1.1 sessions-data.js stays parked ŌĆö proper version is PM-211 already on backlog with materialiser EF.

## 2026-05-25 PM-376 ŌĆö Bottom nav Body icon swapped dumbbell ŌåÆ person-standing (Lucide) [vyve-site `278f2af2`]

Stroke-width 1.7 retained ŌĆö Lucide source is stroke-width 2 but the nav reads softer at 1.7 at the 24px tab size (existing PM-344 calibration, kept on the new path). Same `viewBox="0 0 24 24"`, same `fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"` envelope. The PM-344 comment block updated to document the PM-376 swap inline so the next session reading nav.js doesn't wonder why Body diverged from "all Lucide-canonical."

**Desktop nav untouched.** Desktop nav (`desktopLinks`) is text-only ŌĆö no icon row. Single change covers the only place Body has an icon. Body label on desktop is unchanged.

**┬¦23.70 fired clean.** Parent at commit-time was `9aac3c11` (PM-375 Member Prompts). Two parallel ships landed between PM-373 commit and this commit: PM-374 (food log Coming Soon lockdown, `761a027c`) and PM-375. Neither touched nav.js. Both touched index.html + settings.html + sw.js for marker/cache bumps ŌĆö re-fetched those three live, replayed bumps 259 ŌåÆ 260 on post-PM-375 content. Base-tree-merge safe. PM claim recomputed 374 ŌåÆ 376 at commit-time from live commit messages.

## 2026-05-25 PM-375 ŌĆö Member Prompts system: Lewis-driven in-app questionnaires (4 Supabase tables + Dexie sync + renderer module) [vyve-site `9aac3c11`]

**Scope.** New first-class system for in-app popup questionnaires sourced from Supabase. Lewis (and eventually the Command Centre) authors prompts in the DB; devices pull within 5 min via the ┬¦23.50 catalogue contract; renderer gates per-member dismissal state and shows the highest-priority eligible prompt on surface entry. Two canary prompts seeded for the trial: a Monday weekly-preference picker ("what do you want more of this week?") and a Sunday app-feedback pulse (1-10 sentiment slider + free-text). Both wire into the home page boot path; other surfaces opt in by including the script tag and calling `VYVEPrompts.maybeShow(surfaceName)`.

**Dean's brief.** "Could we have a popup go up, what's your thoughts on the app, would you improve anything, having issues. And maybe one that asks what do you want from the app this week ŌĆö workouts, meditation, etc." Two distinct shapes (feedback + preference) handled by one schema ŌĆö only `question_type` and how Lewis reads the responses differs.

**Architectural shape.**

The system mirrors the ┬¦23.49 (catalogue imagery DB-driven) / ┬¦23.50 (invalidation-key bump on schema changes) / ┬¦23.55 (hub-page hero composition) contracts that govern `service_catalogue`, `mind_videos`, `replay_playlists`, and the rest of the content-driven surfaces. Four tables:

- `member_prompts` ŌĆö the popup container (catalogue, Dexie-mirrored). Columns: `id uuid PK`, `slug text unique`, `title`, `subtitle`, `eyebrow`, `cta_label`, `skip_label`, `image_url` (nullable per ┬¦23.49 even though not used in v1), `trigger_type` (`first_login` | `surface_enter` | `manual` | `weekly` | `date_range`), `trigger_value jsonb` (per-type config ŌĆö `{"surface":"index","day_of_week":1,"day_of_week_window":3}` for the weekly preference), `target_segment jsonb` (`{}` = everyone, `{"persona":"HAVEN"}` to filter, `{"min_days_active":7}` for tenure-based), `dismiss_mode` (`once` | `cooldown_days` | `weekly` | `always`), `cooldown_days int`, `priority int` (higher wins when multiple eligible), `active bool`, `active_from`/`active_until timestamptz`, audit cols.
- `member_prompt_questions` ŌĆö questionnaire body (catalogue, Dexie-mirrored). Columns: `id uuid PK`, `prompt_id uuid FK ON DELETE CASCADE`, `question_text`, `question_hint`, `question_type` (`multi` | `single` | `slider_1_10` | `text_short` | `text_long` | `yes_no`), `options jsonb` ([{value,label}, ...] for multi/single; `{min_label,max_label,default}` for slider), `placeholder` (text inputs), `required bool`, `display_order int`, `active bool`. One prompt ŌåÆ 1..N questions ordered by `display_order`.
- `member_prompt_dismissals` ŌĆö per-member gate state (member-scoped, hydrated). Columns: `id`, `member_email`, `prompt_id`, `first_seen_at`, `last_seen_at`, `last_dismissed_at`, `completed_at`, `dismiss_count int`, `complete_count int`, `last_iso_year int`, `last_iso_week int`. Unique index on `(member_email, prompt_id)` for upsert-into-one-row semantics. Renderer reads this from Dexie to compute "should I show this prompt" without a network round trip.
- `member_prompt_responses` ŌĆö answers (member-scoped, write-only from this module). Columns: `id`, `member_email`, `prompt_id`, `question_id`, `answer_value jsonb` (shape depends on `question_type` ŌĆö `["workouts","meditation"]` for multi, `7` for slider, `"text..."` for textareas, `true` for yes_no), `iso_year int`, `iso_week int`, `submitted_at`. Indexed `(prompt_id, iso_year, iso_week)` for the weekly aggregations Lewis will run from Supabase Studio.

RLS on all four. Catalogue tables: `SELECT TO authenticated USING (active = true)`. Dismissals + responses: `SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE TO authenticated USING/WITH CHECK (member_email = auth.email())`. Updated_at triggers on the three mutable tables via shared `set_updated_at_mp()` function.

**Client wiring.**

`prompts.js` (665 LOC, new file) ŌĆö the renderer module. Self-contained IIFE exposing `window.VYVEPrompts = { maybeShow(surface), showBySlug(slug), refresh() }`. Eight sections inside: (1) eligibility gate (ISO-week helper, day-of-week ┬▒ window math, trigger gate, segment gate, dismissal gate ŌĆö modes `once` / `weekly` / `cooldown_days` / `always`); (2) Dexie-first catalogue + dismissal read; (3) priority-desc selection; (4) modal render ŌĆö `vp-scrim` + `vp-sheet` injected into body, bottom-sheet transform-on-open animation, drag-handle, close-X, scrollable body, sticky footer; (5) per-question-type render (pills for multi/single/yes_no, range slider for slider_1_10 with live value + gradient fill, textarea/input for text); (6) answer capture + required-field validation with shake animation; (7) optimistic-first submit per ┬¦23.39 ŌĆö UI tears down BEFORE the network writes fire, `keepalive: true` on both POSTs so a quick page navigation doesn't kill the writes; (8) public API.

`prompts.css` (335 LOC, new file) ŌĆö bottom-sheet styles. Reuses app theme tokens (`--bg`, `--surface`, `--text`, `--teal`, `--teal-lt`, `--border`, `--scrim`) so dark + light parity comes for free via `data-theme="dark|light"` on `<html>`. Light-mode overrides on five surfaces: CTA (lighter-teal-on-dark ŌåÆ deep-teal-on-white), eyebrow (teal-lt ŌåÆ deep teal for contrast on white), slider thumb border (teal-lt ŌåÆ deep teal), slider value text (teal-lt ŌåÆ deep teal), scrim (rgba black ŌåÆ rgba teal-tinted). All other styling cascades from tokens.

`db.js` SCHEMA_V18 ŌĆö four new stores. Catalogue tables (`member_prompts`, `member_prompt_questions`) via `makeCatalogueTable`; member-scoped (`member_prompt_dismissals`, `member_prompt_responses`) via `makeTable`. Dexie indexes match the schema: `&id` (PK), plus `[member_email+prompt_id]` compound on dismissals for fast per-member upsert, plus `[prompt_id+iso_year+iso_week]` on responses for weekly aggregation reads. Version chain extended `.version(18).stores(SCHEMA_V18)`.

`sync.js` ŌĆö three plan entries (2 catalogue + 1 member-scoped dismissals; responses are write-only from `prompts.js` ŌĆö no pull needed since the renderer never reads its own submissions back). `CATALOGUE_FRESH_TABLES` extended with `member_prompts: 1, member_prompt_questions: 1` (5-min window ŌĆö Lewis edits surface fast). `CATALOGUE_INVALIDATION_KEY` bumped `pm372-persona-welcome-copy` ŌåÆ `pm375-member-prompts` per ┬¦23.50.

`index.html` ŌĆö `<script src="/prompts.js" defer></script>` added immediately after `/sync.js`. `VYVEPrompts.maybeShow('index')` wired into `boot()` after `moodShowPanel(email)` so the first-paint mood card (which has its own dismissal logic) shows before the prompt can. Inside `maybeShow`, a 600ms `setTimeout` defers the actual catalogue+dismissal Dexie read so first paint completes uncontested. vbb-marker 258 ŌåÆ 259.

`sw.js` ŌĆö cache key `pm374-food-log-coming-soon-a` ŌåÆ `pm375-member-prompts-a`. `/prompts.js` + `/prompts.css` added to precache list immediately after `/haptics.js` so cold-boot offline resolves the renderer.

`settings.html` ŌĆö `settings-vbb-marker` 258 ŌåÆ 259 to maintain the dual-marker invariant per memory #10.

**Seed data.**

```sql
INSERT INTO member_prompts (slug, eyebrow, title, subtitle, cta_label, skip_label,
  trigger_type, trigger_value, dismiss_mode, priority, active)
VALUES
  ('weekly-preference', 'This week''s focus',
   'What do you want more of this week?',
   'Pick as many as you like. We''ll shape the live sessions and content around what the community asks for.',
   'Save my preferences', 'Skip this week',
   'surface_enter', '{"surface":"index","day_of_week":1,"day_of_week_window":3}',
   'weekly', 200, true),
  ('app-feedback', 'Quick check-in',
   'How''s the app feeling?',
   '30 seconds. Honest answers ŌĆö it shapes what we build next.',
   'Send feedback', 'Skip for now',
   'surface_enter', '{"surface":"index","day_of_week":7}',
   'cooldown_days', 150, true);
```

`weekly-preference` carries one multi-select with 8 options (Workouts / Cardio / Movement & mobility / Meditation / Sleep content / Mindfulness / Connect & community / Nutrition) plus one optional `text_long` for the "anything specific" follow-up. `app-feedback` carries the 1-10 slider (required, `min_label:"Struggling"`, `max_label:"Loving it"`, `default:7`) plus two optional `text_long` fields ("anything bugging you" + "anything you'd love to see").

**Lewis read surface (deferred build).**

For the trial, Lewis reads responses from Supabase Studio with two saved queries: `select option, count(*) from member_prompt_responses ... where iso_week=...` for the preference aggregate, and a chronological list of the feedback texts for sentiment review. Command Centre editor + reading UI is on the backlog post-trial ŌĆö schema is editor-ready.

**Tooling discipline.**

┬¦23.70 fired **twice** during this build. First fire: parallel sessions shipped PM-372 (hydration COPY_TABLE ŌåÆ catalogue, took SCHEMA_V17) + PM-373 (habits tile palette) between session-start (PM-368 was HEAD) and first commit attempt ŌĆö both touched my five collision files. Re-fetched all five at the new HEAD; rebased SCHEMA_V17 ŌåÆ SCHEMA_V18 (the V17 slot was no longer mine); re-applied invalidation key bump on top of the new PM-372 bump value; re-applied marker bump 255 ŌåÆ 257 ŌåÆ planned 258. Then second fire: parallel session shipped PM-374 (food log Coming Soon) between rebase and final commit attempt ŌĆö touched index.html + settings.html + sw.js. Re-fetched those three at the new HEAD; replayed marker bump 257 ŌåÆ 258 ŌåÆ 259 on post-PM-374 content; replayed cache-key bump `pm374-food-log-coming-soon-a` ŌåÆ `pm375-member-prompts-a`; my non-collision files (prompts.js, prompts.css, db.js, sync.js) carried forward without rebase. PM claim recomputed 372 ŌåÆ 374 ŌåÆ 375 across the chain.

┬¦23.41 first-100-char verify all 7 files clean against committed SHA `9aac3c11`. ┬¦23.52(a) honoured throughout ŌĆö Git Blob API used (`encoding: base64`, written to /tmp file then read; never substituted into bash argv). One blob-API quirk surfaced during commit: GitHub doesn't return `size` on the blob-create response (it does on the read response), so the post-write size-check has to be dropped or routed through a follow-up `GET /git/blobs/{sha}`. Single sed sweep at commit time per memory #21 ŌĆö 6 PM-XXX placeholders cleaned to PM-375 in one pass after the final HEAD recheck.

**What's intentionally NOT built.**

- Command Centre editor. Schema is editor-ready (jsonb config, nullable image_url, dismiss-mode enum, segment jsonb). Lewis edits via Supabase Studio for the trial.
- `first_login` trigger type. Code path exists in `passesTriggerGate` for future use but no v1 prompts use it ŌĆö `surface_enter` + `target_segment.min_days_active` covers the canary use cases without needing a dedicated first-login flag column on `members`.
- Server-side aggregation EF for the response read. Studio SQL is fine for trial scale (<50 members). Aggregation EF gets a backlog slot when the read surface lands in Command Centre.
- Catalogue editor UI for question reordering. The `display_order int` column means manual SQL is fine until volume justifies a UI.

**Files committed atomically (7):**

| file | status | summary |
|---|---|---|
| `prompts.js` | added | 665 LOC renderer module |
| `prompts.css` | added | 335 LOC bottom-sheet styles, dark + light theme |
| `db.js` | modified | SCHEMA_V18 + 4 store registrations (+23 LOC) |
| `sync.js` | modified | 3 plan entries + invalidation key bump + CATALOGUE_FRESH_TABLES (+22 LOC) |
| `index.html` | modified | script tag + boot wire + vbb 258 ŌåÆ 259 (+6 LOC) |
| `sw.js` | modified | cache key bump + precache add (+7 LOC) |
| `settings.html` | modified | settings-vbb-marker 258 ŌåÆ 259 (+1 LOC) |

sw cache: `vyve-cache-v2026-05-25-pm375-member-prompts-a`
vbb-marker: `Update 259` on both index.html + settings.html

## 2026-05-25 PM-374 ŌĆö Food log locked Coming Soon (soft lockdown): nutrition.html CTA ŌåÆ coming-soon card, log-food.html cover + boot suppressed [vyve-site `761a027c`]

**Scope.** Soft lockdown of food-logging UX surfaces ahead of trial launch. PM-353 had already retired the `fuel` focus + `nutrition_logs` from Focus pillar source-table reads. PM-374 closes the entry-point loop ŌĆö `log-food.html` is no longer reachable from `nutrition.html` and direct-link / cached-SW navigation hits a cover, not the live UI. No DB, EF, or bus changes. Substantive UX surfaces preserved (Nutrition page macros / TDEE / hydration / weight tracker unchanged).

**Dean's brief.** "Food logging needs to be locked as coming soon." Decided on **soft lockdown** over hard lockdown: keep the page and the underlying write paths intact in repo, just block reach. Reversibility is one-commit. Matches the PM-353 pattern for the Focus-level retire.

**Surface 1 ŌĆö `nutrition.html` CTA replacement.**

The `<a id="food-coming-soon" href="/log-food.html" class="food-log-link fade-up-3">` block (L544-552 in pre-PM-374) is replaced with a `<div id="food-coming-soon" class="coming-soon fade-up-3">` using the existing house `.coming-soon` CSS already sitting at L217-219 (dashed border, muted text, centered) ŌĆö same idiom as `connect-feed.html` Following v1 (PM-shipped placeholder for unbuilt social graph) and `connect-challenge.html` `.no-challenge` empty state.

Critical preservation: `id="food-coming-soon"` retained verbatim. There's a skeleton-control routine at L913 that iterates `['cal-main-card','cal-stats-row','explainer-box','recalc-wrap','food-coming-soon']` and toggles `display` on `mode==='off'`. Changing the id would silently break the Off-mode toggle. The element type changed (anchor ŌåÆ div) but the id and class-list survive that routine unchanged.

Wording: "Food log ŌĆö coming soon" / "Detailed meal logging is coming in a future update. For now, focus on your calorie and macro targets." ŌĆö sentence case, no exclamation, leans the member back to the macros and water UI which are the productive parts of the surface.

**Surface 2 ŌĆö `log-food.html` full-page cover + boot suppression.**

The file already had `<div id="app" style="display:none">` as its root content wrapper ŌĆö auth.js flips this to visible after auth success (the standard PWA reveal pattern). The cover takes advantage of this: a `<div id="food-coming-soon-cover">` is inserted between `<body>` and `<div id="app">`, with a self-contained `<style>` block immediately above it (no dependency on the page's main stylesheet so it paints instantly even on a cold cache). Z-index 10000 sits above any modal/sheet the page might raise.

The cover is full-viewport, includes a top-bar back-link to `/nutrition.html`, a centered teal-tinted icon tile, "Food log ŌĆö coming soon" headline in Playfair Display, body copy in Inter muted, and a single `.fcs-cta` button styled to match the existing `.empty-cta` pattern on nutrition.html (linear-gradient teal-to-dark, gold-ish border accent, uppercase 0.85rem letter-spaced) ŌåÆ also routes to `/nutrition.html`. Member never reaches dead UI.

Even with the cover, the page's existing scripts would otherwise execute and (a) pre-paint cached diary state inside `#app`, (b) wire bus subscribers, (c) issue Supabase fetches on auth-success, (d) fire a skeleton-timeout false-positive alert to `platform-alert` EF after 10s (because `getComputedStyle` on `diary-skeleton` returns `display:block` even though its ancestor `#app` is hidden). Four boot points gated on `document.getElementById('food-coming-soon-cover')`:

1. `paintLogFoodCacheEarly()` IIFE (~L530) ŌĆö early-return before any DOM read/write, no Dexie cache hydration, no diary-content reveal attempt.
2. `loadPage()` async function (~L555) ŌĆö early-return before reading memberEmail. No Supabase fetches. No bus event publishing.
3. `DOMContentLoaded` listener for the `food:failed` revert subscriber (~L1605) ŌĆö early-return before registering. The page cannot publish `food:logged` / `food:deleted` events from anywhere (since loadPage never runs), so wiring the revert subscriber would be inert. Skipping cleanly avoids any subscriber-count diagnostics drifting.
4. Skeleton-timeout watchdog IIFE (~L1596) ŌĆö early-return before the 10s setTimeout. Prevents false-positive `skeleton_timeout_log-food` severity:high alerts to `platform-alert` EF that would otherwise spam the alert channel for every direct visitor.

All four guards use the same idiom: `if (document.getElementById('food-coming-soon-cover')) return;`. Reversal is a single sed sweep on that string. No JS logic was deleted or modified ŌĆö every gate is a no-op when the cover isn't present, so the file behaves identically to its pre-PM-374 state if the cover is removed.

**Files committed atomically (5):**
- `nutrition.html` ŌĆö CTA replacement (~95KB, blob `4c9e8a63`)
- `log-food.html` ŌĆö cover + style block prepended; 4 boot gates added (~91KB, blob `cb21ce54`)
- `index.html` ŌĆö vbb-marker 257 ŌåÆ 258 (130KB, blob `873df01a`)
- `settings.html` ŌĆö settings-vbb-marker 257 ŌåÆ 258 (134KB, blob `17ffd76c`)
- `sw.js` ŌĆö CACHE_NAME ŌåÆ `pm374-food-log-coming-soon-a` (18.9KB, blob `3a65b4d0`)

Git Data API atomic blobs/tree/commit/ref at vyve-site `761a027c` (parent `90d92495`).

**┬¦23.70 fired clean.** Session start saw vyve-site HEAD at `3244e596` (PM-371). Between mockup approval and the pre-commit recheck, two parallel sessions shipped: PM-372 (`30c2d23b` ŌĆö hydration COPY_TABLE ŌåÆ persona_welcome_copy catalogue) and PM-373 (`90d92495` ŌĆö habits.html tile palette parity with index.html PM-287). Both touched `index.html`, `settings.html`, `sw.js` ŌĆö three of my five staged files. PM-claim recomputed from 372 ŌåÆ 374. Re-fetched index/settings/sw.js at the new HEAD, replayed marker bumps on the post-PM-373 content (255 ŌåÆ 258, not 255 ŌåÆ 256). Source-comment sweep `PM-372 ŌåÆ PM-374` across nutrition.html (1 ref) + log-food.html (5 refs). `nutrition.html` and `log-food.html` were not in either parallel commit's file set ŌĆö substantive edits carried forward without rebase. The recheck-before-commit window saved the ship; without ┬¦23.70 the commit would have either parent-SHA-failed or, worse, succeeded against stale base_tree and silently reverted both parallel sessions' marker bumps + habits/hydration work.

**┬¦23.58 fired clean on brain commit.** Brain HEAD was `cafa5dfe` (PM-371) at session start; by brain-commit time two parallel brain commits had landed ŌĆö `ae3d1381` (PM-372 brain) and `1da18a3f` (PM-373 brain). Re-fetched master.md + changelog.md + backlog.md at current HEAD before composing edits. changelog.md (2.48MB) exceeds GitHub Contents API inline-content limit (~1MB) ŌĆö `content` field returned empty with `size:2487209` and a `download_url`. Switched to the Git Blob API (`/git/blobs/{sha}`) which returns base64-encoded content regardless of size. Codification: ┬¦23.79 candidate banked below.

**┬¦23 candidate banked (NOT codified ŌĆö first occurrence): >1MB file reads via Git Blob API, not Contents API.** GitHub Contents API silently truncates inline `content` to empty string for files >1MB; the file metadata (sha, size, download_url) returns normally so a naive caller writes an empty file to disk and proceeds. changelog.md is currently 2.48MB and growing; master.md (684KB) and backlog.md (734KB) are still under the cap but trending up. Discipline going forward: any brain file read should either use the Git Blob API directly (`GET /repos/.../git/blobs/{sha}`, base64-decode regardless of size) or check `size > 1_000_000` and route to Blob API conditionally. Promotion to ┬¦23 hard rule after a second occurrence ŌĆö single instance for now since this was a first encounter on this codebase. Memory-side workaround: the `download_url` field works for files of any size if you switch protocols to `raw.githubusercontent.com`, but that route has CDN cache lag (┬¦23.41 forbids using raw for post-commit verify; same hazard applies to mid-session re-reads of just-committed files). Blob API is the authoritative read path.

**Reversal recipe.** When food log is ready to ship widely: (1) in `log-food.html`, delete the `<style>` + `<div id="food-coming-soon-cover">` blocks between `<body>` and `<div id="app">`; (2) delete the four `// PM-374 Coming Soon lock` early-return lines (one in paintLogFoodCacheEarly, one in loadPage, one in DOMContentLoaded for bus subscribers, one in the skeleton-timeout IIFE); (3) in `nutrition.html`, replace the `<div id="food-coming-soon" class="coming-soon fade-up-3">` block with the original `<a id="food-coming-soon" href="/log-food.html" class="food-log-link fade-up-3">` anchor + sub-elements. No DB rollback, no EF redeploy, no bus subscriber re-registration ŌĆö every code path is identical to pre-PM-374 once the four gate-lines and the cover block are deleted.

**Out of scope (deferred to backlog).** Bus event scrubbing ŌĆö `food:logged` / `food:deleted` subscribers in `engagement-v2.js`, `achievements-evaluator.js`, and `home-state-local.js` remain registered. They cannot fire (no publisher reachable to members) but the dead subscriber wiring is cosmetic clutter to clean up if PM-374 is ever made permanent rather than coming-soon. Hard-lockdown follow-up would address this; for now, soft lockdown is intentionally reversibility-first.

**Verification.** 7 inline JS blocks node --check clean (3 nutrition.html + 4 log-food.html). sw.js standalone parse clean. First-100-char + byte-size post-commit re-fetch on all 5 files via Contents JSON (Blob API not needed for these ŌĆö all under 1MB). Commit ref update returned 200 with new HEAD `761a027c`.

## 2026-05-25 PM-373 ŌĆö habits.html icon tile palette brought to exact parity with index.html PM-287 [vyve-site `90d92495`]

**Surfaced.** PM-370 inlined Lucide glyphs into habits.html cards but kept the OLD POT_CONFIG palette for tile colours (sleep #6366B8 / movement #4DAAAA / nutrition #2D9E4A / mindfulness #5BA8D9 / social #E879A3) wired via inline `style="background:${pot.color}1A;color:${pot.color}"`. Dean tapped habits.html on device, compared to home, asked for palette parity. Index.html PM-287 uses an entirely different palette matched to the progress-ring colours ŌĆö mindfulness #B186C9 purple / movement #8FC470 green / nutrition #D89760 deep amber / sleep teal-lt / social #F0A075 orange ŌĆö plus a teal done-state override regardless of pot.

**The fix.** Decoupled the tile palette from POT_CONFIG entirely on habits.html, switched to CSS attribute-selector pattern mirroring index.html L803-832 exactly:

1. `.habit-pot-tile` base CSS now defaults to the same teal `rgba(77,170,170,0.15)` + `var(--teal-lt)` fallback that index.html `.habit-ic` uses (covers any habit with missing/unknown `habit_pot` ŌĆö graceful degrade, not a broken inline-style).
2. Five `.habit-card[data-pot="..."] .habit-pot-tile` selectors carry the per-pot palette ŌĆö byte-identical rgba values and hex codes copied from index.html L803-822.
3. `.habit-card.done .habit-pot-tile{background:rgba(77,170,170,0.20);color:var(--teal-lt)}` ŌĆö done-state override matching index.html L825-828.
4. `[data-theme="light"] .habit-card.done .habit-pot-tile{background:rgba(27,120,120,0.14);color:#1B7878}` ŌĆö light-theme done-state matching index.html L829-832 (PM-288 deep-teal-on-light treatment).
5. `habitCardHTML()` rewritten ŌĆö `data-pot="${h.habit_pot || ''}"` added to the `.habit-card` wrapper; inline tile style stripped; tile is now just `<div class="habit-pot-tile">${iconForHabit(...)}</div>`.

**What stayed.** The `.habit-card::before` 3px left rail still reads `--pot-color` from POT_CONFIG via the inline `style="--pot-color:${pot.color}"` on the card. Rail is a habits.html-only design element (no equivalent on index), so swapping it would have been scope-creep. POT_CONFIG colours are now ONLY the rail's source of truth on this page; tile colours are CSS-only. If we ever want to align the rail palette too, single-source POT_CONFIG to the index palette and the rail follows for free.

**┬¦23.70 fired clean.** Parent at commit time was `30c2d23b` (PM-372 hydration COPY_TABLE migration). Two parallel-session ships landed between PM-370 commit and PM-373 commit: PM-371 (weekly check-in recap window clamp, `3244e596`) and PM-372 (`30c2d23b`). Neither touched habits.html / settings.html / index.html / sw.js content, only the marker bumps overlapped. Re-fetched live HEAD versions of all 4 files immediately before applying our patches, replayed marker bumps 256 ŌåÆ 257 on the post-PM-372 content, swept the source-comment PM ref from `PM-371` (initial draft when HEAD was PM-370) to `PM-373` (final live HEAD when commit submitted). Base-tree-merge held ŌĆö none of PM-371/372's blob SHAs conflicted with ours.

**Files committed atomically (4):** habits.html (CSS palette swap + data-pot attribute on card markup), settings.html (vbb-marker 256ŌåÆ257), index.html (vbb-marker 256ŌåÆ257), sw.js (cache key pm372-persona-welcome-copy-a ŌåÆ pm373-habits-tile-palette-parity-a). Git Data API atomic at vyve-site `90d92495`. ┬¦23.41 fresh-HEAD-recheck fired clean. 3 inline JS blocks in habits.html node --check clean. First-100 char verify on all 4 files clean against committed SHA via Contents API.

Both still live as two-file duplicates because backlog L885 already tracks the proper structural fix (`habit_library.icon` column). ## 2026-05-25 PM-372 ŌĆö Hydration welcome COPY_TABLE migrated to Supabase catalogue (persona_welcome_copy) [vyve-site `30c2d23b`]

**Scope.** First migration off the Tier 2 hand-back from the content-surface audit Dean tabled at session start. The brief proposed retiring `sessions-data.js` to `service_catalogue` first (Tier 1.1) ŌĆö that's the surface PM-190.d and PM-333 deliberately moved AWAY from synchronous-load to avoid flash-of-empty hydrate, twice in the last four days. Reversing those calls without the admin editor in place re-opens a UX problem we already paid to fix. Pushed back on the brief, reordered: hydration.js COPY_TABLE first. Smallest, cleanest, Dean owns the copy outcome (`COPY: DEAN TO FINALISE` not `COPY: LEWIS REVIEW`), no editor dependency, the HAVEN hard rule and SAFE_ECHO_GOALS whitelist stay code-side because they're safety logic not content.

**Architectural shape (PM-367 pattern, verbatim).** Supabase catalogue table ŌåÆ sync.js pull on auth-ready ŌåÆ Dexie cache (5-min stale window via `CATALOGUE_FRESH_TABLES`) ŌåÆ page-side reader prefers Dexie, falls back to embedded const for cold paint. Optimistic synchronous render of the fallback line stays identical to pre-PM-372 paint; an async `resolveLiveTable(800)` kicks off in parallel and swaps `lineEl.textContent` in-place if a different live row resolves before the 1500ms minimum-display fade. No layout shift ŌĆö single text node update on a node already in the DOM. The overlay is mid-display; nobody sees the swap.

**Schema (`public.persona_welcome_copy`, migration `pm372_create_persona_welcome_copy`).** `id BIGINT IDENTITY PK ┬Ę persona TEXT CHECK IN (NOVA/RIVER/SPARK/SAGE/HAVEN/generic) ┬Ę goal TEXT NULL ┬Ę line TEXT ┬Ę active BOOL default true ┬Ę created_at/updated_at`. Unique index `(persona, COALESCE(goal,''))` ŌĆö one row per (persona, goal-or-generic). Tried `PRIMARY KEY (persona, COALESCE(...))` first, Postgres rejected expressions in PK; switched to surrogate `id` PK + unique expression index. RLS enabled, single `SELECT TO authenticated WHERE active=true` policy. Partial read index `(persona, goal) WHERE active=true` for the catalogue pull. `set_updated_at_persona_welcome_copy()` trigger. Seeded with the 13 verbatim COPY_TABLE rows from hydration.js ŌĆö `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` so re-runs are safe. HAVEN has only its generic row by design; the brief comment explicitly notes HAVEN goal-echo rows must never be inserted (code short-circuits before the table is read for HAVEN members, but documenting the contract on the table comment doubles up the safety).

**Sync wiring (db.js + sync.js).** db.js `SCHEMA_V17` adds `persona_welcome_copy: '&id, persona, goal, active'`, `db.version(17).stores(SCHEMA_V17)` registered, consumer registered via `makeCatalogueTable('persona_welcome_copy')`. sync.js catalogue plan entry hitting `/persona_welcome_copy?active=eq.true&select=*&order=persona.asc,goal.asc.nullsfirst`. `CATALOGUE_FRESH_TABLES` extended with the new table (5-min). `CATALOGUE_INVALIDATION_KEY` bumped `pm367-mind-videos` ŌåÆ `pm372-persona-welcome-copy` per ┬¦23.50 ŌĆö forces existing devices to re-pull on next visit so PM-367 mind-videos rows AND the new persona-welcome rows arrive in one refresh.

**hydration.js rewrite.** `COPY_TABLE` renamed to `FALLBACK_COPY_TABLE` (17 token replacements). New `buildTableFromRows(rows)` turns flat Supabase rows into the nested `{persona: {goal: line, generic: line}}` shape the picker has always consumed (NULL goal ŌåÆ `generic` key). New `resolveLiveTable(timeoutMs)` does the Dexie read with a hard 800ms Promise.race timeout ŌĆö a stuck IndexedDB cannot block the overlay past its minimum display. `pickLine()` gained a fourth arg `tableSource` defaulting to `FALLBACK_COPY_TABLE` so the existing `_pickLine` test hook keeps working unchanged. `maybeShow()` initial paint stays synchronous against the fallback table; the parallel Dexie resolver swaps `textContent` and recomputes `goalEchoUsed` against the live table if it wins. PostHog `welcome_hydration_shown` event gains `copy_source: 'dexie' | 'fallback'` so the dexie-vs-fallback split is observable in production.

**HAVEN short-circuit preserved.** `pickLine` still tests `if (persona === 'HAVEN')` BEFORE any goal lookup, regardless of which table is in play. Live table's HAVEN row is read only if it exists; if `liveTable.HAVEN.generic` is missing for any reason, code falls back to `FALLBACK_COPY_TABLE.HAVEN.generic` automatically. Belt-and-braces ŌĆö table-level invariant (no HAVEN goal rows seeded), code-level invariant (HAVEN short-circuits), fallback-level invariant (FALLBACK_COPY_TABLE preserved in JS).

**Operational implication for Dean.** Finalising copy is now a `UPDATE public.persona_welcome_copy SET line='...' WHERE persona='NOVA' AND goal='build strength'` in Supabase Studio. No vyve-site commit needed; device picks up on next login within the 5-min `CATALOGUE_FRESH_TABLES` window. Fallback const in hydration.js should stay in lockstep with finalised Supabase rows ŌĆö when copy is locked in production, sweep the const to match so cold-paint and live-paint converge. Forcing immediate pickup across all members can be done with another `CATALOGUE_INVALIDATION_KEY` bump (any vyve-site commit), but unnecessary for content-edit cadence.

**Ship details.** vyve-site `30c2d23b`. 6-file atomic via Git Data API (Composio still down 4 days post-incident; PAT-direct via Vault per memory #20). Files: db.js, sync.js, hydration.js, sw.js, index.html, settings.html. sw cache `pm371-recap-window-clamp-a` ŌåÆ `pm372-persona-welcome-copy-a`. vbb-marker 255 ŌåÆ 256 both index.html span and settings.html div. All three changed JS files `node --check` clean. ┬¦23.41 first-100 verify with MD5 match on all 6 files at commit SHA. ┬¦23.70 PM-claim fired correctly: initial draft was PM-370 at session start (max-PM-seen 369), recomputed to PM-372 at commit time after parallel sessions shipped PM-370 (settings habit picker) and PM-371 (recap window clamp) during the build. No collision in the commit window itself.

**Brain-load drift.** Session-start brain fetch landed clean. Pre-brain-commit re-fetch (┬¦23.58) revealed both `master.md` and `changelog.md` had drifted since session start ŌĆö parallel sessions had committed PM-370 and PM-371 brain entries while my build was in progress. Replayed the PM-372 brain edits against the live versions. `backlog.md` was unchanged (no parallel backlog edits ŌĆö consistent with PM-370 and PM-371 being non-backlog-adjacent ships).

**No new ┬¦23 hard rule earned.** This is ┬¦23.49 + ┬¦23.50 applied for the fifth time (service_catalogue PM-190, replay_playlists PM-235, replay_videos PM-245, mind_videos PM-367, persona_welcome_copy PM-372). Pattern is fully muscle memory.

**Tier 2 content-surface audit next moves.** The session began with a Claude-authored brief proposing four tiers of catalogue migrations. Audit pass against the live repo showed: brief was factually accurate on file existence and most marker counts, but strategically wrong on Tier 1.1 (sessions-data.js retirement reverses PM-190.d/PM-333 deliberate decisions) and inflated some marker counts (`COPY_LEWIS_REVIEW` appears in 2 file-header comments in mind-journal-prompts.js, not on 40 per-entry markers as the brief implied). Reordered priorities surfaced to Dean before code: Tier 2.1 hydration COPY_TABLE shipped (this PM), Tier 4 how-to-pdfs/how-to-videos as catalogue-reads from day one is next-natural, Tier 1.1 sessions-data.js stays parked until admin editor lands. Full audit notes in `/home/claude/brief_analysis.txt` from the session ŌĆö worth capturing into a `playbooks/content-surface-audit.md` if Dean wants the audit pattern preserved for future Claude-brief vetting.

## 2026-05-25 PM-371 ŌĆö Weekly check-in recap: rolling-7 window clamped both sides + habitDays Math.min(7) belt [vyve-site `3244e596`]

**Surfaced bug.** Recap card on `wellbeing-checkin.html` showed 8 days under Daily Habits in what's documented as a rolling 7-day window. Mathematically impossible by definition. Supabase truth for the member (`deanonbrown@hotmail.com`) for the last 8 days: 2 distinct dates (13 ticks across 25 May and 19 May). The 8 came from the PF-10 Dexie-first read path on line 1185ŌĆō1238 of `wellbeing-checkin.html` ŌĆö there's a ghost row in local IndexedDB that lexically passes the lower-bound `>= weekStart` filter but isn't one of the 7 real dates in the window.

**┬¦23.70 collision caught.** A parallel session shipped PM-370 (habits.html difficulty-tag strip + settings habit picker, `5703c9da`) ~90 seconds before my pre-commit recheck. PM-370 modified index.html + settings.html + sw.js ŌĆö three of the four files I had locally staged from the post-PM-369 fresh-HEAD fetch. Per ┬¦23.70, re-fetched all three files from the new post-PM-370 HEAD, replayed the marker bumps (254 ŌåÆ 255) on the fresh content, swept PM-370 ŌåÆ PM-371 in `wellbeing-checkin.html` source comments. wellbeing-checkin.html itself was not in PM-370's file set, so the substantive edits there carried forward cleanly without rebase. The ┬¦23.70 pre-commit recheck did its job ŌĆö without it the commit would have either failed on stale parent SHA or, worse, succeeded with stale base_tree and silently reverted PM-370's habits.html changes.

**Three changes in `wellbeing-checkin.html` shape() helper (lines 1080ŌĆō1183).**

1. **Window upper bound.** `weekEnd = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10)` added alongside `weekStart`. `inWindow` updated from `r[dateCol] >= weekStart` to `r[dateCol] >= weekStart && r[dateCol] <= weekEnd`. Closes the future-date class ŌĆö any Dexie row with a tomorrow-or-later `activity_date` (clock skew at write time, timezone roll, malformed sync envelope) no longer passes the local string filter regardless of how badly Dexie has drifted.

2. **habitDays clamped to 7.** `const habitDays = Math.min(7, new Set(habits.map(h => h.activity_date)).size)`. Mathematical truth as belt-and-braces: a rolling-7 window contains at most 7 distinct dates by definition, so clamping is free and catches future regressions (a filter typo, a Supabase URL that drops the gte/lte combo, a malformed date string that lexically passes both bounds but isn't one of the 7 actual dates). Cheaper than another diagnostic round in three months.

3. **No change to body/mind/connect totals.** Those pillars sum raw activity counts (not distinct days) so the 7-day-cap doesn't apply, and the raw counts were correct. The same `inWindow` upper-bound fix protects all four pillars from the same ghost-row class ŌĆö habits is just where the bug surfaced first because `distinct days` makes a phantom date instantly visible.

**Architectural follow-up banked (NOT actioned this session).** PF-10 Dexie-first reads have no equivalence check against Supabase canonical truth at read time. Sync brings new rows in but doesn't reconcile (a) deletions, (b) stale state from a write the server later rejected, (c) clock-skew ghosts. This same class of drift will surface on engagement-v2, home pills, scoring, anywhere PF-10 is the read source. The right architectural fix is a Dexie reconciliation pass on `visibilitychange` ŌĆö full member-scoped fetch of the last 30 days from Supabase, replace Dexie rows for that window. Banked to backlog as PF-30+ companion. Until then, every PF-10 surface needs defensive bounds at the read site, like this recap.

**┬¦23 candidate ŌĆö defensive bounds on PF-10 reads.** Banking the principle: any PF-10 Dexie-first read that filters by a date range MUST bound both sides (`>= start && <= end`) at the client filter, not just lower-bound. Reason: Dexie is a write-through local store with no integrity check at read time, so a stale or future-dated row passes lower-bound-only filters silently. This is the second drift bug surfaced in PF-10 reads after the engagement-v2 stale-row class ŌĆö promoting to a ┬¦23 rule after one more independent occurrence per ┬¦23 hard-rule promotion criteria.

**Files.** wellbeing-checkin.html (window math, 3 edits), sw.js (CACHE_NAME ŌåÆ `pm371-recap-window-clamp-a`), index.html (vbb-marker 255), settings.html (settings-vbb-marker 255). 4-file atomic commit via Git Data API blobs/tree/commit/ref sequence ŌĆö the `GITHUB_COMMIT_MULTIPLE_FILES` Composio path was unavailable this session (tool_search returned no Composio surface), fell back to direct PAT-from-Vault per memory #8.
