## 2026-05-25 PM-370 ŌĆö Strip easy/medium/hard difficulty tags from Settings habit picker + bring habits.html icons to parity with home (Lucide per-habit glyphs) [vyve-site `5703c9da`]

**Scope.** Two unrelated UI cleanups on a single shipping branch ŌĆö Dean's brief covered both, ships atomically.

**Surface 1: settings.html ŌĆö Manage Habits modal.** Three difficulty surfaces removed:
- "My habits" custom row render at L1551 ŌĆö `<div class="habit-difficulty ${h.difficulty}">${h.difficulty}</div>` deleted.
- Library row render at L1576 ŌĆö same line, same removal.
- Custom-habit creation form L851-863 ŌĆö `<select id="hc-difficulty">` block entirely removed; `<select id="hc-pot">` stays as the sole content of the `.habit-create-row`.
- `toggleCreateForm()` close-reset path: `document.getElementById('hc-difficulty').value = 'easy'` removed.
- `saveCustomHabit()`: the `getElementById('hc-difficulty').value` read removed; `difficulty` now hardcoded `'medium'` in the POST body so the `habit_library.difficulty` column stays populated for any future re-introduction.
- `.habit-difficulty` CSS at L304-326 retained dead-but-harmless. `habit_library.difficulty` DB column untouched (forward-compat over migration discipline). 4 inline JS blocks node --check clean.

**Surface 2: habits.html ŌĆö icon parity with index.html PM-286.** habits.html was rendering an 8px coloured `.habit-pot-dot` next to each habit title ŌĆö no per-habit glyph. Home page (index.html) since PM-286 has rendered the proper Lucide icon per habit (heart-pulse for cardio, footprints for walking, brain for meditation, etc.) in a pot-tinted 36px tile per PM-287. Dean's brief: "edit the symbols so that they match what is in today's habits on the front page." Three changes:

1. Lucide `<symbol id="hi-...">` defs block (36 symbols, ~9KB) inlined immediately after `<body>` open on habits.html ŌĆö verbatim copy of the PM-286 block on index.html (line 1031 onward).
2. `iconForHabit(title, pot)` + `_habitIconSvg(name)` functions inlined into the page script, placed immediately above `habitCardHTML()` ŌĆö verbatim copy of the index.html lookup tables (lines 1583-1660). Both files now host duplicate copies of the lookup table ŌĆö flagged as a ┬¦23 candidate ("either of: extract to a shared module, or land a Dexie-driven `habit_library.icon` column and a single fallback function" ŌĆö backlog L885 already tracks the icon-column work).
3. `habitCardHTML()` card markup: bare `.habit-pot-dot` replaced with `.habit-pot-tile` carrying the icon ŌĆö `<div class="habit-pot-tile" style="background:${pot.color}1A;color:${pot.color}">${iconForHabit(h.habit_title, h.habit_pot)}</div>`. The `${pot.color}1A` suffix is the 10% alpha hex (parity with PM-287's pot-tinted-tile pattern); `color:${pot.color}` lets the Lucide `currentColor` paths pick up the tint automatically.
4. CSS additions: `.habit-pot-tile{flex-shrink:0;width:36px;height:36px;border-radius:10px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;background:var(--pot-color);margin-top:2px}` + `.habit-pot-tile .habit-ic-svg{stroke:currentColor;stroke-width:2;fill:none;stroke-linecap:round;stroke-linejoin:round}`. Dead `.habit-pot-dot` CSS retained.

**Result.** The cardio habit card on habits.html now shows the same heart-pulse glyph the home page shows; walking shows footprints; both tiles tinted to the same pot colour. Glyph + tint exactly match between index.html and habits.html for every habit_library title (34 exact-match titles in `iconForHabit`'s HABIT_MAP, 19 keyword-fallback regexes covering legacy/non-library habits, 5 pot fallbacks, `circle-dashed` generic). 3 inline JS blocks node --check clean.

**Files committed atomically (4):** settings.html, habits.html (gains 36 Lucide symbol defs + iconForHabit/_habitIconSvg + tile CSS + tile in card), index.html (vbb-marker 253ŌåÆ254), sw.js (cache key pm369-settings-toggle-haptics-a ŌåÆ pm370-habits-icons-difficulty-strip-a). Git Data API atomic at vyve-site `5703c9da`. ┬¦23.41 fresh-HEAD-recheck fired clean (parent `026dee9e7f...`, PM-369 still HEAD, claim PM-370 uncontested). First-100-char post-commit re-fetch verify all 4 files clean against the committed SHA via Contents API (not raw CDN).

**Banked, not codified.** Duplicated iconForHabit lookup tables across index.html and habits.html ŌĆö both files have identical 34-title HABIT_MAP + 19-regex keyword fallback + 5-pot fallback + `circle-dashed` generic. If a third surface needs the same lookup (settings habit-picker is the obvious next candidate ŌĆö could give the modal-picker rows the same icon treatment instead of plain text), extract to `/habit-icons.js`. Until then, the discipline is: edit both files together, or wait for the `habit_library.icon` column (backlog L885) and reduce the function to a one-line fallback. Not promoting to ┬¦23 yet ŌĆö single occurrence of the duplication, and the backlog item already tracks the proper fix.

## 2026-05-25 PM-369 ŌĆö Settings toggle haptics: 11 new wires on settings.html, palette now fully populated [vyve-site `026dee9e`]

**Scope.** Closes out backlog item #7 from the PM-278 haptics adoption list (settings toggle switches). `settings.html` had 2 pre-existing PM-359 wires (`vyveSetTheme` + `handleToggle`, both `selection()`). This commit adds 11 more across every meaningful write/toggle surface on the page, bringing the file to 13 haptic call sites total.

**The wires:**

`setDisplayNamePref(pref)` ŌĆö leaderboard name privacy buttons (anonymous/initials/first_name/full_name):
- Synchronous flip ŌåÆ `selection()` immediately after `markActiveNamePrefBtn(pref)` ŌĆö locks to the gesture, NOT the awaited `supaFetch` PATCH (PM-368 lesson). A no-op guard added (`if (pref === prev) return`) ŌĆö tapping an already-active button no longer fires a haptic.
- Revert path on `supaFetch` failure ŌåÆ `error()`. UI undoes itself; the haptic confirms the failure to the member who's already moved their attention elsewhere.

`savePersona()` ŌĆö persona commit:
- Haptic placed at the synchronous bus-publish moment (line ~1330, BEFORE the Dexie `await` at line ~1347 which can stall on iOS WKWebView per PM-368). `success()` ŌĆö persona switch is a meaningful configuration commit, not a low-stakes toggle.
- The button's disabled+spinner state visually carries the "saving" affordance during the post-haptic Dexie wait. The haptic itself confirms the gesture committed.

`toggleHabit(id)` ŌĆö habit picker modal selection:
- Both add and remove branches ŌåÆ `selection()` (picker semantics, both directions get the same tick ŌĆö no winner).
- Cap-reached path (`_selectedHabitIds.size >= 10`) ŌåÆ **`warning()` ŌĆö FIRST USE in the codebase.** Bridge has had `warning()` since PM-277 but nothing has called it. Bridge docs: "caution". Fits the cap-rejection cleanly. Closes the "warning unused" caveat noted in PM-365's brain entry ŌĆö the palette is now fully populated.

`saveHabits()` ŌĆö habits commit:
- Modal-close moment (line ~1707, after Dexie/bus/cache writes complete) ŌåÆ `success()`. Per ┬¦23.39 / PM-151: network is fire-and-forget background. Haptic locks to the perceived "saved" moment.
- Background-write failure publishes `habits:set-changed:failed` and `habits.html` handles the optimistic revert silently. No counter-haptic ŌĆö the member has already moved on; surfacing the failure haptically on a now-closed modal would be confusing.

`saveCustomHabit()`:
- Optimistic-render success (form close + new habit appears in list) ŌåÆ `success()`. Mirrors PM-365 log-food add pattern.
- `catch` path ŌåÆ `error()`.

`deleteCustomHabit()`:
- Optimistic delete-render ŌåÆ `medium()`. Matches PM-366 `deleteCustomWorkout` pattern. Note unlike PM-366: this flow's `renderHabitsList()` runs AFTER the awaited `supaFetch` DELETEs (not before). Acceptable because delete latency on small Supabase rows is typically <100ms and the `confirm()` dialog is the user's real commitment gate.
- `catch` path ŌåÆ `error()`.

**Skipped surfaces.** `toggleCreateForm()` (the open-the-create-form gesture, no write) ŌĆö too low-value, would over-buzz. Auth/sign-out ŌĆö security-critical surfaces shouldn't have feedback that could be confused with a "saved" signal.

**Files (3, atomic at `026dee9e`):**
- `settings.html` ŌĆö 11 new haptic wires + 1 no-op guard added to `setDisplayNamePref`
- `index.html` ŌĆö vbb-marker 252 ŌåÆ 253
- `sw.js` ŌĆö cache `pm368-home-habit-haptic-realign-a` ŌåÆ `pm369-settings-toggle-haptics-a`

**Marker invariant recovered.** PM-367 (parallel session, mind-videos) and PM-368 (mine, habit haptic realign) both bumped 251 ŌåÆ 252, leaving marker 252 dual-claimed in the brain narrative. PM-369 ŌåÆ 253 restores per-PM uniqueness for the marker semantics going forward. Cache key (pm369-...) is unambiguous as always.

**Verification.** All 4 inline JS blocks `node --check` clean. sw.js standalone parse clean. ┬¦23.41 first-100 verify all 3 files at SHA `026dee9e` clean. Remote `VYVEHaptics` call-site count in settings.html = 13 (expected 13). ┬¦23.70 PM-claim fired correctly: parent `f33a231a` (PM-368) ŌåÆ claim 369, no parallel-session drift in the commit window.

**Palette codification ŌĆö ┬¦23.74 candidate banked.** With PM-369 the haptic palette is now consistently used across **7 PMs and 8 surfaces** with no internal contradictions:

| Method | Use case | First PM | Last PM |
|---|---|---|---|
| `selection()` | pickers, toggles, stepper +/- | PM-359 | PM-369 |
| `light()` | un-ticks, optimistic deletes from a list | PM-364 | PM-368 |
| `medium()` | destructive commits, confirmations | PM-366 | PM-369 |
| `success()` | additive logs | PM-359 | PM-369 |
| `heavy()` | achievement tier reveal | PM-363 | (single) |
| `error()` | failure paths | PM-365 | PM-369 |
| `warning()` | caution / rejection | PM-369 | (just landed) |

Rule shape proposal for next codification pass: "These are the seven haptic call shapes; use the closest semantic match. Reach for a heavier band only when the moment is qualitatively heavier (additive log ŌēĀ destructive commit ŌēĀ achievement reveal). Reach for a lighter band when the action is a correction or chrome interaction." Codifying as ┬¦23.74 (the prior ┬¦23.73-candidate PM-356-banked "helper-context-extension" lesson is still on the bench; it gets ┬¦23.75 when codified).

**No additional new ┬¦23 hard rule earned this commit.** The PM-368-lesson "haptic at synchronous UI-flip site" was applied repeatedly here (`setDisplayNamePref`, `savePersona`) but is still single-occurrence at the codification threshold. Banked.

## 2026-05-25 PM-368 ŌĆö Home habit haptic re-aligned: synchronous tap-flip site + untick branch added [vyve-site `f33a231a`]

**Dean's report.** "haptics aren't on the habits on index yet" ŌĆö said after PM-360 had already shipped a wire in `index.html` `togglePill()`. Investigation showed PM-360's haptic was placed at line 2270, **after** `await VYVELocalDB.daily_habits.upsert(...)`. On iOS WKWebView the indexedDB transaction can queue and the await stall 100-400ms. The haptic landed long after the tap, decoupled from the gesture, so Dean's read was correct: from the user's POV nothing fired.

**Root cause.** ┬¦23.39-adjacent. The optimistic-first contract says "flip UI synchronously, then un-awaited background work." PM-360 followed the data half correctly (Dexie + REST optimistic) but stitched the haptic to the awaited Dexie branch instead of to the synchronous UI flip. The haptic IS part of the UI flip ŌĆö it belongs in the same animation frame as the `.classList.add('ticked')` (workouts precedent) or the `renderHabitList()` (home precedent). Treating the haptic as post-write feedback breaks the immediacy.

**Fix.** Moved haptic dispatch to fire IMMEDIATELY after `renderHabitList()` at line 2253, before any await begins. Same animation frame as the visual flip. Both branches haptic'd:
- Tick   (`next === true`) ŌåÆ `VYVEHaptics.success()` ŌĆö matches habits.html PM-359 manual-flow pattern (Dean's "feels really good" reference)
- Untick (`next === false`) ŌåÆ `VYVEHaptics.light()` ŌĆö overrides PM-360's deliberate "untick stays silent" call

PM-360's "don't celebrate correcting a mistake" rationale loses out to "a dead-feeling untick tap is the bigger UX problem". The workouts-set-untick precedent (PM-364) has settled at `light()` and the home habit untick should match. Removed the now-redundant PM-360 haptic call at the old line 2270.

**Files (3, atomic at `f33a231a`):**
- `index.html` ŌĆö `togglePill()` rewrite, haptic block inserted at synchronous flip site, old wire removed
- `settings.html` ŌĆö settings-vbb-marker 251 ŌåÆ 252
- `sw.js` ŌĆö cache `pm366-custom-workout-delete-haptic-a` ŌåÆ `pm368-home-habit-haptic-realign-a`

**Verification.** 9 inline JS blocks node --check clean. First-100 post-commit re-fetch on all 3 files at SHA `f33a231a` clean. Targeted lookup confirms `VYVEHaptics.success()` + `.light()` dispatch at line 2253-2264; old PM-360 haptic site at line 2270 gone.

**Process notes.** ┬¦23.70 fired at commit-time. Parallel session shipped PM-367 (Mind videos Supabase catalogue, vyve-site `5fb88b63`) between session restart and HEAD recheck ŌĆö PM claim re-computed 367ŌåÆ368. Verified PM-367's 4-file touch on mind.html / meditation.html / sleep.html / visualisation.html did NOT drop my PM-364 haptics.js inserts (base-tree-merge held; all 4 still have haptics.js refs). **Marker collision noted**: both PM-367 and PM-368 bumped 251 ŌåÆ 252. Live marker is 252, but it now covers two ship narratives. Cache key (`pm368-...`) is unambiguous; only the vbb-marker UI string is dual-claimed. Next vyve-site commit should bump to 253 to recover per-PM uniqueness.

**Lesson ŌĆö not yet a ┬¦23 rule.** "Haptic dispatch belongs at the synchronous UI-flip site, not gated behind an awaited write." Single-occurrence right now (PM-360 ŌåÆ PM-368 is the only instance), so banking rather than codifying. If a second haptic surface gets this wrong it earns a ┬¦23 entry ŌĆö would probably be a sharpening of ┬¦23.39 explicitly listing haptics as part of the UI-flip phase, not a standalone rule.

## 2026-05-25 PM-367 ŌĆö Mind videos Supabase catalogue: mind.html / meditation.html / sleep.html / visualisation.html read from mind_videos table [vyve-site `5fb88b63`]

**Scope.** Dean's call: "I need it linked so that, um, if I wanna update the videos in the future, I can just do it because eventually we're gonna just be uploading and uploading our own links rather than, um, doing it through, like, hard coordinate into the page." Mind v1 (PM-173 ŌåÆ PM-183, 20 May) shipped with the YouTube IDs / titles / sub-copy / duration / hero status baked into HTML literals across four files ŌĆö the meditation.html / sleep.html / visualisation.html hero blocks + streams grid plus mind.html's FOCUS_POOL daily rotation. Lewis/Calum will eventually replace these with real produced content; in the meantime Dean wants to edit live in Supabase without a vyve-site commit per swap.

**Why this was hardcoded in the first place.** Master ┬¦19 line 1544 captured it correctly ŌĆö "YouTube embed bridge for meditation/sleep/visualisation pending ElevenLabs/Calum real audio". The whole Mind v1 was sequenced ahead of the real audio/video production, so the placeholder approach was hardcode-and-ship. `affirmations_library` and `breathwork_patterns` got the catalogue treatment (Lewis can edit live) but videos didn't, because videos were always going to be replaced wholesale by Calum/ElevenLabs production. Now the wholesale-replacement model is being re-scoped to ongoing-incremental-replacement, so the catalogue pattern is the right shape.

**Design choice (Option A in talk-first).** Single `mind_videos` table with `kind` discriminator drives which sub-page surfaces each row. The alternative (separate `featured_in_pool` flag for the daily-rotation curation) was banked for later ŌĆö the current pool is just "everything we have minus ambient sleep tracks", and when Calum's content lands the pool growing alongside the category pages is fine. If pool curation becomes a thing later we add the flag then; the column add is trivial.

**Schema (`public.mind_videos`).** youtube_video_id TEXT PK ┬Ę kind TEXT CHECK IN ('meditation','sleep','visualisation') ┬Ę title ┬Ę sub ┬Ę duration_minutes INT ┬Ę is_hero BOOL ┬Ę display_order INT ┬Ę active BOOL default true ┬Ę created_at / updated_at. Partial unique index `idx_mind_videos_one_hero_per_kind ON (kind) WHERE is_hero=true` enforces one hero per kind. Primary read index `(kind, display_order) WHERE active=true`. `set_updated_at` trigger. RLS enabled, single SELECT policy for authenticated reading active rows. Seeded with 12 rows from the existing hardcoded sets (5 meditation, 4 sleep, 3 visualisation), is_hero matched to the static hero on each sub-page (cvmFC3IAbOg / g0jfhRcXtLQ / _ZeEPo8w-n8). Supabase migration `pm363_create_mind_videos` ŌĆö the migration name carries the early-session PM-363 claim that collided with the parallel haptics-tier-reveal ship; migration names are append-only Supabase history, the canonical brain reference is PM-367.

**Sync wiring (db.js + sync.js).** db.js SCHEMA_V16 adds `mind_videos: '&youtube_video_id, kind, is_hero, display_order, active'`, db.version(16).stores(SCHEMA_V16) registered, `mind_videos: makeCatalogueTable('mind_videos')` consumer registered next to replay_videos. sync.js catalogue plan entry hitting `/mind_videos?active=eq.true&select=*&order=kind.asc,display_order.asc`. CATALOGUE_FRESH_TABLES extended (5-min stale window). CATALOGUE_INVALIDATION_KEY bumped `pm251-live-state-machine` ŌåÆ `pm367-mind-videos` per ┬¦23.50 ŌĆö forces one refresh on next visit so existing devices pick up the schema change. Both behaviours canonical per ┬¦23.49/┬¦23.50.

**mind.html rewire.** FOCUS_POOL renamed to FALLBACK_POOL, preserved verbatim for cold-load paint. New `loadFocusPoolFromDexie()` filters `mind_videos` to rotation-eligible entries (duration_minutes IS NOT NULL ŌĆö preserves pre-PM-367 exclusion of Rain Sounds + Ocean Waves ambient tracks) and maps Supabase column names back to the legacy {{video_id, kind, title, sub}} shape consumed by pickFocus + renderFocus. Pool refresh wired via `vyve-localdb-table-pulled` bus listener AND a 1000ms probe timer (covers both the late-listener-attach case and the hydrate-already-landed case). pickFocus normalises `youtube_video_id` ŌåÆ `video_id` so renderFocus / openPlayer paths are byte-identical to PM-183.

**meditation.html / sleep.html / visualisation.html rewire.** Static .vz-hero block (the big hero card at top) and static .streams grid (the 3-5 stream-card children) gutted to empty containers. Inline render script paints hero + cards from `VYVELocalDB.mind_videos` filtered by PAGE_KIND. Hero title/sub overrides preserved per page (sleep.html hero shows "Wind Down" not the row's title "Sleep Meditation"; visualisation.html hero shows "Your Calm Place" not "The Beach"; meditation.html hero shows the row's title since it matches the static "Calm Your Mind"). Fallback array embedded in each page so cold-load paints the original content immediately, replaced by Dexie hydrate.

**Click handler rewrite (event delegation).** The static binding `document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => { document.querySelectorAll('[data-video]').forEach(el => el.addEventListener('click', ...)) })` runs once at parse time, before my dynamic render script repaints the cards ŌĆö dynamically-injected cards would have no handler. Rewritten to a single delegated listener on `document.body` matching `e.target.closest('[data-video]')` ŌĆö survives any future re-render cleanly. openPlayer stays in the IIFE closure scope, called from the delegated handler. This is also a meaningful resilience improvement for any future Mind surface changes; the old pattern was a latent bug waiting for someone to inject content.

**Ship details.** vyve-site `5fb88b63`. 9-file atomic via Git Data API (Composio still down ŌĆö 21 May outage continues, four days now; PAT-direct via Vault `GITHUB_PAT_CLAUDE` per memory #20). Files: mind.html / meditation.html / sleep.html / visualisation.html / db.js / sync.js / sw.js / index.html / settings.html. sw cache `pm366-custom-workout-delete-haptic-a` ŌåÆ `pm367-mind-videos-supabase-a`. vbb-marker 251 ŌåÆ 252 both index.html span and settings.html div. All 13 inline JS blocks `node --check` clean. ┬¦23.41 first-100 verify on all 9 files at commit SHA matched. ┬¦23.52(b) files[].status: 9├Ś modified, 0├Ś added/removed/renamed. ┬¦23.70 PM-claim fired correctly ŌĆö initial draft was PM-363 (max-PM-seen at session-start was 362), recomputed mid-build when commit-message list re-read showed PM-363 through PM-366 had shipped during the build (four parallel-session haptics ships in ~3 hours), claim re-bumped to PM-367 cleanly. Build-script PM-XXX placeholder discipline (memory #21) saved the rename cascade ŌĆö single sed sweep at recompute time.

**Operational implication for Dean.** Editing video content is now a Supabase Table Editor task. Insert a new row (specify youtube_video_id + kind + title + sub + duration_minutes + display_order + active=true) ŌåÆ device picks up on next visit within the 5-min CATALOGUE_FRESH_TABLES window. Updating an existing row: same. Removing a video: set `active=false` (preserves history) rather than DELETE. To force immediate pickup across all members rather than waiting for the 5-min window, bump `CATALOGUE_INVALIDATION_KEY` in sync.js and ship a vyve-site commit ŌĆö but this should be reserved for schema changes, not content edits.

**Future moves not in scope this ship.** (1) A `featured_in_pool` boolean if curation diverges from catalogue contents (banked above). (2) Per-kind hero override fields on `mind_videos` (current hero copy is hardcoded into the page-specific HERO_TITLE_OVERRIDE / HERO_SUB_OVERRIDE map ŌĆö works fine, but if Lewis wants the hero copy to also be edit-live we'd add `hero_title` + `hero_sub` columns and drop the overrides). (3) Eventually-coming admin UI at admin.vyvehealth.co.uk/mind-videos for non-technical edit access. (4) Achievement integration ŌĆö currently mind-section watch achievements live on `mind_activities` not `mind_videos`, no schema implication.

**No new ┬¦23 hard rule earned.** This is ┬¦23.49 + ┬¦23.50 (DB-driven catalogue imagery contract + invalidation key bump on schema change) applied for the fourth time (service_catalogue PM-190, replay_playlists PM-235, replay_videos PM-245, mind_videos PM-367). The pattern is well-established muscle memory now.

## 2026-05-25 PM-366 ŌĆö Custom workout delete haptics: medium() on confirmed delete, error() on failure [vyve-site `61876943`]

**Scope.** Backlog line 581 listed "swipe-to-delete confirm on the custom workouts list" as a haptic target. Audit revealed the swipe gesture itself isn't built yet ŌĆö current shipped flow is a ├Ś button + native `confirm()` dialog. Wired the haptics into what's actually there now; the threshold-crossing haptic for the eventual swipe gesture will be a one-line add at the gesture-confirm callback when the gesture lands.

**Build.** Single function patched (`deleteCustomWorkout` in `workouts-builder.js` lines 138-205). Three haptic call sites:
- Server reject (`!res.ok`) ŌåÆ `VYVEHaptics.error()` before the failure alert
- Server 2xx ŌåÆ optimistic in-memory filter + `renderCustomWorkouts()` ŌåÆ `VYVEHaptics.medium()` at the visual flip
- `catch` block (network throw) ŌåÆ `VYVEHaptics.error()` before the catch alert

The visual flip is the perceived "deleted" moment ŌĆö the row vanishes from the list. Fired there, NOT after the background `loadCustomWorkouts()` reconcile, because on slow networks the reconcile can take 200-500ms and a haptic at that point would feel uncoupled from the original tap. Native `confirm()` is the user's commitment gate; once they confirm and the server says OK, the haptic locks to the row-vanish.

**Style choice ŌĆö why `medium()`.** Heavier than the `light()` set-untick on `workouts-session.js` because deletion is a destructive commit; lighter than `success()` which we now reserve for additive logs (food, weight, set-tick, TDEE save) ŌĆö that reservation is implicit in PM-363/364/365's pattern and is starting to look like a real palette. Matches the bridge's documented "long-press confirm" energy band. Destructive commits and confirmations cluster at medium.

**Files (4, atomic at `61876943`):**
- `workouts-builder.js` ŌĆö 3 try/catch single-line haptic calls inserted, narrative comments above each
- `index.html` ŌĆö vbb-marker 250 ŌåÆ 251
- `settings.html` ŌĆö settings-vbb-marker 250 ŌåÆ 251
- `sw.js` ŌĆö cache `pm365-nutrition-haptics-a` ŌåÆ `pm366-custom-workout-delete-haptic-a`

**Verification.** `node --check` clean on workouts-builder.js (parses standalone ŌĆö it's a script-tag loadee). First-100-char post-commit re-fetch on all 4 files pinned to commit SHA `61876943` ŌĆö all match. `VYVEHaptics.medium` + `.error` ├Ś 2 confirmed in remote at line 160 / 185 / 191. Cache key confirmed. workouts.html loads workouts-builder.js (line 518) AND loads haptics.js (PM-364 sweep) ŌĆö wiring is live.

**No new ┬¦23 hard rule earned.** PM-359-style "wire what's actually shipped, not what's on the backlog" lesson reinforces a discipline already implicit in ┬¦23.69 (feature-collision detection); the inverse-direction case (backlog item references a gesture that doesn't exist yet, don't go build the gesture as a side-quest) is just a corollary. Worth a one-line addition to ┬¦23.69 next time it's touched, not a separate rule.

## 2026-05-25 PM-365 ŌĆö Nutrition + food-log haptics: water stepper, weight log, TDEE save, food add/delete [vyve-site `d328c9c7`]

**Scope.** Wires `VYVEHaptics` to the write actions on `nutrition.html` and `log-food.html` now that the bridge is loaded platform-wide (PM-364). Strict scope: write actions and their outcomes only. Mode toggles, range tabs (7d/30d/90d), unit buttons (kg/st/lb), and goal/activity pills left bare ŌĆö wiring every UI control would over-buzz the page.

**Investigation surfaced one architectural correction.** Brain referenced a home-page hydration sibling at `index.html ~line 2407` needing a parallel wire. There isn't one. Line 2407 is a Dexie-hydrate `vyve-localdb-table-pulled` comment (table hydration), not water-hydration UI. The only water/weight UI lives on `nutrition.html`. **`hydration.js`** is the PF-13 Dexie-hydration welcome overlay (PM-92 ŌĆö persona greeting + COPY_TABLE awaiting Dean finalisation), completely unrelated to water tracking. Naming clash flagged here so future sessions don't hunt for a phantom renderer. See ┬¦23.73 codification below for the broader bridge-load lesson.

**Wires on `nutrition.html`:**
- `adjustWater(+/-)` ŌåÆ `selection()` per tap (bridge's documented stepper use case ŌĆö subtle, members tap 4-8├Ś to fill the day, heavier haptic would over-buzz). Threshold-crossing tap (`willHitTarget` guard ensures it fires exactly once on the tap that crosses target, not every tap once at target) upgrades to `success()`.
- `logWeight()` success ŌåÆ `success()` at the optimistic chart-paint moment (saveWtLog returns post-Dexie-patch, before network; PM-60 local-first contract ŌĆö haptic locked to paint, not network resolve).
- `logWeight()` catch ŌåÆ `error()`.
- `saveTargets()` success ŌåÆ `success()` (TDEE/configuration commit ŌĆö heavier than per-stepper selection because this is a real commit moment).
- `saveTargets()` catch ŌåÆ `error()`.

**Wires on `log-food.html`:**
- Food add: optimistic `renderDiary()` at line ~1232 ŌåÆ `success()`. Fires regardless of online/offline since the optimistic paint is identical in both paths. `food:failed` dead-letter revert (PM-62 in writeQueued tail) does NOT get a counter-haptic ŌĆö silent recovery contract.
- Food delete: optimistic `renderDiary()` at line ~814 ŌåÆ `light()`. Lower-energy than the insert `success()` ŌĆö mirrors the workouts-session.js untick `light()` pattern.

**Files (5, atomic at `d328c9c7`):**
- `nutrition.html` ŌĆö 7 haptic call sites total (3 in adjustWater, 2 in logWeight, 2 in saveTargets)
- `log-food.html` ŌĆö 2 haptic call sites (1 in food add success path, 1 in food delete optimistic path)
- `index.html` ŌĆö vbb-marker 249 ŌåÆ 250
- `settings.html` ŌĆö settings-vbb-marker 249 ŌåÆ 250
- `sw.js` ŌĆö cache `pm364-haptics-sweep-a` ŌåÆ `pm365-nutrition-haptics-a`

**Verification.** 7 inline scripts across both files `node --check` clean. First-100 re-fetch all 5 files at SHA `d328c9c7` clean. Spot-checked `VYVEHaptics.selection|success|error|light` all present at expected lines. Cache key + marker bumps verified.

**Emerging haptic palette (worth noting for future surfaces).** Looking across PM-359/360/363/364/365/366:
- `selection()` ŌåÆ stepper/picker/toggle ticks (water +/-)
- `light()` ŌåÆ un-ticks, optimistic deletes from a list (food-row, workout-set untick)
- `medium()` ŌåÆ destructive commits, confirmations (custom workout delete)
- `success()` ŌåÆ additive logs (set logged, food logged, weight logged, TDEE saved, habit ticked)
- `heavy()` ŌåÆ achievement tier reveal (one moment, big feel)
- `error()` ŌåÆ failure path
- `warning()` ŌåÆ not yet used anywhere ŌĆö reserved for "are you sure?" pre-confirm states

This isn't yet a rule, but it's consistent across six PMs of wiring. Promote to a ┬¦23-banked palette doc on next haptic ship if the pattern holds.

## 2026-05-25 PM-364 ŌĆö Haptics bridge swept to 38 activity surfaces + workouts-session set-tick wired [vyve-site `ddba1577`]

**The bug PM-363 surfaced.** PM-359/360 wired `haptics.js` into `index.html` / `habits.html` / `settings.html` only ŌĆö 3 pages. The bridge was loaded on 3 surfaces and silently absent on every other surface. PM-363 (this same session, shipped 50 minutes earlier) wired `VYVEHaptics.heavy()` at the `achievements.js` `showNext()` chokepoint ŌĆö a global wire that fires from any activity surface when an achievement lands. Bridge-load gap meant the toast haptic was only haptically alive on the same 3 pages. Wire global, bridge local. Silent failure mode everywhere else ŌĆö calls to `window.VYVEHaptics.heavy()` no-op'd because the object simply wasn't defined.

**Discovery.** Audit during PM-364 prep: grepped `haptics.js` references across every root-level HTML in `vyve-site`. Hit-count 3. Reality-check on what PM-363 was supposed to cover: every page that can earn a tier ŌĆö every activity surface. Mismatch.

**Fix.** Single atomic 42-file commit. `<script src="/haptics.js" defer></script>` inserted immediately after the existing `/theme.js` tag on each of 38 candidate pages. theme.js chosen as the anchor because it's present on 38/38 candidates (with `engagement.html` excluded ŌĆö pure redirect stub to `engagement-v2.html`, no UI). Indentation preserved per page. Idempotent: pages that already had the tag (index/habits/settings) skipped. No CSP issues ŌĆö all pages use `script-src 'self'` or no CSP.

**Surfaces gaining the bridge (38):** activity, affirmations, apple-health, breathwork, cardio, cardio-history, certificates, connect, connect-calendar, connect-challenge, connect-checkin, connect-feed, engagement-v2, exercise, journal, leaderboard, log-food, meditation, mind, mind-insights, mind-library, monthly-checkin, movement, movement-history, nutrition, nutrition-setup, personal-bests, podcast, replay-category, replays, running-plan, sessions, settings-account, sleep, visualisation, wellbeing-checkin, workout-history, workouts.

**Workouts set-tick wired in the same commit.** `workouts-session.js` `tickSet()` has two branches: tick (set logged) and untick (correction). Pre-PM-364 both branches were silent.
- Tick ŌåÆ `VYVEHaptics.success()` (matches PM-359 daily-habits log-success precedent)
- Untick ŌåÆ `VYVEHaptics.light()` (gentle, smaller correction)

Fired adjacent to the `.ticked` class flip (lines 332 and 346) so haptic and visual land together. NOT in `saveExerciseLog()` or the `set:logged` bus publish ŌĆö those fire after the network/Dexie round-trip and feel uncoupled from the original tap. State-restoration path in `restoreDom` (line 161) intentionally bypasses ŌĆö programmatic restore, not a user gesture.

**Method (atomic 42-file commit via Git Data API):**
1. Parallel fetch of all 42 candidate HTMLs (10 workers, ~3s)
2. Anchor regex `(?m)^([ \t]*)(<script[^>]*\bsrc\s*=\s*["\']/?theme\.js["\'][^>]*></script>)\s*$` ŌĆö captures indent
3. Per-file insert post-anchor-line, idempotency guard via `if 'haptics.js' in c: skip`
4. 42 blobs ŌåÆ tree ŌåÆ commit ŌåÆ ref via Git Data API (direct PAT, Composio still down per memory #8)

**Files (42, atomic at `ddba1577`):** 38 HTML inserts + `workouts-session.js` patch + `index.html` marker (249) + `settings.html` marker (249) + `sw.js` cache (`pm364-haptics-sweep-a`).

**Verification.** Sample first-100 char re-fetch on 6 representative files (activity, nutrition, engagement-v2, sw, workouts-session, settings) ŌĆö all match. Targeted spot-check on nutrition.html: line 20-21 shows `<script src="/theme.js" defer>` followed by `<script src="/haptics.js" defer>`. workouts-session.js `VYVEHaptics.light` + `.success` confirmed at lines 336 and 358.

**Lesson banked ŌåÆ ┬¦23.73 below.** When wiring a cross-cutting capability behind a bridge, audit bridge-load coverage on every target surface BEFORE claiming any per-surface wire complete. PM-359/360 retros only counted the success-branch wire; the bridge-load gap was invisible because every call site was guarded with `if (window.VYVEHaptics)` ŌĆö the no-op was silent. Codification earned by tonight's three-step retro: wire-PM (silent no-op everywhere except 3 surfaces) ŌåÆ discover during downstream PM ŌåÆ sweep-PM unblocks the original.

## 2026-05-25 PM-363 ŌĆö Haptic at achievement tier reveal: single wire in achievements.js showNext() chokepoint [vyve-site `db9ae1e8`]

**Scope.** First haptic wire from tonight's PM-361 multi-surface pass plan. Targets the achievement-earned toast ŌĆö the moment a tier reveals ŌĆö across the entire platform via the single visible-toast chokepoint in `achievements.js`. Plan was: highest-ROI per line, one wire fires from every metric ladder (32 metrics ├Ś 327 tiers).

**The chokepoint.** Every tier reveal ŌĆö whether from a fresh inline-evaluator hit, a replay of unseen-cache entries on page boot, or a manual `VYVEAchievements.queueEarned([...])` call ŌĆö flows through `queueEarned()` ŌåÆ `showNext()`. Inside `showNext()` the toast is built, appended, and then animated up via a double `requestAnimationFrame` (one frame to flush the offscreen DOM insert, one to start the opacity+transform transition). Haptic wired inside the inner rAF ŌĆö the moment the toast starts its rise. NOT on `appendChild` (one frame too early ŌĆö DOM exists but isn't yet visible). NOT after the 320ms transition completes (uncoupled from the user-perceived arrival).

**Style.** `VYVEHaptics.heavy()` ŌĆö per the bridge's own documented intent in `haptics.js` comments: "strong feedback, e.g. achievement earned". Literal documented use case. Tier reveal is the heavier moment in the haptic palette; per-log success-ticks (food, set, weight, habit) sit at `success()`. Two energy bands cleanly separated by which surface fires them.

**Build.** Single 4-line try/catch insertion inside the inner rAF callback at `showNext()`:
```js
try { if (window.VYVEHaptics) window.VYVEHaptics.heavy(); } catch(e) {}
```
Guard pattern matches the in-place `_publishSetLogged()` style elsewhere ŌĆö defensive null-check on the namespace, swallowed throws. Bridge itself wraps every method in `try/catch` already (per `haptics.js` SAFETY block) so the call never escapes ŌĆö the outer try/catch here is belt-and-braces for the namespace presence.

**Files (4, atomic at `db9ae1e8`):** `achievements.js` (single rAF insertion + narrative comment), `index.html` (vbb-marker 247 ŌåÆ 248), `settings.html` (settings-vbb-marker 247 ŌåÆ 248), `sw.js` (cache `pm362-weekly-checkin-recap-pillars-a` ŌåÆ `pm363-haptic-tier-reveal-a`).

**Verification.** `node --check` clean on achievements.js + sw.js. First-100-char re-fetch all 4 files at SHA `db9ae1e8` ŌĆö clean. `VYVEHaptics.heavy` confirmed at line 157 of achievements.js post-commit.

**Latent bug discovered downstream (fixed PM-364).** `achievements.js` is loaded across all 38 activity surfaces (confirmed via code search post-ship), but `haptics.js` was only loaded on index/habits/settings. So this wire fired correctly on those 3 surfaces and silently no-op'd everywhere else. The bridge-load gap was the actual scope of the failure mode; PM-364 swept the bridge platform-wide and retroactively unblocked PM-363's intent. ┬¦23.73 codified to prevent recurrence.

**Process note.** ┬¦23.70 PM-claim-at-commit-time fired ŌĆö parallel session shipped TWO PM-362 commits (`6037f3cc` weekly-checkin-recap + `22885751` connect-checkin-flip) between session-start max-PM check (361) and commit time. Our claim re-computed to PM-363. Without ┬¦23.70 we would have collided.

## 2026-05-25 PM-362.b ŌĆö Weekly check-in recap rebuilt: canonical 4 pillars (Habits/Body/Mind/Connect) + rolling 7-day window + kind-aware drilldown + Lucide pillar icons [vyve-site `6037f3cc`]

**Why this label is .b.** Parallel session shipped a different PM-362 (Connect check-in optimistic-flip, `22885751`) between my ┬¦23.70 fresh-HEAD recheck (at which point max-PM was 361) and my actual blob-tree-commit-ref sequence. Both sessions independently incremented to 362. The parallel session's brain entry shipped first; mine prepends here as PM-362.b per the ┬¦23.70 brain-narrative-collision precedent (PM-294 case). Code-level: both bumped Update 247 ŌåÆ 248 independently and both arrived at the same number; both bumped sw.js cache key (last-writer-wins, mine is live as `pm362-weekly-checkin-recap-pillars-a` ŌĆö parallel session's `pm362-connect-checkin-optimistic-flip-a` was overwritten, which is benign for cache-bust semantics). Files touched were disjoint (mine: wellbeing-checkin.html + sw.js + index.html + settings.html; theirs: connect-checkin.html + sw.js + index.html + settings.html), so the base-tree-merge model gave a clean superset with no content collision.

**Scope.** Dean's call to start with the weekly check-in surface ahead of monthly + certificates. Live audit surfaced the recap card on `wellbeing-checkin.html` was wired to the legacy 4-row Habits/Workouts/Cardio/Sessions model from before the canonical Mind/Body/Connect framing landed ŌĆö emoji icons, ISO-week Mon-Sun window with a Monday cliff Dean had been seeing on device. Rebuild around the post-PM-307/PM-334 canonical 4-pillar surface shape (Habits / Body / Mind / Connect), with the deliberate divergence that this surface is uncapped (raw counts not ╬Ż min(2, dayCount)) because it's a backwards-looking factual recap, not a gamified score. Plus rolling 7-day window (not Mon-Sun ISO-week) so Wednesday-cadence members don't see a Monday cliff. Submission slot stays ISO-week-locked via the `wellbeing_checkins` UNIQUE constraint on `(member_email, iso_week, iso_year)`; recap window decoupled by design.

**Build.** 4-file atomic via Git Data API. `wellbeing-checkin.html` (main rewrite, 84KB after edits ŌĆö `fetchMemberData` Dexie+REST branches both rewritten, 10 tables read in parallel, sessions de-duped by `(activity_date, category)` across the four session/replay view tables per PM-294/PM-304 overlap shape, kind-grouped cardio + movement + mind for drilldown, `renderActiveFlow` reshaped to canonical 4-pillar grid + Lucide icons + per-pillar accent colour from canonical palette via `[data-pillar=X]` CSS selectors); `sw.js` cache key bump; `index.html` + `settings.html` vbb-marker 247 ŌåÆ 248. EF compat: new shape (habitsDays/bodyWorkouts/cardioByKind/etc) maps back to legacy field names (habitsThisWeek/workoutsThisWeek/etc) in the EF POST payload ŌĆö zero EF change.

**Icon choices.** Habits ŌåÆ Lucide `check` (Dean's pick). Body ŌåÆ Lucide `person-standing` ŌĆö separate Body-nav iconography decision Dean made tonight, will be parallel-session-shipped as a nav.js change shortly; the recap pre-mirrors that target identity. Mind ŌåÆ Lucide `brain`. Connect ŌåÆ Lucide `users`. All stroke-width 2 (engagement-v2 canon). Per-pillar accent colours from `index.html` `.pill[data-pillar=X]` palette: `#4DAAAA` habits / `#E09B3D` body / `#9B7AE0` mind / `#E06060` connect.

**Drilldown rules.** Cardio + Movement: when a single kind present, surface it specifically ("4 running"); when multiple kinds present, fall back to generic ("4 cardio"). Mind always shows per-kind because the kinds ARE the activities ("2 breathwork ┬Ę 1 journal"). Habits: distinct days as big number, "N habits total" as detail (Dean specified "habits total" not "ticks total"). Sessions watched band: de-duped category tags across the four view tables (`session_views Ōł¬ session_live_views Ōł¬ replay_views Ōł¬ replay_video_views`). Empty drilldowns suppressed (no "0 of X" framing).

**Copy.** Greeting "Hi {firstName} ŌĆö here's where you're at" (replaces "You've been putting the work in"). Sub "Your last 7 days, in your own activity." (sets the rolling-7 frame explicitly). Active-flow title "Here's where you're at, {firstName}" (replaces "Here's your week, {firstName}" ŌĆö old copy implied Mon-Sun which the recap no longer respects).

**Process notes.** ┬¦23.41 fresh-HEAD fired three times ŌĆö once at session-start (HEAD `7795deb5` PM-357 era; my draft was PM-358), once mid-build (HEAD had moved to `d4a6dcae` PM-359 ŌåÆ my draft re-claimed PM-360), once at commit (HEAD had moved to `8a164f17` PM-361 ŌåÆ my draft re-claimed PM-362). Then ┬¦23.70 PM-claim-at-commit fired a fourth time during the actual commit-write sequence ŌĆö the parallel session's PM-362 landed in the gap between my last HEAD check and the blob-tree-commit-ref API calls. The race-condition gap ┬¦23.70 was designed to catch is still possible when two sessions are committing within seconds of each other; the .b suffix is the documented escape hatch. 6 inline scripts node --check clean. First-100-char post-commit verify clean on all 4 files at SHA `6037f3cc`.

**┬¦23 candidate banked.** "The weekly check-in recap is a deliberate uncapped surface ŌĆö rolling 7-day frame + raw row count is by design; capped pillar maths on engagement-v2 / home pills / certificates is the score-relevant surface, not the recap. Future Claude that finds the recap maths disagrees with engagement-v2's `╬Ż min(2, dayCount)` should not 'fix' the recap ŌĆö it's intentional." Second occurrence of a deliberate canonical-divergence call on this surface family (first was the Check-ins pillar where engagement-v2 counts only `wellbeing_checkins` weeks but Dean's stated model also counts `monthly_checkins`). One more occurrence promotes to a hard ┬¦23 rule. The rule shape would be: "deliberate canonical-divergence is allowed on this surface but MUST be commented inline at the divergence site AND logged in the changelog under the ┬¦23 candidate banner."

**Out of scope this ship.** Slider copy mirror to onboarding wording (Lewis blocker, ship structure first); whether weekly check-in should become 8-slider mini-monthly (Lewis scope call); achievements tier copy review (P0 backlog, blocks on Lewis); monthly check-in trigger gap (database-only ship, next on Dean's list after this).

Existing 3 monthly_checkins rows (Dean/Lewis/Kelly, all April 2026) retroactively creditable via one-shot UPDATE.

## 2026-05-25 PM-362 ŌĆö Connect check-in optimistic-flip: UI navigates back immediately after Dexie + bus, not gated on fetch [vyve-site `22885751`]

**Scope.** Dean reported: opened VYVE on his iPhone, went to Connect, typed today's prompt response, tapped "Post check-in" ŌĆö nothing happened. Page sat there with the button disabled, no feedback, no navigation. Diagnosis from reading `connect-checkin.html` `postCheckin()`: the ┬¦23.39 optimistic-first contract was broken on this surface. The Dexie write + bus publish were correct, but `history.back()` (the UI flip) was bolted onto `fetch().then(res.json()).then(...)`, so any wedge in `jwtHeaders()` (Supabase `getSession()` pending) or a stalled fetch left the page visually dead despite the data being live everywhere else. PM-289 (the prior touch on this code path) had tried to fix a different problem ŌĆö the success-path navigate beating the network commit ŌĆö by gating navigation on the json parse. That introduced this new failure mode: if the fetch never resolves, navigation never fires.

**The fix.** Move `history.back()` to run immediately after the bus publish, before any awaited network work. The page disappears the moment Dexie + bus succeed. The POST fires un-awaited in the background with `keepalive: true` (kept from PM-289 ŌĆö still the right call for iOS WKWebView teardown). All failure paths now operate on a page that's already gone: 4xx deletes the Dexie row + publishes `connect:checkin:failed` (hub subscriber can react); 5xx + network throw enqueue to outbox. Success path (2xx) reconciles the canonical server row into Dexie silently ŌĆö no navigate, page is already on `/connect.html` and its bus subscriber will pick up the canonical-id update via the next Dexie read.

**Why this is the canonical ┬¦23.39 shape.** Reference impl `focus-shell.js` `complete()` ŌĆö write Dexie ŌåÆ publish bus ŌåÆ return synchronously after step 3 ŌåÆ background POST. `journal.html` `saveEntry()` same shape. `connect-checkin.html` was the outlier; PM-289's well-intentioned defensive coding turned the surface synchronous-in-UI-but-async-in-data into synchronous-everywhere. The page sitting dead with the button disabled is the user-visible symptom of awaiting work that the contract says should not be awaited.

**Files (4, atomic at `22885751`):**
- `connect-checkin.html` ŌĆö postCheckin() refactored. Dexie write ŌåÆ bus publish ŌåÆ history.back() + fallback /connect.html safety net ŌåÆ un-awaited fetch in background. Failure paths unchanged in shape, just no longer driving navigation. PM-289's keepalive: true preserved. ~70 lines structurally rearranged; net -465 bytes.
- `sw.js` ŌĆö cache `pm361-journal-premium-prompt-a` ŌåÆ `pm362-connect-checkin-optimistic-flip-a`.
- `index.html` ŌĆö vbb-marker Update 247 ŌåÆ 248.
- `settings.html` ŌĆö settings-vbb-marker Update 247 ŌåÆ 248.

**Verification.** node --check clean on both inline JS blocks (81 chars + 26031 chars). Post-commit first-100-char re-fetch on all four files pinned to commit SHA `228857514bb96a0650c8c0337ad114bbb3f8ded2` ŌĆö all match. Live HEAD re-checked immediately before commit per ┬¦23.70 (was `8a164f17` PM-361, PM-362 free). Live device test pending Dean's next force-quit + reopen.

**No new ┬¦23 hard rule earned.** This is ┬¦23.39 enforced after a prior session drifted off the contract. The lesson ŌĆö "navigation belongs in the optimistic path, not the fetch chain" ŌĆö is already implicit in ┬¦23.39's "flip UI / clear inputs / repaint; THEN un-awaited background POST" sequence. The audit signal ┬¦23.39 specifies (a write inside a page without a `VYVEBus.publish` line above it) didn't catch PM-289's regression because the bus publish WAS still there ŌĆö just with extra work bolted on after it. Worth a sharpening pass on ┬¦23.39's audit signal at some point: "and the page must navigate / flip view immediately after publish, before any awaited fetch." Not earning a new rule today; noting for next time the doctrine gets touched.

## 2026-05-25 PM-361 ŌĆö Journal premium pass: editorial prompt card + self-contained past-entry prompts [vyve-site `8a164f17`]

**Scope.** Dean opened the journal on his iPhone and flagged it as "feeling less premium" than the rest of the app. Three surfaces, one underlying problem: the prompt copy was visible (title bold, body muted) on the compose card itself, but stripped to title-only on the active-prompt strap and on every past-entry card in calendar view. Reading back "Prompt: Your steady ground" ŌåÆ "hello" tells you nothing about why you reflected on whatever you reflected on. The question that produced the entry was gone the moment you saved.

Mockup-before-code per default. Built `/mnt/user-data/outputs/journal-mockup.html` with before/after columns for all three surfaces (compose prompt card / active-prompt strap / past-entry card). Dean: "looks good. action this". Shipped without further dial-twisting.

**Three surgical CSS + HTML + JS edits to `journal.html`, plus the canonical sw + vbb bumps.**

**1. `.jr-prompt` compose card.** Replaced the icon-block + bold-title + muted-body + two-equal-buttons layout with editorial premium pass: gold eyebrow (`Today's prompt` with a glowing dot), italic Playfair Display title at 1.32rem, muted body, single gold primary CTA with `ŌåÆ` arrow + soft gold shadow, secondary `Free write instead` as a muted text-button beside it (not a competing border-button). Added a soft gold radial bloom in top-right corner (`::before` pseudo) for a hint of editorial decoration without being noise. Background gradient deepened (`rgba(27,120,120,0.24) ŌåÆ rgba(13,43,43,0.4)`), border bumped to `--line-accent-strong`. Removed the empty `.jr-prompt-ico` thumb entirely ŌĆö was a decorative box that didn't carry information.

**2. `.entry-card-prompt` becomes a block.** Single source of truth for prompt display on both past-entry cards AND the active-prompt strap (which already used this class via `<div id="active-prompt-strap" class="entry-card-prompt hidden">`). Old: one-line `font-style:italic` strap with `Prompt: <title>` textContent. New: container with subtle gradient + 2px gold/teal left rail, contains `entry-card-prompt-eyebrow` (gold caps), `entry-card-prompt-title` (italic Playfair), `entry-card-prompt-body` (muted small text). Both surfaces now render the question, not just its label.

**3. `applyPromptToTextarea()` + `renderEntryView()` rewires.** Strap function switched from `textContent = 'Prompt: ' + title` to `innerHTML` with the three-line block. Past-entry render switched from `<div class="entry-card-prompt">Prompt: ...</div>` one-liner to the same block structure. `clearActivePrompt()` updated to clear `innerHTML` not `textContent`. `escHtml(prompt.body)` added at both write sites to defuse any future XSS risk if prompt copy ever picks up user-influenced data (currently inline-static via mind-journal-prompts.js, but the safety is free).

Result: opening a past entry from the calendar surfaces the full question ŌĆö eyebrow `Prompt`, italic title `Your steady ground`, body `What feels solid in your life right now? Lean on that for a minute.` ŌĆö above the entry text itself. Re-reading is now self-contained. The compose strap keeps the question in peripheral vision while typing, instead of the title evaporating into a label-only chip.

**Ship details.** vyve-site `8a164f17`. 4-file atomic commit via Git Data API (Composio still down ŌĆö continuing 21 May outage; PAT-direct curl via Supabase Vault `GITHUB_PAT_CLAUDE` per memory #8). Files: `journal.html` (56088ŌåÆ58615 bytes, delta +2527 for the CSS block expansion + HTML restructure + JS block-render rewires), `sw.js`, `index.html`, `settings.html`. sw cache `pm360-haptics-home-habit-a` ŌåÆ `pm361-journal-premium-prompt-a`. vbb markers 246 ŌåÆ 247 on both index.html and settings.html.

┬¦23.41 first-100 verify at commit SHA matched on all 4 files. Tag balance preserved on journal.html (49/49 divs, 12/12 buttons). `node --check` clean on both inline scripts (766-line main block + 0-line trailing).

**┬¦23.66 + ┬¦23.70 fired correctly ŌĆö and earned their keep.** First draft was claimed as PM-359, then I checked the recent commit list (┬¦23.69) and saw `d4a6dcae` had just shipped as PM-359 (haptics wiring pass 1). Recomputed to PM-360 per ┬¦23.70 and finished the edits. At ┬¦23.41 pre-commit re-fetch, HEAD had advanced again to `11229513` ŌĆö `PM-360 ŌĆö Home-page habit tick haptic` had shipped in the gap between mockup approval and my commit (~10 min window). Three of my four files were conflicted (PM-360 had bumped sw.js cache key and both vbb markers to 246). Re-fetched the three files against new HEAD, re-bumped to `pm361-...` / Update 247, journal.html clean (untouched on main). Atomic 4-file commit landed at PM-361 cleanly. **Two parallel sessions, two ┬¦23.70 collisions caught and resolved without rework on the actual feature code.** The discipline works.

**No new ┬¦23 rule earned** ŌĆö ┬¦23.66 / ┬¦23.69 / ┬¦23.70 / ┬¦23.41 all fired and resolved cleanly. The pattern of "collision check during build ŌåÆ re-fetch immediately before commit ŌåÆ re-bump everything that drifted" is now well-established muscle memory.

**Follow-ups not blocking.**

Bodies are mostly fine (the new editorial treatment actually flatters them) but Lewis should still pass through them with the new surface in mind ŌĆö some titles are now competing with the eyebrow's `Today's prompt` and could read more like questions than labels.

Worth checking on device whether its treatment matches the new journal language ŌĆö possibly worth a follow-up alignment pass so the same prompt looks the same wherever it appears (compose card / strap / past-entry card / reflection focus card = four surfaces, three different treatments before PM-361, two after).

## 2026-05-25 PM-360 ŌĆö Home-page habit tick haptic [vyve-site `11229513`]

**Scope.** Dean tested PM-359 on device ŌĆö habits.html log felt good ("brilliant ... feels really good actually"), V-logo long-press now triggers properly on iPhone, settings toggles + theme buttons tick correctly. One miss: tapping habits **from the home page** didn't haptic. PM-359 wired the renderer in `habits.html` but missed that `index.html` has its own home-page habit renderer at the same time (line ~1576 `// Renders up to 5 habits as tap-to-tick rings`) ŌĆö two independent code paths to the same user-visible feature.

**Fix.** `index.html` line ~2270: `VYVEHaptics.success()` fires immediately after the optimistic upsert + before `optimisticPatch` sign:+1, matching the pattern from PM-359 habits.html exactly. Undo branch at line ~2313 (sign:-1) intentionally NOT wired ŌĆö you don't celebrate correcting a mistake; the visual unticking is feedback enough.

**Lesson.** "Wire the success branch in habits.html" sounded complete because that's the page the feature is named after. But this is the second time (after PM-319 `mind:*` bus events) where a feature looks page-local but has a sibling renderer elsewhere. **Rule of thumb earned:** before claiming a haptic wiring complete, grep for the underlying data write ŌĆö here, `VYVEHomeState.optimisticPatch('daily_habits'` returns *two* hits (habits.html + index.html), one per renderer. Both need the haptic. Same logic likely applies to workouts.html / index.html workout summary, nutrition steppers on home vs nutrition page, etc ŌĆö audit each before claiming a surface done.

## 2026-05-25 PM-359 ŌĆö Haptics wiring pass 1: habits log + V-logo long-press + settings toggles/theme [vyve-site `d4a6dcae`]

**Scope.** First adoption pass of the `VYVEHaptics` bridge (shipped PM-278). Three highest-value, lowest-risk surfaces wired in one atomic commit:

- **`habits.html`** ŌĆö `<script src="/haptics.js" defer></script>` added next to achievements.js. `VYVEHaptics.success()` fires inside the manual-log `if (window.VYVEBus)` block, immediately before `optimisticPatch` ŌĆö success branch only, never on tap. Auto-tick site (line ~1122) intentionally skipped: auto-tick fires silently on page load when activity-derived completions are detected, so a haptic there would buzz the device unprompted (wrong UX). Manual log is the most-fired button in the app per backlog priority #1.
- **`index.html`** ŌĆö PM-259 long-press V-logo reset migrated from `navigator.vibrate(35)` ŌåÆ `VYVEHaptics.medium()`. The old vibrate call was a silent no-op on iOS Safari and Capacitor iOS (Safari doesn't implement vibrate, Capacitor iOS doesn't either ŌĆö only Android `navigator.vibrate` does anything). Now the bridge routes it through `@capacitor/haptics` `impact({style:'MEDIUM'})` natively on iOS Capacitor ŌĆö first time this gesture actually feels like it worked on iPhone.
- **`settings.html`** ŌĆö `<script src="/haptics.js" defer></script>` added next to healthbridge.js. `VYVEHaptics.selection()` fires inside `handleToggle()` (both notif toggles) and inside the `window.vyveSetTheme` override (theme button taps). `.selection()` is the right tap-tick feel here ŌĆö not `.success()`, which is reserved for state-changing wins like a habit log.

**Why selection vs success vs medium.** The backlog spec calls out which API to use per surface, but the underlying mental model: `.success()` = "you accomplished something" (habit logged, achievement unlocked); `.selection()` = "you picked something" (toggle, segmented control, theme); `.medium()`/`.heavy()` = punctuation on a non-success interaction (long-press confirmation, swipe-to-delete threshold). Got this right first time ŌĆö easy mistake to use `.success()` for settings toggles because the toast says "enabled", but the user didn't *succeed* at anything, they just flipped a switch.

**Mechanics.** All call sites `try {} catch (_) {}` wrapped + guarded with `if (window.VYVEHaptics)` ŌĆö never throws, no-ops cleanly if the script hasn't loaded. The bridge's internal platform probe handles native iOS / Android web / silent fallback; nothing page-specific needed.

**Not in scope (backlog).** Other targets from line 581 of backlog.md:
- `workouts.html` exercise set logged ŌåÆ `.light()` ŌĆö when next touching workouts-notes-prs.js
- `engagement.html` achievement-earned first-reveal ŌåÆ `.success()`/`.heavy()`
- `nutrition.html` hydration stepper + weight increments ŌåÆ `.selection()`
- Swipe-to-delete confirm (workouts custom list, exercise_logs) ŌåÆ `.medium()` at threshold
- `breathwork.html` phase transitions ŌåÆ `.light()` per existing PM-173-followup spec
- Pull-to-refresh threshold (PF-26, when built) ŌåÆ `.light()`

These remain opportunistic ŌĆö drop in when next touching each file properly, not a campaign.

**┬¦23.41 + ┬¦23.70 + ┬¦23.58 + portal checklist all observed.** sw.js cache key `pm358-builder-header-safearea-lucide-dumbbell-a` ŌåÆ `pm359-haptics-pass1-a`. vbb-marker 244 ŌåÆ 245 in both `index.html` (`<span id="vbb-marker">`) AND `settings.html` (`<span id="settings-vbb-marker">`). Fresh HEAD re-fetched immediately before commit (still PM-358, PM-359 claim held). Brain re-fetched immediately before brain commit. Inline JS syntax validated via `node --check` for all 3+9+4 = 16 inline script blocks across the three HTML files. Atomic 4-file commit via Git Data API (blobs ŌåÆ tree ŌåÆ commit ŌåÆ ref update); byte-exact blob sizes verified at the commit SHA (94834 / 128009 / 129268 / 18651).

**Testing on device.** Dean must test through the Capacitor TestFlight binary (iOS 1.2/1.3) ŌĆö mobile Safari intentionally no-ops. Sanity: open devtools (or remote debug WebKit), call `window.VYVEHaptics._platform()` ŌåÆ should return `'native'` on the iOS Capacitor build. iPhone must have silent switch OFF and Settings ŌåÆ Sounds & Haptics ŌåÆ System Haptics ON for haptics to fire (Apple-controlled, never code around).

**Files changed.**

| File | Bytes | Change |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `habits.html` | 94834 | `<script src="/haptics.js" defer>` + `VYVEHaptics.success()` on manual log success branch |
| `index.html` | 128009 | `navigator.vibrate(35)` ŌåÆ `VYVEHaptics.medium()` on PM-259 long-press; vbb-marker 244 ŌåÆ 245 |
| `settings.html` | 129268 | `<script src="/haptics.js" defer>` + `.selection()` in handleToggle + .selection() in vyveSetTheme override; settings-vbb-marker 244 ŌåÆ 245 |
| `sw.js` | 18651 | Cache key `pm358-builder-header-safearea-lucide-dumbbell-a` ŌåÆ `pm359-haptics-pass1-a` |

## 2026-05-25 PM-358 ŌĆö Workout builder header safe-area + Lucide dumbbell empty-state [vyve-site 245a8af]

**Scope.** Dean confirmed PM-357 worked ("that worked", screenshot showing "No custom workouts yet" empty state). Same screenshots surfaced two follow-ups: (1) Cancel button on the New Workout builder overlaps with the iOS status bar/clock ŌĆö header `padding:0 20px` + fixed `height:60px` with no safe-area-inset; (2) the empty-state dumbbell glyph is the old hand-rolled SVG (rounded rectangles), not the Lucide canonical dumbbell used everywhere else (PM-336/338, exercise.html `ico-dumbbell`).

Header now drops below notch/Dynamic Island, content area expands accordingly. Cancel button + "New Workout" title + right spacer reflow naturally into the visible band.

Worth a one-shot grep sweep next session for any remaining `position:fixed` + bare-px positioning in modal/overlay surfaces.

**Fix 2: empty-state dumbbell glyph (workouts-programme.js:480).** Old SVG was a stack of `<rect>` elements approximating a dumbbell shape ŌĆö rounded plates + bar + handle. Identical to `<symbol id="ico-dumbbell">` defined in exercise.html (PM-343). Inlined directly rather than referenced via `<use href="#ico-dumbbell"/>` because workouts.html doesn't have the SVG sprite ŌĆö only exercise.html (Body hub) hosts the symbol set. Could be promoted to a shared sprite file in future, not worth it for one icon today.

Size bumped 24├Ś24 ŌåÆ 28├Ś28 to better fill the 56├Ś56 `.empty-state-icon` container. Stroke + fill conventions match Lucide canonical (`stroke="currentColor"` + `stroke-width="2"` + line-cap round + no fill) so it picks up the existing `color:rgba(77,170,170,0.4)` from `.empty-state-icon` CSS automatically.

## 2026-05-25 PM-358 ŌĆö Tier copy review CSV exported [VYVEBrain brain/staging/]

Lewis-reviewable CSV exported from `achievement_tiers` so he can mark up the inaccurate titles and bodies ŌĆö many of the PM-322 placeholders and a fair chunk of the supposedly-approved rows have problems (e.g. "Fifty Habit Days" when threshold is actually 50 logs not 50 days; "the default is shifting in your favour" ŌĆö poetic but doesn't tell the member what they did).

**File:** `brain/staging/tier-copy-review-pm354.csv` (528 rows, 102KB)

**Columns:** `pillar, sort_order, slug, display_name, unit, wired_status, tier_index, threshold, current_title, current_body, copy_status, proposed_title, proposed_body, approved_y_n`

**Filter:** Excluded 10 `hidden=true` achievements (anniversary, came_back, comeback_PB, first_light, full_house, honest_checkin_tier5, owl, quiet_wins, reciprocity, streak_saver) ŌĆö they're easter eggs/surprises and shouldn't appear in tier copy review. Kept everything else: 528 rows from 97 distinct metrics. md5 of body verified server-side as `7b581625f0d517e06ac7313e53a1fcd7`.

**Sort order:** pillar (focus ŌåÆ habits ŌåÆ body ŌåÆ mind ŌåÆ connect ŌåÆ checkins ŌåÆ cross_cutting) ŌåÆ sort_order ŌåÆ slug ŌåÆ tier_index. Cross-cutting metrics (DB `pillar IS NULL`) bucketed at end as "cross_cutting".

**Workflow:** Lewis returns the CSV with `approved_y_n=Y` and his `proposed_title`/`proposed_body` filled in ŌåÆ Claude runs bulk UPDATE on `(metric_slug, tier_index)` setting `title`, `body`, `copy_status='approved'`.

**Side-task surfaced ŌĆö catalog `wired` flag drift:** Filtering by `m.wired=true` would have wrongly dropped ~232 rows. `achievement_metrics.wired` has drifted from reality:
- PM-335 wired the 11 Habits metrics client-side but never `UPDATE`d the catalog flag
- PM-342 wired ~50 Body/Mind/Connect/Check-ins metrics client-side, same omission
- PM-350's 8 Focus metrics were the only ones that got the explicit `UPDATE achievement_metrics SET wired=true`

CSV includes `wired_status` column (literal "wired" or "NOT WIRED" string from catalog) so Lewis can prioritise the actually-firing tiers. Backlog task: one-time sweep `UPDATE achievement_metrics SET wired=true WHERE slug IN (...)` for the Habits/Body/Mind/Connect/Check-ins metrics that have shipping evaluators, to bring the catalog flag back in line with reality. Any future filter that trusts `wired` is misleading until this is done.

**Build path:** Composio still down (continuing 21 May outage). Used PAT-direct curl via Supabase Vault `GITHUB_PAT_CLAUDE` per memory #8. CSV built server-side via Postgres `string_agg` with proper RFC-4180 quoting (`"`-escaped via `REPLACE`). Encoded base64 server-side, retrieved in 3 chunks (~45K each) via a tmp table `_tier_csv_export_tmp`, decoded client-side, md5-verified. Tmp table dropped post-extract.
