## 2026-05-25 PM-357 ŌĆö Custom workout delete purges Dexie mirror [vyve-site 7795deb]

**Scope.** Dean: "in my workouts it won't let me delete my custom workout." Tapped delete, confirmed the dialog, and the workout reappeared (because it never actually disappeared from the rendered list).

**Root cause.** `deleteCustomWorkout` in workouts-builder.js was Supabase-only:

```js
async function deleteCustomWorkout(id, name) {
  if (!confirm(...)) return;
  await fetch(`${SUPA_URL}/rest/v1/custom_workouts?id=eq.${id}`, { method: 'DELETE', ... });
  await loadCustomWorkouts();
}
```

The DELETE hits Supabase and succeeds. Then `loadCustomWorkouts()` runs ŌĆö and that function (workouts-programme.js:430-475) is local-first:

```js
const rows = await VYVELocalDB.custom_workouts.allFor(memberEmail);
if (rows && rows.length) {
  customWorkouts = rows;
  renderCustomWorkouts();
  return;  // ŌåÉ short-circuits before any Supabase pull
}
```

Dexie still has the row (never purged). Function returns early with stale data. List re-paints with the still-present row. To Dean it looks like delete did nothing.

This is the canonical Dexie-first read + server-only write anti-pattern. Every write path needs to purge the local mirror in the same operation, otherwise the next read serves stale.

**Fix.** Rewrite `deleteCustomWorkout` (workouts-builder.js:138-178):
1. Confirm dialog (unchanged)
2. DELETE on Supabase with `Prefer: return=minimal` for slightly cheaper response
3. **Check `res.ok`** ŌĆö if the DELETE failed (RLS block, JWT expired, network), surface an alert and stop. Previous code swallowed errors silently.
4. **Purge Dexie**: `VYVELocalDB.custom_workouts.delete(id)` ŌĆö uses the makeTable `.delete(key)` factory (db.js:485-493), single-arg primary-key path since `id` is the PK per schema string `'id, member_email, workout_name'`.
5. **Flush VYVEData cache** for the legacy fall-through path: `VYVEData.invalidate('custom_workouts:${memberEmail}')`. Defensive `typeof` check ŌĆö VYVEData doesn't currently expose `invalidate` (only `fetchCached` + `writeQueued`), so this is a no-op today but future-proofs.
6. **Optimistic in-memory drop**: `customWorkouts = customWorkouts.filter(w => w.id !== id)` + immediate `renderCustomWorkouts()` so the row vanishes from the UI before any async pull settles.
7. **Background re-pull**: `await loadCustomWorkouts()` reconciles state with server truth (now Dexie is clean, so this paints correctly).
8. Catch block around the whole thing surfaces network errors with an alert.

**Why optimistic + background re-pull, not just optimistic.** The optimistic local drop is for the visual "felt instant" outcome. The `loadCustomWorkouts()` re-pull is the safety net ŌĆö if anything else mutated `custom_workouts` between Dean's click and now (e.g. a shared workout invite landed via Realtime), we reconcile to the actual server state. Cheap insurance.

**Latent risk verified clean post-ship.** sync.js:337 uses `replaceForMember(e, rows)` for `custom_workouts`, not upsert-only. Per db.js:500-548, replaceForMember bulkPuts incoming rows then deletes locals whose PK isn't in the incoming set. Any background hydrate after this delete fetches server state (row absent), calls replaceForMember with the shorter list, the diff step removes the row again if it somehow lingered ŌĆö converges to correct state under any race condition. No re-insert hazard; PM-357 fix is complete.

**Pattern banking ŌĆö ┬¦23 candidate.** Every Dexie-first read path needs a matching local-purge in the corresponding write path. This is the second occurrence (first was PM-153 optimistic INSERT FAT rows for denormalised stores, ┬¦23.7.9). Promote to hard rule on next occurrence:
- ┬¦23.7.9 (existing): optimistic INSERT rows must be FAT for denormalised stores
- ┬¦23.7.10 (candidate): server-only DELETE/UPDATE must purge corresponding Dexie row(s) before next read

**Ship details.** vyve-site `7795deb5`. Single-file behavioural rewrite (`workouts-builder.js` `deleteCustomWorkout` function), plus the ┬¦23.41 portal triplet. sw cache `pm356-video-hide-modal-a` ŌåÆ `pm357-custom-workout-delete-purges-dexie-a`. vbb 242 ŌåÆ 243 on both marker files. Atomic 4-file commit via Git Data API. ┬¦23.41 first-100 verify on all 4 files at commit SHA matched. No parallel collision.

## 2026-05-25 PM-356 ŌĆö Fullscreen video hides picker view (z-index bump didn't beat iOS compositor) [vyve-site 6ed2a4f]

**Scope.** PM-355's z-index 2000 ŌåÆ 20000 didn't work. Dean confirmed running build 242 (sw.js cache `pm355-...` on device, verified via Settings vbb-marker reading "Update 241") and the video was STILL rendering behind the picker. Z-index alone is insufficient under iOS WKWebView when the modal above is an opaque fullscreen layer the compositor has promoted to its own GPU layer.

**Diagnosis.** Walked the z-index chain: nothing else on the page is at z-index Ōēź 20000. Overlay was appended to `document.body` (sibling of `#exercise-search-view`, which is inside `#app` but `#app` has no stacking-context-creating CSS). On paper, body-level z-index 20000 should sit above `#exercise-search-view` at z-index 10000. In Safari desktop and Chrome it does. In iOS WKWebView, it doesn't ŌĆö the compositor appears to render `#exercise-search-view` as a single composited tile that captures the video element's render in a stacking context that's resolved at the compositor layer, not the DOM stacking-context level.

This is a known WKWebView quirk that surfaces specifically with opaque-background modals + child video elements. The official workaround in iOS web dev circles is "don't fight the compositor ŌĆö remove the conflicting element from the visual tree entirely."

**Fix.** `display: none` the picker view (and history view, defensively) while the fullscreen video is open. Store previous `display` values in a `hiddenViews` array. Restore on close. Two views in scope today, both at z-index 10000: `#exercise-search-view` and `#history-view`.

```js
const hiddenViews = [];
['exercise-search-view','history-view'].forEach(id => {
  const el = document.getElementById(id);
  if (el && getComputedStyle(el).display !== 'none') {
    hiddenViews.push({ el, prev: el.style.display });
    el.style.display = 'none';
  }
});
// ... open video ...
closeBtn.onclick = () => {
  vid.pause();
  document.body.removeChild(overlay);
  hiddenViews.forEach(h => { h.el.style.display = h.prev; });
};
```

UX-wise this is actually better, not worse ŌĆö the user is watching a demo fullscreen; nothing behind it matters. When they close the video they're back exactly where they were in the picker, scroll position preserved (DOM untouched, just hidden).

**Why z-index won in non-iOS testing.** WKWebView's compositor is more aggressive about layer promotion than Chrome/Firefox. The video element creating its own native iOS playback layer + the modal being a composited tile = z-index resolution happens at the GPU compositor not the CSS engine. `display:none` removes the element from layout entirely, so the compositor can't promote it.

**Four-strike retro for the PM-346 feature.** PM-346 (wiring) ŌåÆ PM-351 (close button safe-area) ŌåÆ PM-352 (binding mechanism) ŌåÆ PM-355 (z-index) ŌåÆ PM-356 (compositor workaround). FIVE ships for one feature. Each one revealed the next layer underneath. The original `openVideoFullscreen` helper was written in May 2026 for a single caller in a single context; bolting on a second caller from inside a modal exposed five different latent assumptions in sequence. Each fix was correct given what was known at the time, but the cumulative cost is high.

**┬¦23 candidate ŌĆö promoting.** This is now a strong enough pattern to codify: when extending a pre-existing helper to a new caller context, all of its baked-in assumptions need an audit. For visual helpers specifically: z-index hierarchy, stacking context creation, compositor layer behaviour, opaque vs transparent backgrounds, focus management, scroll context, keyboard shortcut ownership. Banking as ┬¦23.73 candidate; will write up properly if a similar five-ship cascade ever happens again, or if I find I'm citing the principle in code review notes.

**Ship details.** vyve-site `6ed2a4fd`. Single-function rewrite in `workouts-programme.js` (`openVideoFullscreen`). sw cache `pm355-video-overlay-zindex-a` ŌåÆ `pm356-video-hide-modal-a`. vbb 241 ŌåÆ 242 on both marker files. Atomic 4-file commit via Git Data API. ┬¦23.41 first-100 verify on all 4 files at commit SHA matched. No parallel collision this turn ŌĆö clean ship.

## 2026-05-25 PM ŌĆö PM-335 through PM-353 ŌĆö Dexie-first achievements: complete rollout across all 6 pillars [vyve-site: f27b198f + earlier ┬Ę supabase: achievement-claim EF v1]

**Big architectural campaign closed in one extended session.** PM-335 shipped the Dexie-first evaluator with Habits as proof-of-shape; PM-339ŌåÆ347 hardened it through real device testing; PM-342 wired Body + Mind + Connect + Check-ins pillars; PM-347 fixed toast deep-linking; PM-350 added Focus pillar via the `focus_slug`-on-source-tables read pattern; PM-353 retired the `fuel` focus when Food Log was deferred. Net result: 5 of 6 pillars fully live with instant on-threshold-cross toasts; Focus also live but with proxy logic for its calendar-coupled metrics.

**The architectural redirect (┬¦11A.v3 supersedes earlier PM-322/328 plan).** Server-side achievement evaluation (`log-activity evaluate_only` path + `_shared/achievements.ts` inline eval) was demonstrably the wrong shape ŌĆö round-trip cost on the threshold-crossing tap is 200-400ms best case, 2-3s on cellular. Achievements *must* feel instant; that means the evaluator runs client-side, reads from Dexie (already canonical at bus-event time per VYVE Bus's optimistic-publish semantics), fires toast on local detection, then writes the earn to Supabase in the background via a new JWT-required `achievement-claim` EF. Idempotent on the `(member_email, metric_slug, tier_index)` unique constraint so the v1 server path + v2 client path coexist safely until v1 is retired.

**Toast latency on device, confirmed.** Dean tested through habits.html / cardio.html / mind pages. Threshold-cross toasts fire within ~1 second of the tap. Backfilled accumulated activity (e.g. the 500km cardio) catches up on next page load via the evaluator's `evaluateAll()` boot pass ŌĆö multiple toasts queue cleanly into the existing `achievements.js` toast UI.

**What shipped ŌĆö vyve-site commits, in order:**

- **PM-335** `18d62139` ŌĆö Initial Dexie-first evaluator + Habits handlers + Path B tier-framed UI in engagement-v2.html. Replaced the empty "Achievements are being overhauled" placeholder with 6-pillar accordion (Habits live, others stub).
- **PM-339** `80494be3` ŌĆö Anon key fix (catalog fetch was 401-ing silently); `VYVE_AUTH_READY` promise replaces event-listener-only path (was hanging when auth fired before script loaded); state.debug surface for device diagnostics.
- **PM-340** `4606e370` ŌĆö `r.is_yes` ŌåÆ `r.habit_completed` everywhere (the actual Dexie column name on `daily_habits`; `is_yes` only exists on the bus event envelope). 22 reads fixed.
- **PM-341** `3bbb916e` ŌĆö Settings ŌåÆ "Reset achievements (debug)" button + `window.VYVEAchEval.devResetEarns()`. Needed because sync.js is additive-pull for `member_achievements`, doesn't reconcile server-side deletes ŌĆö and Capacitor app has no Web Inspector to wipe Dexie manually.
- **PM-342** `cbbeb62f` ŌĆö Body / Mind / Connect / Check-ins handlers all wired in one commit (19 files). Mind handlers fire silently behind `phil_approved=false` UI filter. Mind included `volume_lifted_total` with ┬¦11A sanity caps (repsŌēż100, weightŌēż500). Evaluator script added to 14 publishing pages so toasts fire on the same page as the action.
- **PM-347** `d9f53c56` ŌĆö Toast tap routing fixed. Was routing to `/engagement.html#achievements...` but engagement.html is a redirect stub (PM-305) whose meta-refresh dropped the hash. Toast now routes direct to `/engagement-v2.html?score=v2&tab=achievements&slug=X&tier=Y`. engagement-v2 boot parses params, switches tab, opens correct pillar, opens ladder modal on the exact metric. engagement.html meta-refresh line dropped so JS-based redirect preserves query/hash as defensive fallback.
- **PM-350** `1ee4c49c` ŌĆö Focus pillar live via Option A: read `focus_slug` from 5 source tables (cardio, connect_checkins, mind_activities, movement_activities, nutrition_logs) and derive completion + pillar from `home-focus-catalogue.js write_target.table` mapping. 8 Focus metric handlers (`focus_cards_completed`, `daily_focus_all_complete` simplified to Ōēź3 distinct focuses/day, `same_focus_completed_10x`, `focus_from_every_pillar_week`, `distinct_focuses_tried`, `streak_focus`, `weekly_focus_completion` simplified to 7-day-week proxy, `first_focus_complete`). No new table, no new bus event ŌĆö already-existing tagged data backfills automatically.
- **PM-353** `f27b198f` ŌĆö `fuel` focus retired (Food Log deferred Coming Soon). nutrition_logs dropped from source tables; FOCUS_TO_PILLAR + UI mirror + bus subs all aligned. `home-focus-catalogue.js`: fuel COPY removed, Wednesday morning slot reassigned to `hydration`.

**Supabase changes:**
- New EF `achievement-claim` v1 (JWT-required, idempotent insert, ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, fans out to `achievement-earned-push` only when newly_inserted=true). Live, ACTIVE.
- 13 Mind metrics flipped `phil_approved=true` via single MCP UPDATE (Dean's call: "you called the gate, you can release it"). UI requires fresh catalog fetch via Reset achievements debug button or 24hr cache expiry.
- 8 Focus metrics flipped `wired=true` via single MCP UPDATE in PM-350.

**Hard lessons earned this session ŌĆö banked but not codified as ┬¦23 rules yet:**
1. **Hardcoded anon keys are foot-guns.** PM-335 had a stale anon key copy-pasted into the evaluator. Silently 401-ed on catalog fetch. Lesson: use `window.vyveSupabase` (already auth'd) for PostgREST reads, or read the constant from auth.js ŌĆö never duplicate the key value.
2. **Bus envelope ŌēĀ Dexie row column names.** `habit:logged` event publishes `is_yes`; Dexie `daily_habits` table column is `habit_completed`. PM-340 retro. Lesson: when reading from Dexie in an evaluator, always confirm the column name against `db.js` SCHEMA_V<N>, never assume it matches the bus envelope.
3. **Capacitor app diagnostics ŌĆö no Web Inspector access.** Any debug tooling needs to live as in-app UI surfaces (Settings buttons, debug rows, etc), not console-only. PM-341 codified this via the Reset achievements button.
4. **sync.js for `member_achievements` is additive-pull only.** Server-side deletes don't propagate to Dexie. Need either a "forget local" client-side helper, or extend sync.js to do delete-reconciliation for specific tables. PM-341 took the helper route; sync upgrade is the cleaner long-term fix and goes on the backlog.
5. **Toast routing must go to live page, not redirect stub.** PM-347. Old `engagement.html#anchor` style dropped query params through meta-refresh.

**┬¦23.66/┬¦23.69/┬¦23.70 fired *seven times* this session.** Parallel sessions shipped PM-333, 334, 336, 337, 338, 344, 345, 346, 348, 349, 351, 352 during the build. Each rebase: 5-min loop. Cost Ōēł40 min of session budget across the whole campaign. Discipline held ŌĆö zero overwrites, vbb-marker monotonic 219ŌåÆ239, sw cache key monotonic `pm332-yt-ready` ŌåÆ `pm352-fuel-focus-retired`.

**Composio outage continued** ŌĆö used PAT-direct via Supabase Vault throughout (memories #8/#9). 30+ atomic commits via Git Data API blobsŌåÆtreeŌåÆcommitŌåÆref pattern. Zero corruption, every commit md5-verified post-commit.

The Achievements v3 (Dexie-first) campaign opened in PM-335's brain close is now substantially closed: Habits Ō£ō Body Ō£ō Mind Ō£ō Connect Ō£ō Check-ins Ō£ō Focus Ō£ō all wired. What's left:

## 2026-05-25 PM-352 ŌĆö Picker thumb tap-to-preview made robust (PM-346 ŌåÆ PM-352 follow-up) [vyve-site 3ae13c7]

**Scope.** Dean's report after PM-346 + PM-351 shipped: "I still can't watch the videos when swapping an exercise." His screenshot from the swap modal (16:44) showed play icons on EVERY thumbnail including Box Jump ŌĆö which has `video_url = NULL` and SHOULD have been dimmed to `opacity: 0.55` per the PM-346 dim-no-video cue. The absence of dimming on Box Jump was the diagnostic tell: **PM-346's code path either wasn't running, or `ex.video_url` was always falsy in the picker's `allExercises` array** so every row took the no-video branch (which paints the play icon via CSS pseudo-element regardless).

**Diagnostic walk.** Traced the data flow:
- Live `main` branch: PM-346 code IS deployed (verified via raw fetch).
- `allExercises` populated by `loadAllExercises` (workouts-programme.js:240). Two paths:
  - Path A: localStorage cache `vyve_exercise_library_v2` ŌĆö if cached payload pre-dates v2 select-with-video_url, would be missing. But cache key was bumped to v2 simultaneously with the select change, so this should be safe.
  - Path B: Dexie via `VYVELocalDB.workout_plans.allFor()`. Sync uses `select=*` (sync.js:424), so all columns including `video_url` flow through.
- In-session card thumbnails (which Dean confirmed working) use `loadSessionVideos` (workouts-programme.js:317-333) ŌĆö `thumb.onclick = () => openVideoFullscreen(videoUrl)` via direct JS closure, NOT inline string onclick.
- Picker thumbnails (PM-346) used `onclick="event.stopPropagation(); openVideoFullscreen('${url}')"` inline string ŌĆö different mechanism, different fragility profile.

**Two hypotheses, both addressed by the same fix:**
1. **Lazy-loaded `<img>` covers the parent div's tap region.** The `<img class="es-lazy-thumb">` has `width:100%; height:100%; object-fit:cover` and lives inside `.es-ex-thumb`. iOS WKWebView has documented intermittent issues where bubbled clicks from image children don't fire the parent's inline `onclick=` attribute (works in dev, fails in production WKWebView builds). Behaviour is most visible on lazy-loaded images where the bubble path becomes async-ish.
2. **`ex.video_url` mismatch via en-dash vs hyphen.** The catalogue uses en-dash (ŌĆō, U+2013) in names like "Bench Press ŌĆō Dumbbell" while some upstream paths might compare against hyphens. `getVideoUrl()` (workouts-programme.js:306) has a `normaliseExName` step that handles all dash variants + parens + whitespace. The PM-346 path bypassed this and read `ex.video_url` directly.

**Fix shape (single-function rewrite of `renderExerciseList` in `workouts-exercise-menu.js`):**
1. Replace `ex.video_url` direct read with `getVideoUrl(ex.exercise_name)` ŌĆö the same normalised lookup the in-session cards use. If `getVideoUrl` is unavailable (defensive), fall back to `ex.video_url || null`.
2. Drop inline `onclick=` strings entirely. Store exercise name in `data-ex-name` and video URL in `data-video-url`, then attach `addEventListener('click', ...)` post-render in a `querySelectorAll` loop. Pattern mirrors the working in-session-card path.
3. Add `pointer-events: none` to the inner `<img>` and `<svg>` so taps always fire on the thumb div itself, never on the child. Eliminates the iOS WKWebView bubble-through ambiguity entirely.
4. HTML-encode the exercise name (`"` ŌåÆ `&quot;`) for safe round-trip through the dataset attribute. The previous quote-escape (`'` ŌåÆ `\'`) was correct for the inline-onclick path but wrong for the dataset path ŌĆö fixed.

**Hit rate after fix.** Even if hypothesis 1 was wrong on this device, hypothesis 2's normalised lookup catches every name-mismatch row that PM-346 missed. Net coverage strictly increases.

**Misread retro.** PM-346 shipped a working *implementation* of tap-to-preview but used a fragile *binding pattern* (inline onclick strings) that doesn't match the rest of the codebase's wiring style. The pre-existing `loadSessionVideos` had been quietly working with `addEventListener` for months ŌĆö the right call would have been to copy that pattern verbatim from the start, not invent a new inline-string variant. Bank for future: when a similar mechanism already works elsewhere in the codebase, ship the copy of that mechanism, not a fresh design.

**Ship details.** vyve-site `3ae13c71`. Single source-file change (`workouts-exercise-menu.js`) plus the ┬¦23.41 portal triplet. sw cache `pm351-fullscreen-video-x-safe-area-a` ŌåÆ `pm352-picker-thumb-click-robust-a`. vbb 238 ŌåÆ 239 on both marker files. Atomic 4-file commit via Git Data API (memory #11). ┬¦23.41 first-100 verify on all 4 files at commit SHA matched.

**┬¦23 candidate ŌĆö bank.** "When a similar mechanism already works elsewhere in the codebase, copy that mechanism verbatim rather than designing a fresh variant." Strong signal but tied to one occurrence pattern ŌĆö won't promote to a hard rule yet, watching for a second instance to confirm it's a recurring class. Adjacent to ┬¦23.71 (stop patching when not moving the needle) and ┬¦23.66 (rebase to live before commit) but distinct: this is about pattern reuse at design time.

## 2026-05-25 PM-351 ŌĆö Fullscreen video close ├Ś clears iOS safe-area inset [vyve-site 9bef8bc]

**Scope.** Dean's report after testing PM-346: "the X is too high in the corner that I can't actually click it to cancel off the video." The close button on the fullscreen exercise demo player (used by both in-session card thumbnails since the original feature shipped, and now the picker thumbnails since PM-346) sat at `top: 16px` ŌĆö i.e. 16px from the top of the viewport. On every iPhone with a notch / Dynamic Island (every model from XS forward), the iOS status bar + safe-area inset takes ~47-50px before usable content area begins. The 40x40 button at top:16px was rendering *behind* the status bar, untappable.

**Fix.** Replace fixed `top: 16px` / `right: 16px` with `top: calc(env(safe-area-inset-top, 0px) + 12px)` and `right: calc(env(safe-area-inset-right, 0px) + 16px)`. `env(safe-area-inset-*)` resolves to the iOS-reported inset on devices that have one (notch, rounded corner, Dynamic Island, landscape inset), and to `0px` on devices that don't. Fallback `0px` parameter to `env()` is safe browser-side. Result: button always sits 12px below the status bar with 16px right margin past any rounded-corner inset, regardless of device or orientation.

**While I was there.** Bumped hit target from 40x40 ŌåÆ 48x48 (Apple's minimum thumb-reach guideline is 44pt; we were below it), the ├Ś glyph from 1.5rem ŌåÆ 1.75rem for clarity, and tightened the background from `rgba(0,0,0,0.6)` ŌåÆ `rgba(0,0,0,0.7)` for slightly stronger contrast against light-coloured video frames. Added flex centring + `padding:0` + `line-height:1` so the ├Ś is mathematically centred in the circle (was off-centre before due to default button padding).

**Misread on my part this turn.** Dean's earlier message used "library" and I went hunting for a standalone Exercise Library surface. There isn't one ŌĆö `exercise.html` is the Body hub landing page (Movement / Workouts / Cardio streams), and the workouts.html "Browse Library" tab is a *programme* catalogue not an exercise catalogue. The picker shown in his screenshot IS where the PM-346 tap-to-preview wiring lives, and his screenshots confirm it's working: clear play overlays on every thumb. Followed up to verify rather than ship a redundant patch ŌĆö correct call.

**Ship details.** vyve-site `9bef8bcd`. Single-file behavioural change (`workouts-programme.js`) plus the obligatory sw.js cache key + vbb markers (┬¦23.41 portal-commit triplet). sw cache `pm350-ach-focus-pillar-live-a` ŌåÆ `pm351-fullscreen-video-x-safe-area-a`. vbb 237 ŌåÆ 238 on both index.html and settings.html. ┬¦23.66/┬¦23.70 fired once ŌĆö initial claim PM-349 collided with parallel session shipping PM-349 (nav.js bottom tab order Mind-before-Body), then PM-350 (Focus pillar live) landed during my fresh-HEAD check; clean sed-sweep to PM-351 + marker rebase 236 ŌåÆ 238 + cache key swap. ┬¦23.41 first-100 verify on all 4 files at commit SHA matched.

**┬¦23 candidate ŌĆö modal close buttons must use safe-area-inset.** This is the second time we've shipped a fixed-pixel `top:` value on a fullscreen modal element only to discover it's hidden under iOS chrome (first time was the session view sv-close earlier in the build cycle, now this). Promoting to a ┬¦23 hard rule: **any fixed-position modal control within the top 48px or bottom 48px of viewport must use `env(safe-area-inset-*)` insets, not bare px values**. Will codify in next master.md touch.

**exercise.html (Body hub, 4 section tiles).** ico-walk ŌåÆ Lucide footprints (matches movement.html walk row from PM-336 ŌĆö cohesion across the Body cluster). ico-dumbbell ŌåÆ Lucide canonical dumbbell (kettlebell-grip geometry, reads strength clearly). ico-run ŌåÆ Lucide heart-pulse (universal cardio glyph; gym treadmills + fitness trackers use the same shape). ico-leaf ŌåÆ Lucide apple (literal food; resolves the leaf=yoga conflict from PM-336 which would otherwise have leaf doing double duty on the Body hub). Custom cardio_type strings fall through to other. mvlog-icon CSS expanded with per-kind tints: running #E06060 (red), cycling #4DAAAA (teal), swimming #5EA6DB (blue), rowing #F5B056 (amber), other #B28DD9 (purple). Row container carries dual class `mvlog-icon mvlog-icon-<kind>` matching movement.html PM-336 pattern. Title gets text-transform:capitalize (parity with cardio-history.html).

Now feeds `r.cardio_type` through correctly. Pre-existing bug introduced when cardio-history was first built; surfaced only because adding the per-kind map made the wrong-key result visible.

**Decisions locked.** heart-pulse used twice (Cardio tile + running row) intentionally ŌĆö Lucide has no good running icon, heart-pulse + red colour tint + "Running" label is the cleanest landing. swimming ŌåÆ droplet (Lucide has no waves icon despite docs implying so). rowing+other ŌåÆ both activity (no good Lucide rowing-specific icon; differentiation comes from label + colour tint). apple over leaf for Nutrition resolves the existing leaf=yoga conflict from PM-336.

**Tooling lift in this commit.** Map duplicated inline across cardio.html and cardio-history.html; flagged for future lift to shared module if a third surface needs it.

## 2026-05-25 PM-342 ŌĆö Dexie-first achievements: Body + Mind + Connect + Check-ins pillars wired [vyve-site cbbeb62f]

**(Retro brain entry ŌĆö commit shipped by parallel session earlier today, no brain entry at the time. Reconstructed from commit message + commit diff.)**

**Scope.** Single ship extends PM-335's Habits-only proof to all four remaining wirable pillars. Collapses what PM-335's backlog had broken into four pillar sessions (PM-336/337/338/339) into one atomic 19-file commit. Focus stays blocked (no `focus_completions` table ŌĆö separate campaign). Mind UI gated on `phil_approved` (handlers wired + earns write silently; UI hides until Phil signs single SQL flip).

**Body pillar (19 metrics).** Driven by `workout:logged` / `set:logged` / `cardio:logged` / `movement:logged` / `workout:shared`. Metrics: workouts_logged, workout_minutes_total, streak_workouts, workout_before_7am, workout_days_in_a_row, programme_week_complete, first_workout, programme_complete_8wk, cardio_logged, cardio_minutes_total, streak_cardio, first_cardio, distance_total (cardio + movement union), movement_sessions_logged, streak_movement, reps_lifted_total + sets_completed_total + volume_lifted_total (all sanity-capped reps<=100 weight<=500), first_custom_workout, workouts_shared_v2. `muscle_groups_week` left as v1 no-op (needs muscle taxonomy join). HK `lifetime_steps_v2` + `lifetime_active_energy_v2` stay server-sweep.

**Mind pillar (13 metrics, ALL phil_approved=false).** Driven by `mind:logged`. Metrics: mind_sessions_logged, breathwork_sessions, journal_entries, meditation_sessions, mind_minutes_total (watch_seconds/60), distinct_mind_types, streak_mind, meditation_streak, mind_before_8am, visualisation_logged, first_mind_session, long_meditation (seconds-mapped tier 1/2/3 at 600/1200/2700s), affirmations_saved. Handlers run + write `member_achievements` rows when thresholds cross BUT engagement-v2 UI filters them via `phil_approved=false`. When Phil signs: `UPDATE achievement_metrics SET phil_approved=true WHERE pillar='mind'` makes all 13 rows visible with already-earned backdated state ŌĆö no code change needed.

**Connect pillar (15 metrics).** Driven by `session:viewed` / `replay:viewed` / `connect:reaction:logged` / `workout:shared`. Metrics: sessions_watched (session_views + session_live_views union), replays_watched (replay_views + replay_video_views union), streak_sessions, session_minutes_yoga/mindfulness/therapy/education (regex-matched category), reactions_given (count of own-authored checkin_reactions), distinct_session_categories, first_session_watched, first_replay_watched, workouts_shared_v2 (custom_workouts row count). v1 no-op stubs: reactions_received + checkins_with_reactions (local Dexie only has the current member's given reactions ŌĆö "received" needs server data or Realtime channel to other members' reactions on own check-ins). chat_messages_posted no-op (no Dexie store yet). charity_tips_v2 + personal_charity_contribution_v2 stay server-sweep.

**Check-ins pillar (9 metrics).** Driven by `wellbeing:logged` / `monthly_checkin:submitted`. Wirable: checkins_completed, monthly_checkins_completed, first_checkin, streak_checkin_weeks (ISO-week walk), checkin_text_responses (monthly note_* columns), weekly_score_climb + monthly_avg_improved (both phil-gated, consecutive composite/avg score increases). v1 no-op: daily_mood_checkins + daily_mood_streak (no Dexie store + no bus event for those yet).

**UI rewrite (engagement-v2.html).** HABITS_DISPLAY/ICONS preserved; added BODY/MIND/CONNECT/CHECKINS display + icon maps + per-pillar computePillarValues functions. `renderHabitsPillar` + `renderStubPillar` replaced with single generic `renderPillar(slug)` using PILLAR_META lookup to dispatch to the right compute fn. Tallies count across ALL non-hidden metrics so even phil-gated metrics contribute to the count once earned. `openLadder` uses lookupIcon/lookupDisplayName helpers (was HABITS_*-only). repaint() now Promise.all-renders all 6 pillars. Focus retains the "Tracking goes live next" stub (no compute fn registered for it). Bus subscriptions expanded: all 13 achievement-relevant bus events repaint the achievements tab on emit (debounced 150ms).

**Script tag fan-out.** 14 publishing pages get `/achievements-evaluator.js` script include so threshold-cross toasts fire instantly from the page that emits the bus event (no cross-page round-trip needed): movement.html, cardio.html, workouts.html (after existing achievements.js), affirmations.html, breathwork.html, journal.html, meditation.html, sleep.html, visualisation.html (before `</head>`), connect-checkin.html, connect-feed.html, connect.html (before `</head>`), wellbeing-checkin.html, monthly-checkin.html (after existing achievements.js).

**Mind silent-write justification.** The 13 Mind handlers write earns to `member_achievements` when thresholds cross, but engagement-v2 hides those rows via `phil_approved=false`. Members can earn invisibly; UI reveal is a one-line UPDATE SQL when Phil signs (no code change needed). Earn rows are Dean-only test territory pre-trial anyway.

**Server-side coexistence.** `_shared/achievements.ts` + `log-activity evaluate_only` path still live in parallel. The unique constraint on `member_achievements` makes double-claim safe (server insert ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; client claim EF too). Full server-side retirement deferred to a future PM once all 5 remaining pillars verified client-side over a few days of trial.

The collapse of PM-336/337/338/339 into one ship is worth banking as a class-pattern lesson ("when four planned ships all follow an identical template, consider collapsing ŌĆö saves four separate rebase cycles") but not yet recurrent enough for a formal rule.

## 2026-05-25 PM-341 ŌĆö Settings "Reset achievements" debug button + devResetEarns helper [vyve-site 3bbb916e]

**(Retro brain entry ŌĆö commit shipped by parallel session earlier today, no brain entry at the time. Reconstructed from commit message + commit diff.)**

Sequence: (1) delete every member_achievements row for current member from Dexie, (2) empty in-memory earnedSet in the evaluator, (3) `window.VYVESync.hydrateTable('member_achievements')` re-pull from Supabase, (4) rebuild earnedSet from freshly-synced Dexie, (5) UI repaints next bus event tick. Member-facing copy stays generic ("debug" subtitle indicates dev-only). Button can be removed after trial-phase pipe verification is done.

## 2026-05-25 PM-348 ŌĆö Reorder Exercises drag shows live destination slot [vyve-site 3c14226]

**Scope.** Dean's feedback on the Reorder Exercises modal: "it's a drag and drop and it feels a little bit offŌĆ” all we need to fix it so that as you're dragging it, it fits in so you can know where you're dragging that exercise to." Considered the alternative he suggested (replace drag with up/down arrow buttons) but landed on fixing the drag rather than removing it ŌĆö drag is the iOS-native pattern members already expect (Reminders, Lists, Settings all use it). The actual defect was missing visual feedback, not the gesture itself.

**Root cause.** The old `pointerdown ŌåÆ pointermove ŌåÆ pointerup` shape (workouts-exercise-menu.js, pre-PM-348 lines 101-148):
- Created a static dashed placeholder at the dragged row's *original* position and never moved it.
- Used `transform: translateY(deltaY)` on the dragged row, which floats it over other rows but leaves the other rows unmoved ŌĆö no visual indication of "where will this land".
- Only computed the target slot on `pointerup` (closest-midpoint search), so until release, the member is guessing.
- Result: the row appears to float in space disconnected from the list; you find out where it landed only after dropping it, often needing a second drag to correct.

**New shape.** Three changes, all in `pointerdown`/`rOnMove`/`rOnUp`:
1. **Lifted floating tile.** `pointerdown` switches the source row to `position: fixed` at its current viewport coordinates with `scale(1.02)` + `box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.35)` + `opacity: 0.96` + `pointer-events: none`. Reads clearly as "in motion" while the list below remains stable.
2. **Travelling placeholder.** A dashed teal "destination slot" `<div>` is inserted where the source row was. `rOnMove` recomputes target index every pointer-move by finding which other row's midpoint the pointer is now above (pointer-y < otherRow midpoint = insert before). When the target changes, the placeholder is moved with `insertBefore`/`appendChild` to the new slot. Other rows naturally reflow CSS-side. The placeholder is the visual contract: "release now and the row lands here."
3. **Final commit.** `rOnUp` no longer re-runs closest-midpoint search ŌĆö it trusts `rTargetIdx` (the placeholder's current position) and splices the source row into `reorderExercises[rTargetIdx]`. Index conversion verified for top/middle/bottom/end-of-list cases (otherRows excludes source ŌåÆ splicing source out makes otherRows order match reorderExercises order, so rTargetIdx directly = insertion index).

**Index conversion verification.** `[A, B, C, D]`, drag B (idx 1), otherRows = `[A, C, D]`:
- Drop before A: rTargetIdx=0 ŌåÆ splice B out ŌåÆ `[A,C,D]` ŌåÆ insert at 0 ŌåÆ `[B,A,C,D]` Ō£ō
- Drop before C: rTargetIdx=1 ŌåÆ `[A,C,D]` ŌåÆ insert at 1 ŌåÆ `[A,B,C,D]` (no-op) Ō£ō
- Drop before D: rTargetIdx=2 ŌåÆ `[A,C,D]` ŌåÆ insert at 2 ŌåÆ `[A,C,B,D]` Ō£ō
- Drop after D: rTargetIdx=3 (otherRows.length) ŌåÆ `[A,C,D]` ŌåÆ insert at 3 ŌåÆ `[A,C,D,B]` Ō£ō

**iOS interrupt safety.** Added `pointercancel` listener routing to `rOnUp` so an incoming call / app backgrounding mid-drag cleans up state and falls back to the source row's original position rather than leaving an orphaned floating tile with `position:fixed`.

**Scroll behaviour notes.** `.reorder-item` already has `touch-action: none` (workouts.html:163), so the row itself doesn't initiate page scroll on touch. The dragged row being `position: fixed` is unaffected by `.reorder-list`'s `overflow-y:auto`. Placeholder positioning uses `getBoundingClientRect` (viewport-relative), correct under any scroll state.

**Limitation noted, not fixed.** No auto-scroll of `.reorder-list` when dragging near top or bottom of visible area. For sessions Ōēź8 exercises where not all rows fit in the modal viewport, the member has to release, scroll, re-grab. Acceptable for current typical 4-6 exercise PPL sessions; iOS-Reminders does have this auto-scroll behaviour and it's worth adding eventually. Backlog candidate.

**Ship details.** vyve-site `3c142262`. sw cache `pm347-ach-toast-deeplink-a` ŌåÆ `pm348-reorder-live-slot-a`. vbb 234 ŌåÆ 235 on both index.html and settings.html. Atomic 4-file commit via Git Data API. ┬¦23.66/┬¦23.70 fired once mid-build ŌĆö initial claim PM-347 collided with parallel session's ach-toast-deeplink ship that landed between my fresh-HEAD check at session start and the actual commit. Clean sed-sweep PM-347 ŌåÆ PM-348 in source + re-fetch of live sw.js/index.html/settings.html (rebased markers from 233 ŌåÆ 235 since parallel had already taken 234), cache key swap. ┬¦23.41 first-100 verify on all 4 files at commit SHA matched.

**No new ┬¦23 rule earned** ŌĆö UI polish on existing infrastructure, nothing procedural to codify. The "fix the gesture, don't replace it" call is worth banking as a general principle but doesn't need a hard rule.

## 2026-05-25 PM-346 ŌĆö Exercise picker thumbnail tap-to-preview [vyve-site 2b1a3db]

**Scope.** Dean's feedback: "they sit behind the thumbnail and if they click it the video loads. With signal it should be close to instant ŌĆö it's a short video." The exercise picker (swap modal + builder multi-select modal) showed static thumbnails that were inert ŌĆö no way to preview the actual movement before selecting a replacement or adding to a custom workout. Demo videos exist on `workout_plans.video_url` for 168/297 rows (~57%) and the in-session card thumbnails already opened them via `openVideoFullscreen()` (workouts-programme.js:335-346), but the picker rows had no wiring.

**Implementation.** Single-file patch in `workouts-exercise-menu.js` `renderExerciseList()` (the function that paints every row in the picker, used by both swap and builder modes). For each row:

- If `ex.video_url` is present: add `onclick="event.stopPropagation(); openVideoFullscreen('${safeVideoUrl}')"` + `cursor:pointer` to the `.es-ex-thumb` div. `stopPropagation` is the key bit ŌĆö the row's outer `onclick="selectExercise(...)"` would otherwise also fire on thumbnail tap, conflating preview with select. In builder mode the row click is a multi-select toggle, same propagation hazard, same fix.
- If no `video_url`: dim the whole thumb to `opacity:0.55` as a visual cue that this exercise has no demo to preview. The play-icon `::after` pseudo-element in `.es-ex-thumb` CSS (workouts.html:183) already paints on every thumb regardless; the opacity cascade dims image + pseudo together so faded thumb = "no preview" reads clearly.

No new CSS, no new modal, no new JS function ŌĆö reuses the existing `openVideoFullscreen()` overlay verbatim. The overlay creates a `<video controls autoplay playsInline>` taking max viewport with a close button at top-right; same behaviour members already see when they tap an in-session card thumbnail. Backend (Supabase Storage URLs on `workout_plans.video_url`) unchanged.

**Why the patch is so small.** All the heavy lifting was already shipped: `workout_plans` schema has both `video_url` and `thumbnail_url`; `loadAllExercises` already SELECTs both into `allExercises`; the Dexie path via `VYVELocalDB.workout_plans.allFor()` returns full rows including `video_url`; `openVideoFullscreen` is global because all three workout JS files load as top-level `<script>` tags in workouts.html. The only missing wire was the picker-row tap ŌåÆ open. Surface area: 12 net lines added (4 of which are the JSDoc comment).

**Data gap surfaced.** 129 of 297 `workout_plans` rows have NULL `video_url` (43%). Members will see dimmed thumbs on those exercises, which is honest but suboptimal UX. Pre-existing data-completion task, not a regression ŌĆö flagging for backlog as "fill video_url across remaining 129 workout_plans rows" (likely scoped per programme: PPL has higher coverage than Home Workouts / Movement & Wellbeing). Out of scope for this ship.

**Builder-mode behaviour.** Verified the picker is used in both `mode === 'swap'` and `mode === 'builder'` (workouts-exercise-menu.js:208 ŌĆö multi-select toggle path). Preview tap with `stopPropagation` correctly bypasses both selection paths. Selection only fires when the row text/area is tapped, not the thumbnail.

**Ship details.** vyve-site `2b1a3db8`. sw cache `pm345-swap-preserves-progress-a` ŌåÆ `pm346-picker-thumb-tap-preview-a`. vbb 232 ŌåÆ 233 on both index.html and settings.html. Atomic 4-file commit via Git Data API (memory #11 ŌĆö Composio still not loading via tool_search this session). ┬¦23.41 first-100 verify on all 4 files at commit SHA matched.

**No new ┬¦23 rule earned** ŌĆö behavioural addition reusing existing infrastructure; nothing procedural to codify.

## 2026-05-25 PM-345 ŌĆö Workout swap preserves in-flight session state [vyve-site d296abf]

**Scope.** Mid-session exercise swap was destroying in-flight progress across the whole workout, not just the swapped slot. Dean's feedback: "if you swap out an exercise, it resets the workout. So it removes all of your, like, sets of reps completed." Verified the report and found the bug was actually broader than reported ŌĆö every unticked kg/reps input across every card was being wiped on swap, not only the swapped exercise's state. Plus the swapped slot's `sessionLog` entry was orphaned, and `completedSetsCount` (the in-session counter) wasn't reconciled when ticks were dropped.

**Root cause.** `workouts-exercise-menu.js` line 223 (pre-patch) rewrote `sessionExercises[swapExerciseIdx]` with a fresh template `{ sets: 3, reps: '8-12', rest_seconds: 90, notes: '' }`, then called `renderSessionBody()`. `renderSessionBody` rebuilds the whole `#session-body` innerHTML from `sessionExercises` ŌĆö every set card is rebuilt from scratch, so any kg/reps input the member had typed but not yet ticked vanished. Ticked sets *appeared* preserved because `sessionLog` (the source of truth for the eventual save) is keyed by `exercise_name` and persists across re-renders ŌĆö but the swapped slot's old name was now orphaned in `sessionLog` and the new name had no entry. Save Session would emit nothing for the swapped slot regardless of how many ticks had been placed before the swap.

**Three independent bugs in one shape, all fixed in one patch:**

1. **Cross-card DOM wipe** ŌĆö every unticked input on every other exercise died on re-render. Fix: snapshot `domState` (kg/reps/ticked/bw/note keyed by exercise_name, same shape as `saveSessionState`'s persister at workouts-session.js:67-103) BEFORE the swap, then replay the snapshot post-render for every slot except the swapped one (matched by `swappedIdx`, captured before nulling `swapExerciseIdx`).
2. **Template clobber** ŌĆö replacement exercise was forced to `sets: 3, reps: '8-12', rest_seconds: 90` regardless of what the original slot's targets were. If the member was on a 5x5 strength block, a swap dropped them to 3x8-12. Fix: carry `sets`, `reps`, `rest_seconds`, `letter_badge` from the original slot; only `exercise_name` and `notes` reset.
3. **Orphan sessionLog + completedSetsCount drift** ŌĆö old-name `sessionLog` entry never saves; `completedSetsCount` (gates rest timer, progress display, completion eligibility) wasn't reduced when ticked sets on the swapped slot were discarded. Fix: `delete sessionLog[original]` and `completedSetsCount = max(0, completedSetsCount - droppedTicks)` where `droppedTicks` = ticked count in the snapshot of the swapped slot.

Final step: call `saveSessionState()` after replay so the localStorage `vyve_active_session` shape matches the post-swap DOM. App backgroundŌåÆforeground (or full page reload within the 4-hour staleness window) will restore the swapped slot with its replacement exercise and every other slot with its preserved inputs.

**Edge cases handled.** Replacement exercise already present elsewhere in the session: name-keyed snapshot lookup works because `swappedIdx` skip is by index not name, so the existing same-name slot keeps its snapshot. Replacement is the *same* as the original (no-op swap): treated as a normal swap, snapshot replay reapplies what was there, net result is a `notes` reset and a Supabase swap-log row (acceptable noise). Member swaps the same slot twice in quick succession: each swap snapshots fresh from DOM, so chains compose correctly.

**Limitation (pre-existing, not introduced).** `saveSessionState` and the new replay loop both key DOM state by `exercise_name`. If a member has the same exercise in two slots and one of them is swapped, the snapshot will overwrite during the forEach and the surviving same-name slot may pick up the wrong inputs on replay. This is the same limitation that exists for backgroundŌåÆforeground restore today; out of scope for this fix, flagged for future tightening (use slot index + exercise_name as composite key).

**Ship details.** vyve-site `d296abf4`. sw cache `pm345-swap-preserves-progress-a`. vbb-marker 232 on both `index.html` and `settings.html`. Single atomic 4-file commit via Git Data API (PAT direct path ŌĆö memory #11). Post-commit verified per ┬¦23.41 ŌĆö all 4 files re-fetched at the commit SHA, expected markers present.

**Verification path for Dean.** Open a workout in the iOS app, mid-session: type kg+reps into a few cards without ticking, tick one set on another card, then long-press an exercise ŌåÆ Swap ŌåÆ pick a replacement. Pre-fix: typed inputs disappear, sessions/reps on the swapped card lose any ticks silently. Post-fix: every typed-but-untickąĄd input survives, ticks on non-swapped cards stay ticked, the swapped card shows the new exercise name with the original's sets/reps targets and blank input rows. Force-quit + reopen the app ŌåÆ swapped state restores intact from localStorage.

**┬¦23 candidate.** Logged as PM-345 in changelog only. No new ┬¦23 rule earned ŌĆö this is a behavioural fix, not a procedural one. The cross-card wipe was a missed test case at the time of the original swap implementation (commit history shows swap was a one-shot drop-in, never instrumented for partial-session integrity).

## 2026-05-25 PM-336 ŌĆö movement.html icon refinement (5 swaps) + movement-history.html row dedupe by client_id [vyve-site e509ac6]

**Scope.** Two surgical fixes on Movement surfaces, shipped together as one atomic commit.

**1. movement.html Recent Movement Lucide icon picks ŌĆö refines PM-329.** PM-329 swapped the hand-rolled stick figure + asterisk pair to Lucide icons but the picks weren't all the right ones. Dean reviewed against the live row size (20px) and proposed 5 specific swaps. Pushback offered on 1 of 5 (yoga); 4 of 5 accepted as-proposed.

- `walk` ŌĆö `footprints` unchanged Ō£ģ
- `other` ŌĆö `activity` unchanged Ō£ģ
- `stretch` ŌĆö `wind` ŌåÆ `stretch-horizontal`. `wind` (flowing horizontal lines) reads as weather, not stretching. `stretch-horizontal` is two stacked rounded bars ŌĆö reads as the verb literal.
- `yoga` ŌĆö `flower-2` ŌåÆ `leaf`. Light call. `flower-2` (lotus-radial petals) is the more semantically-on-the-nose yoga icon, but at 20px it's busy and crowds the row visually. `leaf` is calmer and pairs better visually with `stretch-horizontal` (both have simple silhouettes at that size). Noted in the comment header that the swap was made on visual weight grounds, not semantics.
- `mobility` ŌĆö `move` ŌåÆ `rotate-ccw`. `move` (4-way arrows) reads as the universal drag-window/reposition affordance ŌĆö wrong meaning for mobility. `rotate-ccw` (curved rotational arrow) lands the range-of-motion meaning that mobility actually carries.
- `pilates` ŌĆö `dumbbell` ŌåÆ `infinity`. `dumbbell` was a stretch ŌĆö pilates is the un-equipment-y end of the spectrum (mat-and-springs). `infinity` reads as "controlled, continuous, looping motion" which IS pilates.

All five canonical Lucide SVG paths pulled from upstream `lucide-icons/lucide` main branch ŌĆö not freehand approximations. First draft of stretch-horizontal had wrong rect dimensions from memory (`height="4" y="6"/14`); caught against upstream and corrected to canonical `height="6" y="4"/14` before commit. Pattern worth keeping: when swapping Lucide icons, always pull the raw SVG from the upstream repo's `/icons/<name>.svg` rather than reconstructing.

**2. movement-history.html row doubling ŌĆö dedupe by `client_id`.** Reported as "every entry showing twice". Server SQL audit confirmed clean: 30 rows, 30 distinct `client_id`s, 30 distinct `id`s for the test account. So the bug was render-side, not data-side.

**Root cause analysis.** Local optimistic write to Dexie uses `id = _mvClientId` (movement.html line 876). Server hydrate row arrives with `id = gen_random_uuid()` (Postgres default) and `client_id = <local uuid>`. These are two different `id`s in Dexie for the same logical activity. `replaceForMember`'s stale-sweep IS architecturally correct ŌĆö it diffs `incomingIds` against local primaryKeys and bulkDeletes stale rows. But in narrow windows (slow hydrate, mid-write navigation, cold-load before sweep completes) both rows can coexist locally for several seconds. The opening hypothesis (legacy cardio + workouts fan-in) was wrong ŌĆö PM-306 already retired that fan-in; movement-history reads `movement_activities` only.

**Fix.** Defensive dedup at the render layer in `collectLogs()`. `dedupKey = r.client_id || r.id` ŌĆö `client_id` is the canonical idempotency key per PM-167/PM-294, so deduping by it strips dual local-optimistic + server-hydrated copies of the same logical row. Falls back to `id` for any pre-migration row that might lack `client_id`. Doesn't disturb hydrate semantics ŌĆö pure render guard.

**Bonus fix.** Brittle `setTimeout(1500ms)` re-render replaced with bus-driven repaint. The old timer was a guess at hydrate completion time; on slow networks it could be too short (hydrate still running, re-render sees same data), on warm cache it's already done (re-render is wasted work). Now subscribes to `movement:logged` (own writes + cross-tab via BroadcastChannel) and `vyve-localdb-table-pulled` (sync.js dispatches this exact event after each successful pull, line 587). Repaints exactly when state changes, never on a clock guess. `_repainting` reentrancy guard included.

**Tooling.**
- Composio fallback throughout ŌĆö `tool_search "github commit multiple files"` returned only Google Drive/Chrome tools (Composio outage continuation from earlier session). Pivoted to Vault PAT + direct GitHub REST + Git Data API for atomic multi-file commit per memory #8.
- ┬¦23.69 session-start collision check fired at opening ŌĆö scanned 8 most recent commits, found PM-329/330/331 already shipped (no overlap with planned movement work). Cleared to design-talk.
- ┬¦23.41 fresh-HEAD re-check at commit time caught FOUR commits since session start ŌĆö PM-332/333/334/335 from parallel session (replays YT.ready fix + home pills refinement x2 + achievements evaluator Habits UI). None touched movement.html or movement-history.html. Clean rebase, no surgical re-application needed.
- ┬¦23.70 PM-claim-at-commit-time: drafted source with `PM-XXX` placeholder, sed-swept to `PM-336` immediately after the fresh-HEAD recheck claimed `max(seen)+1 = 336`.
- ┬¦23.41 marker/cache discipline: vbb-marker `222 ŌåÆ 223` in BOTH index.html AND settings.html, sw.js cache-key `pm335-achievements-dexie-first-a ŌåÆ pm336-movement-icons-history-dedupe-a`, all in the same atomic commit.
- ┬¦23.52(c) blob SHAs all 40-char hex from `/git/blobs` POST. ┬¦23.53 post-commit first-100-char re-fetch verified all 5 files at the committed SHA ŌĆö all start with expected file shape (DOCTYPE x4 / "// VYVE Service Worker"). Spot-check: `stretch-horizontal|rotate-ccw|leaf|infinity` all present in committed movement.html. 7 matches for `dedupKey|vyve-localdb-table-pulled|VYVEBus.subscribe.*movement:logged` in committed movement-history.html.
- `node --check` clean on all inline JS blocks in both edited files (2 blocks per file, 0 errors).

**Files (vyve-site `e509ac6`).** movement.html 70582 ŌåÆ 70337 bytes, movement-history.html 12895 ŌåÆ 14682 bytes (+33 lines for bus wiring + dedup logic), index.html vbb-marker 222 ŌåÆ 223, settings.html settings-vbb-marker 222 ŌåÆ 223, sw.js cache key bumped to `pm336-movement-icons-history-dedupe-a`.

**No new ┬¦23 hard rules earned.** All patterns established doctrine ŌĆö ┬¦23.41 fresh-HEAD recheck + marker/cache pair, ┬¦23.45 Composio-fallback PAT-direct, ┬¦23.52(a/b/c) blob hygiene, ┬¦23.53 first-100-char post-commit verification, ┬¦23.69 session-start scan, ┬¦23.70 PM-claim-at-commit-time. Worth flagging: the Lucide-icon-swap pattern (pull canonical SVG from upstream `/icons/<name>.svg` rather than reconstruct from memory) caught one wrong-dimension bug pre-commit. If we do another icon-swap-heavy session this might warrant a ┬¦23 codify; one occurrence isn't enough yet.

## 2026-05-25 PM-317 (movement variant) ŌĆö movement.html cold-load mirrors cardio.html exactly [vyve-site b890dbb]

**NOTE on PM number collision.** Two different commits share the PM-317 label. The brain entry "PM-319 ŌĆö mind.html video watch-time tracking" already documents this collision in its tooling section: `b3f72f7` = PM-317 mind tracker (parallel session, renumbered to PM-319 in brain), `b890dbb` = PM-317 movement cold-load (this entry ŌĆö Dean's main thread). This entry is the deep-doc for `b890dbb`.

**Scope.** movement.html cold-load painted empty on first open even when Dexie had rows (because hydrate was racing the paint). cardio.html had previously been hardened against exactly this race; movement.html still had the older fragile pattern. Refactor copied the cardio.html cold-load pattern verbatim ŌĆö Dexie-first read with non-empty gate, REST fallback when Dexie is empty, hydrate fan-out kicked off as a side-effect, paint triggered both inline and on `vyve-localdb-table-pulled`.

**Net effect.** No more empty-paint flicker on cold-load. Hot-load (warm Dexie) paints inline at <16ms. Cold-load (empty Dexie) paints from REST in ~200-400ms, then re-paints from Dexie on hydrate completion if the REST + hydrate races diverge.

**Files (vyve-site `b890dbb`).** movement.html (+251/-49), plus standard marker + cache bumps.

**No new ┬¦23 hard rules earned.** Pattern was already established by cardio.html; this is just propagation to the matching surface.

## 2026-05-25 PM-335 ŌĆö Dexie-first achievements evaluator + Habits pillar UI shipped [vyve-site 18d62139 + supabase EF achievement-claim v1]

**Architectural redirect.** Dean's mid-session pivot retired the server-side achievement evaluation path (PM-322/328's `log-activity evaluate_only` + `_shared/achievements.ts` inline evaluator) in favour of a Dexie-first, bus-subscribed client evaluator. Reason: server round-trip on the threshold-crossing tap (200-400ms best case, 2-3s on cellular) is fundamentally incompatible with "instant on the tenth habit." Local Dexie read post-bus-event is sub-50ms, deterministic, no network on the hot path.

**The new shape.**

1. **`achievements-evaluator.js`** (new client module, 460 LOC). Subscribes to `habit:logged` (only event wired this session ŌĆö other pillars next). On each event, debounce 100ms, run all 11 Habits handlers, compute current value from Dexie, diff against in-memory `earnedSet`, fire optimistic toast via existing `window.VYVEAchievements.queueEarned()`, POST to new `achievement-claim` EF in background. `earnedSet` populated at boot from Dexie `member_achievements` table (already synced by sync.js). Catalog cached in localStorage 24hr TTL.
2. **`achievement-claim` EF v1** (new, JWT-required, deployed live). Resolves `member_email` from JWT, verifies `(metric_slug, tier_index)` exists in `achievement_tiers`, inserts to `member_achievements` ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, fans out to `achievement-earned-push` via `EdgeRuntime.waitUntil()` if `newly_inserted=true`. Idempotent ŌĆö re-claims are a server no-op with no second push.
3. **`engagement-v2.html` Achievements tab** ŌĆö placeholder retired, replaced with 6-pillar accordion (Habits / Body / Mind / Connect / Check-ins / Focus). Habits pillar fully populated with Path B tier-framed badges (10 gemstone CSS tiers) + Pattern 3 levels-up rows (gold dots under each row showing earned tiers). Tap row ŌåÆ ladder modal with full tier list + status pill (EARNED / NEXT / LOCKED). Other 5 pillars stub "Tracking goes live next."

**11 Habits metric handlers wired** (all driven by `habit:logged`):
`habits_logged`, `streak_habits`, `three_habits_day`, `five_habits_day`, `all_assigned_habits_day`, `habits_before_9am`, `first_habit`, `weekly_habits_total`, `distinct_habit_types`, `same_habit_repeats`, `seven_day_including_weekend`. Read from `daily_habits` + `member_habits` Dexie stores. The 22 already-wired metrics from the v1 server path keep firing through that path until retired in a future PM (no double-claim risk because of the unique constraint).

**┬¦23.66 / ┬¦23.69 / ┬¦23.70 fired hard.** Parallel session was shipping fast ŌĆö between session start and commit moment, vyve-site moved from PM-332 (`739660cb`, my session-start baseline) ŌåÆ PM-333 (`65038984`, Home pills bigger) ŌåÆ PM-334 (`2f0762d1`, pills maths fix). I rebased twice on the three touched files (index.html, settings.html, sw.js ŌĆö same three each time). Final PM claimed at commit moment from a fresh `/commits?sha=main` fetch immediately before `POST /git/commits`: max-PM-seen 334 ŌåÆ PM-335. Net effect: zero overwrites, vbb-marker chain monotonic (220 ŌåÆ 221 ŌåÆ 222), CACHE_NAME chain monotonic (`pm333-pills-bigger` ŌåÆ `pm334-pills-engagement-v2-maths` ŌåÆ `pm335-achievements-dexie-first-a`).

**Composio fallback used throughout** (per memories #8/#9). Composio GitHub tools didn't load via `tool_search`; PAT-direct via Supabase Vault. Atomic 6-file commit via Git Data API (blobs ŌåÆ tree ŌåÆ commit ŌåÆ ref update, parents=[2f0762d1], non-force ref update). Post-commit verification via `GITHUB_GET_REPOSITORY_CONTENT` (md5 match on all 6 files at `18d62139`).

**Files shipped (6, vyve-site `18d62139`):**
- `achievements-evaluator.js` ŌĆö NEW, 29.8 KB
- `engagement-v2.html` ŌĆö Achievements tab rebuilt (~430 LOC added: CSS + Path B badges + 6-pillar accordion + modal + inline JS renderer)
- `habits.html` ŌĆö `<script src="/achievements-evaluator.js" defer>` added next to `achievements.js`

- `settings.html` ŌĆö same script include + vbb 221ŌåÆ222

**Supabase changes:**
- New EF `achievement-claim` v1 ACTIVE, JWT-required, sha `ca829a1ec610bf05`
- No schema changes ŌĆö operates on existing PM-322 schema (achievement_metrics + achievement_tiers + member_achievements)

**Device walk owed.** Dean to confirm: open habits.html on iOS, tap a yes habit, expect toast "Achievement earned ŌĆö First habit" within ~1 second. Tap until tier 1 of `habits_logged` (threshold likely 1 ŌĆö confirm in catalog), expect second toast. Open engagement-v2.html ŌåÆ Achievements tab, expect Habits pillar showing tier 1 badge on those metrics with progress bars against tier 2.

**Stub pillars.** Body / Mind / Connect / Check-ins / Focus all render in the UI as accordion cards but their bodies show "Tracking goes live next." Body has 22 metrics, Habits-pattern repeat per pillar is roughly:

**Not done this session:**

**┬¦23 candidates earned (not codified this commit ŌĆö too campaign-specific yet):**
- "Bus event publish always optimistic-before-server-write means Dexie row is canonical at evaluator time" ŌĆö useful pattern, worth a rule if we hit a counter-example. Banked.
- The "claim EF returns `newly_inserted` boolean and gates push fan-out on it" pattern is a generalisation of the v1 push-on-earn behaviour ŌĆö applies anywhere a client detects an event the server needs to verify-and-persist. Banked.

**PM-329 handover (`brain/staging/handover-pm329.md`) ŌĆö OBSOLETE.** That handover assumed a server-side evaluator extension across all 65 unwired metrics. Architecture is now Dexie-first; handover is wrong shape. File kept for historical context; do not follow.

**Backlog updates (`tasks/backlog.md` ┬¦ Achievements v2 build):**
- PM-329 handover marked obsolete.
- New campaign opened: "Achievements v3 ŌĆö Dexie-first evaluator rollout across pillars" with Habits Ō£ō done, Body / Mind / Connect / Check-ins as discrete next-session asks, Focus deferred behind data-layer campaign.

## 2026-05-25 PM-334 ŌĆö Home pills count match engagement-v2 Progress tab maths exactly [vyve-site 2f0762d1]

**Bug.** Dean reviewed PM-333 on device: pills painted but **certificate stars were firing across Mind/Body/Connect** despite him being well under 30 *days* of activity in those buckets. Root cause: PM-331's `loadPillarCounts` used raw row counts (e.g. `mind_activities.allFor(email).length`) ŌĆö tables that accept multiple rows per day (Mind/Body/Connect at 2/day cap, plus rows-per-tap on Connect via session_views + replays + live_views) blew past 30 fast.

**Fix.** Strict mirror of engagement-v2.html `renderProgressTab` (lines 970-1078). The canonical Progress tab maths:

| Pillar | Cap | Formula |
|---|---|---|
| Habits | 1/day | distinct activity_date days |
| Mind | 2/day | ╬Ż min(2, dayCount) over `mind_activities` |
| Body | 2/day | ╬Ż min(2, dayCount) over `workouts + cardio + movement_activities` merged |
| Connect | 2/day | ╬Ż min(2, dayCount) over `connect_checkins + session_views + replay_video_views + session_live_views` merged |
| Check-ins | 1/week | distinct ISO weeks via `wellbeing_checkins` |

`loadPillarCounts` now reads the same 10 tables engagement-v2 pulls. Day-key extraction identical (`activity_date` primary, falling back to `checkin_date` / `logged_date` / `submitted_at` / `created_at` per table). ISO week key derivation identical (Jan 4 anchor, 7-day floor). The `dayKey()` helper inlined to keep the home implementation self-contained.

**Doctrine.** Home pills and engagement-v2 Progress tab must never disagree. The maths is currently duplicated rather than factored into a shared module because engagement-v2.html ships it inline. Worth flagging for a future refactor: lift the pillar maths into `pillar-math.js` so both surfaces consume the same code. Until then, every engagement-v2 maths change needs a matching home patch (and vice versa).

**Where Dean's stated model diverges from canonical.** Dean's brief: "Habits 1/day, Mind 2/day, Body 2/day, Connect 2/day, Check-ins = 1 weekly wellbeing + 1 live session weekly + 1 monthly per month." Engagement-v2 currently only counts unique weeks of `wellbeing_checkins` for the Check-ins bucket ŌĆö it doesn't add the live session weekly check-in or the monthly_checkins. PM-334 ships the engagement-v2 behaviour exactly because home should never be ahead of the canonical surface. If Dean's stated model is the destination, engagement-v2 changes first (or both together via the proposed shared helper) ŌĆö banked for a future ship.

**Table-pulled handler extended.** Added `session_views`, `replay_video_views`, `session_live_views` to the list of tables that trigger a `loadPillarCounts` refresh ŌĆö needed since the new Connect maths reads from those three.

**Tooling.** ┬¦23.41 fresh-HEAD clean twice (pre-blob, pre-tree, no drift across the build window). ┬¦23.52(a) all three blob bodies via base64-encoded urllib JSON. ┬¦23.52(c) all SHAs 40-char hex asserted. ┬¦23.52(b) post-commit `files[].status` `{modified: 3, added: 0, removed: 0}`. ┬¦23.53 first-100-char re-fetch on all three files at committed SHA matched. 9 inline scripts `node --check` clean. Tag balance 29/29.

**Files.** vyve-site `2f0762d1`. `index.html` 125544 ŌåÆ 129334 bytes (+3790; engagement-v2 mirror logic is longer than the prior row-count version). `sw.js` cache key bump. `settings.html` vbb-marker 220 ŌåÆ 221.

**No new ┬¦23 hard rules earned.** Lesson worth banking (not rule-shaped yet): *"row count is never activity count ŌĆö always match the canonical surface's dedupe/cap maths"*. PM-334 is the second time this same class of bug has hit (the first was PM-159 ŌĆö engagement v1 over-counting workouts before the source-aware cap). Pattern repeats whenever a new surface reads from raw `.length` instead of the per-day-capped formula. Worth a ┬¦23 if it recurs once more.

## 2026-05-25 PM-333 ŌĆö Home pills bigger + Mind before Body + carousel switched to VYVE_SESSIONS [vyve-site 65038984]

**Scope.** Three small follow-ups to PM-331's home dashboard ship after Dean reviewed on device.

**(1) Pills bigger.** Ring `56ŌåÆ64px`, stroke `4ŌåÆ4.5`, ring-val font `0.86ŌåÆ0.95rem`, `/30` small `0.56ŌåÆ0.6rem`. SVG `viewBox 52ŌåÆ60`, circle `r 22ŌåÆ26`, C constant `138.2ŌåÆ163.36`. The numbers (e.g. `7/30`) now read at arm's length on phone. Five-across still fits within the row.

**(2) Mind before Body.** PROGRESS_TRACKS array reordered. Final order: **Habits ŌåÆ Mind ŌåÆ Body ŌåÆ Connect ŌåÆ Check-ins**. Same reorder will land on other surfaces (engagement-v2, achievements page when it ships, hub navigation) as they get touched ŌĆö Dean's spec was explicit about applying everywhere progressively. PM-333 only touches the home pills; downstream surfaces stay as-is until their own ship.

**(3) Carousel switched to `VYVE_SESSIONS`.** This was the bug Dean flagged: home showed nothing in Live Sessions This Week while Connect's "Live This Week" displayed the full carousel. Root cause: two different data sources entirely. PM-331 wired home to `calendar_occurrences` (Dexie-synced from Supabase, async, depends on sync.js pull having fired). Connect uses `window.VYVE_SESSIONS` from `sessions-data.js` (hardcoded recurring catalogue of 8 session types, synchronous, always available). On Dean's device the calendar_occurrences sync hadn't fired (or had stale Dexie state), so the home painted empty while Connect painted full. PM-190.d (21 May) already solved this on Connect by switching from `service_catalogue` Dexie reads to the hardcoded `sessions-data.js` ŌĆö same lesson now applies to home.

**Renderer port.** Verbatim port of `connect.html` `renderLiveThisWeek` (lines 1274-1325) into `index.html` `paintLiveCarousel`. Same chronological sort by `getNextOccurrence`, same "Sunday ┬Ę 06:00" day-name format, same image-with-onerror-fallback to gradient pattern, same `(s.tags && s.tags[0])` for category eyebrow, same `s.liveUrl` for tap target. Cards paint synchronously on first frame ŌĆö no Dexie wait, no skeleton, no race condition. Identical to Connect's "Live This Week" by construction.

**Sessions-data.js loading.** Added `<script src="/sessions-data.js" defer>` to index.html head between sync.js and firstPaintHydrate.js. sw.js precache list already included it from earlier (PM-190.d era), so no precache change needed. The defer attribute means the script loads after document parse ŌĆö `paintLiveCarousel` gets called from boot which is also after parse, so the `window.VYVE_SESSIONS` global is reliably present. Added a `setTimeout(paintLiveCarousel, 100)` retry inside the renderer as belt-and-braces in case boot races with the defer load (defensive only; not observed to happen).

**Dropped code.** `LIVE_CAT_SHORT` constant (8-entry category-name shortener), `fmtLiveWhen` (today/tomorrow/day-name formatter), `DAY_SHORT` short-name array ŌĆö all no longer needed because the new renderer uses Connect's "Sunday ┬Ę 06:00" format directly via `DAY_NAMES`. The `calendar_occurrences` branch in `vyve-localdb-table-pulled` handler removed (no consumer reads from it on home anymore). `calendar_occurrences` Dexie table itself stays ŌĆö `connect-calendar.html` still reads it for the full calendar view, and the table's broadcast-ID linkage matters for any future surface that needs to know whether a session is currently live. Just the home doesn't need it.

**Tooling.** ┬¦23.41 fresh-HEAD fired once mid-build. Started at PM-331 HEAD `af32355a`; lived HEAD moved to PM-332 `739660cb` (parallel session shipped YT.ready + referrerpolicy mirror on live shells ŌĆö touched index.html / sw.js / settings.html again, marker-only). Fresh re-fetch, sed-rebased PM-332 ŌåÆ PM-333, Update 219 ŌåÆ 220, cache key `pm332-yt-ready-referrerpolicy-a` ŌåÆ `pm333-pills-bigger-reorder-carousel-sessionsdata-a`. ┬¦23.70 PM claim-at-commit-time resolved my draft PM-332 ŌåÆ committed PM-333. Clean rebase ŌĆö parallel session's diff was 3 marker lines only.

┬¦23.52(a) all three blob bodies via base64-encoded urllib JSON. ┬¦23.52(c) all blob SHAs 40-char hex. ┬¦23.41 fresh-HEAD checked twice (pre-tree, no drift). ┬¦23.52(b) post-commit `files[].status` `{modified: 3, added: 0, removed: 0}`. ┬¦23.53 first-100-char re-fetch on all three files at committed SHA matched.

9 inline scripts `node --check` clean. Tag balance 29/29 (was 28; +1 for sessions-data.js script tag in head).

**Files.** vyve-site `65038984`. `index.html` 126177 ŌåÆ 125544 bytes (-633, net smaller because the new sessions-data renderer is more compact than the LIVE_CAT_SHORT + fmtLiveWhen + calendar_occurrences Dexie-read it replaces). `sw.js` cache key bump. `settings.html` vbb-marker 219 ŌåÆ 220.

**No new ┬¦23 hard rules earned.** All patterns are established doctrine. **Lesson worth noting** (not ┬¦23-rule-shaped yet): when a surface reliably works on one page (Connect) and unreliably on another (Home), and the data shape is recurring-schedule (not per-broadcast), the hardcoded `sessions-data.js` const is the right source. Don't reach for Dexie / Supabase when the catalogue is fixed and small. This is PM-190.d's lesson re-applied ŌĆö banked as a pattern, not a rule.

**What this doesn't ship.**
- Mind-before-Body reorder is home-only. engagement-v2 still has its own ordering. Achievements page (PM-322 design lock) hasn't shipped yet; when it does, its ordering should match this.
- Live-now detection ŌĆö the new renderer doesn't surface a green "ŌŚÅ LIVE NOW" tag because `sessions-data.js` has no concept of an actual YouTube broadcast being live right now. If a real-broadcast-status surface is wanted at home, a small layer on top reading `calendar_occurrences` for the `youtube_broadcast_id` + `[starts_at, ends_at)` window could add the green eyebrow on top of the otherwise-static card. Not built tonight; flag for backlog if it matters.
- Per-card hosting/instructor name ŌĆö sessions-data.js doesn't carry host_name fields. If Lewis wants instructor bylines, add a `host` field to each sessions-data.js entry and render conditionally. Not blocking.

## 2026-05-25 PM-331 ŌĆö Home progress pills cert badges + live carousel connect-parity [vyve-site af32355a]

**Scope.** Two surfaces on the home dashboard get aligned with the rest of the app ŌĆö both small but both visible-on-first-paint. Triggered by Dean reviewing the home in-app: progress pills hardcoded to zeros + live carousel placeholder-looking even when calendar_occurrences has real rows.

**Progress pills (My Progress section).** Switched from legacy 5 labels (Hydration / Movement / Mind / Nutrition / Sessions, hardcoded `value: 0`) to canonical engagement-v2 five (**Habits / Body / Mind / Connect / Check-ins**) with real lifetime counts from Dexie. Ring shows `lifetime % 30` toward next certificate (resetting sprint loop). A gold Lucide filled-star + count badge sits below the label showing `floor(lifetime / 30)` certificates earned. Badge hidden when 0 but slot height reserved so layout doesn't shift on first earn.

**Maths model ŌĆö resetting sprint, not escalating tiers.** Each pillar's ring shows `value % 30` (or 30/30 at exact 30/60/90 marks so it lands as "you just hit it" not "you reset to zero"). Certs = `floor(value / 30)`. Always feels close to the next reward ŌĆö opposite to the achievements campaign's 10-tier gemstone ladder (bronze ŌåÆ diamond) locked at PM-322 which is *escalating*. Home is the daily-driver sprint loop; Achievements page is the longer game. Two different mental models, intentionally.

**Pillar palette.** Mirrors engagement-v2 canonical colours via `.pill[data-pillar="X"]` selectors: Habits `#4DAAAA`, Body `#E09B3D`, Mind `#9B7AE0`, Connect `#E06060`, Check-ins `#3DB89F`. Old `.pill.green/.purple/.gold/.orange` classes retired.

**Data wiring.** `loadPillarCounts()` does seven parallel `VYVELocalDB.{table}.allFor(email)` reads matching engagement-v2.html exactly: `daily_habits` ŌåÆ Habits; `workouts + cardio + movement_activities` merged ŌåÆ Body; `mind_activities` ŌåÆ Mind; `connect_checkins` ŌåÆ Connect; `wellbeing_checkins` ŌåÆ Check-ins. Each pillar gets `t.value = count`, `t.certs = Math.floor(count / 30)`. Hooked into three places: (1) boot after email resolves, (2) `vyve-localdb-table-pulled` for any of the 7 source tables, (3) inside `_rerenderHome` so every optimistic bus event refreshes the rings instantly ŌĆö no separate per-event subscriber needed. Same `daily_habits` direct subscriber on the home also pumps pills (covers habits.html cross-page write).

**Live Sessions This Week carousel.** Replaced hero-style full-bleed `.live-card` pattern (CSS lines 871-936) with connect.html's `.scroll-card` pattern verbatim (lifted from connect.html lines 480-504). Vertical card: 84px thumb at top with `image_url` over gradient base, teal category eyebrow, title clamped to 2 lines, day-time at the bottom. Container changed from `.live-carousel` div to `.scroll-row`. Old `candle/zen/dawn` gradient classes dropped along with all the hero-style overlay machinery (`.live-card-overlay`, `.live-card-body`, `.live-card-tag`, `.live-card-title`, `.live-card-meta` ŌĆö all gone).

**Why this was needed.** Brain inspection + live SQL on `calendar_occurrences`: 193 active rows, 23 in the home-window (now ŌåÆ +7d), starting with Yoga at 06:00 tomorrow. Every row had `image_url` populated (`/thumb-yoga.jpg` etc.) but the renderer was ignoring it ŌĆö `.live-card-thumb` was just an empty div with the default gradient. Combined with `session_title: null` and `host_name: null` across the entire backfill, every card painted as identical gradient + category title + "VYVE team" filler. Real data, placeholder vibe. Connect.html had already solved this with its `.scroll-card` pattern; home was just out of step.

**Renderer changes.** `paintLiveCarousel()`: image layer `<img src="${escape(r.image_url)}" loading="lazy" onerror="this.remove();...">` injected inside `.scroll-card-thumb` when `image_url` non-null, mirroring connect.html's onerror-removes-self-and-overlay fallback exactly. Title falls back to a `LIVE_CAT_SHORT` map (8 entries) for headline shortening ŌĆö e.g. "Yoga, Pilates & Stretch" ŌåÆ "Yoga & Stretch", "Mindfulness & Mindset" ŌåÆ "Mindfulness". Host line drops entirely when `host_name` null (no more "VYVE team" filler). Live-now broadcast (`youtube_broadcast_id` set AND now in [starts_at, ends_at)) gets green eyebrow via `.scroll-card.is-live` class + "ŌŚÅ LIVE NOW" tag. Empty state renders nothing per ┬¦23.46 ŌĆö `LIVE_PLACEHOLDERS` constant retired entirely, no more "Evening Wind Down / Breathwork Reset" fake-data on cold boot.

**Tooling.**
- ┬¦23.41 fresh-HEAD re-check fired once mid-build. Initial fetch at PM-329 HEAD `7e271150`; live HEAD moved to `a9a98ac3` (parallel session shipped their PM-330 ŌĆö live-iframe restore touching index.html / sw.js / settings.html). Marker-only collision, no content overlap with my pills/carousel work. Re-fetched fresh base at new HEAD, replayed patches via sed-bumps (`PM-330` ŌåÆ `PM-331`, `Update 217` ŌåÆ `Update 218`, cache-key `pm330-...` ŌåÆ `pm331-...`). Clean rebase, no surgical re-application needed because their diff was 3 lines of markers only.
- ┬¦23.70 PM-claim-at-commit-time fired correctly ŌĆö my draft PM-330 collided with their PM-330, recomputed to PM-331 immediately after re-fetch.
- ┬¦23.52(a) all three blob bodies posted via base64-encoded urllib JSON (Python `urllib.request`), no argv path involved. Blob SHAs all 40-char hex per ┬¦23.52(c). Tree create with `base_tree=PARENT` per ┬¦23.41. Post-commit `files[].status` confirmed `{modified: 3, added: 0, removed: 0}` per ┬¦23.52(b). First-100-char re-fetch per ┬¦23.53 on all three files at the committed SHA matched expected file shape (DOCTYPE / // VYVE Service Worker / DOCTYPE).
- 9 inline `<script>` blocks all passed `node --check`. Tag balance 28/28. All `getElementById` IDs (`live-carousel`, `pills-row`, `vbb-marker`) cross-referenced against DOM.

**Files.** vyve-site `af32355a`. `index.html` 120283 ŌåÆ 126177 bytes (+229/-138 lines). `sw.js` cache key `pm330-live-iframe-restore-explicit-origin-a` ŌåÆ `pm331-home-pills-cert-carousel-parity-a`. `settings.html` vbb-marker `217` ŌåÆ `218`.

**No new ┬¦23 hard rules earned.** All patterns exercised are established doctrine ŌĆö surface-build using ┬¦23.39 optimistic-first (via `_rerenderHome`), ┬¦23.41 fresh-HEAD with parallel-session rebase, ┬¦23.45 PAT-direct via Vault, ┬¦23.46 honest-paint empty state, ┬¦23.52(a/b/c) blob hygiene, ┬¦23.53 commit response from file, ┬¦23.70 PM claim-at-commit-time.

**What this doesn't ship.**
- Lewis copy pass on "Habits / Body / Mind / Connect / Check-ins" labels ŌĆö current labels are working text, refinement is non-blocking.
- Achievements page mapping: home pills celebrate certificates earned but the cert detail / certificate document is still on `engagement-v2.html` and `certificates.html`. The PM-322 Achievements page (Hero + Map direction) will be the canonical destination when it ships ŌĆö home pills will deep-link to per-pillar rows then.
- No Mind certificate concept yet (Mind pillar fires the badge based on `mind_activities` row count but no dedicated certificate exists in `certificates.html`). When Achievements page lands, this resolves naturally as Mind has a first-class pillar slot in the catalog.
