## 2026-05-17 PM-166/167/168 + DB ŌĆö movement & cardio logging: data-loss, UUID and instant-paint fixes

**Session focus.** A live-debug session on movement.html / cardio.html logging. The exercise.html audit "commits 5-7" were the nominal task; commit 5 shipped (PM-158 below), then a cascade of production logging bugs took over. Four vyve-site commits + one Supabase migration. Commits 6 and 7 (Past Sessions / Browse-library Dexie wiring) did NOT ship ŌĆö still outstanding.

**PM-158 (`a3b32065`) ŌĆö workout-history.html + workouts.html view-all.** New uncapped day-grouped gym-workout history page, cloned from cardio-history.html, Dexie-first, reads the local `workouts` store filtered to `plan_name!=='Movement'` (movement-logged sessions share the workouts table ŌĆö they belong to movement-history). "View all workout history" link added to the Past Sessions view. sw.js precache + key bump. NOTE: "PM-158" was also used by the parallel Mind session for the nav.js Mind-tab commit ŌĆö number collision, SHAs are canonical.

**DB migration `drop_before_insert_cap_triggers_cardio_workouts`.** Dropped `enforce_cap_cardio` and `enforce_cap_workouts` ŌĆö `BEFORE INSERT` triggers that diverted any over-2/day insert to `activity_dedupe` and returned NULL, i.e. they **physically discarded logged activity**. Confirmed root cause of "log shows then disappears": cycling/swimming/extra walks were being binned. The cap is correctly a *credit* concept (certificates, charity, engagement) ŌĆö the `AFTER INSERT` counter triggers (`counter_cardio`, `charity_count_cardio`, etc.) already credit-cap at 2/day by checking sibling rows, and they remain in place. Dean's directive: raw activity tables store EVERYTHING (100 sessions = 100 rows); the cap applies only when computing credits. The full conversion of the increment-counters to the stateless recompute pattern (cf. `update_cert_sessions_count` for sessions, which already does it right) is a separate P0 ŌĆö see backlog.

**PM-166 (`5ab7a148`) ŌĆö cardio history excludes movement-page walks.** A walk logged on movement.html is a `cardio` row with `logged_via='movement'` (PM-159). cardio.html `fetchHistory` (Dexie filter + REST `&logged_via=is.null`) and cardio-history.html `collectLogs` now exclude those, so movement walks stay on the Movement page's history. Cardio-page logs leave `logged_via` null and are unaffected.

**PM-167 (`304258cf`) ŌĆö client_id UUID fix (logged-then-vanished, all activity).** ROOT CAUSE, confirmed in postgres logs (`invalid input syntax for type uuid: "c-1778978..."`). `client_id` is a `uuid` column. When `VYVEData.newClientId()` was unavailable the per-page fallbacks emitted invalid values: cardio.html / workouts-session.js used `'c-<ts>-<hex>'` (not a uuid -> Postgres rejected the POST -> optimistic row reverted), movement.html used `null` (row inserted but the optimistic Dexie upsert could not key a null-id row -> Recent Movement read back nothing). Fix: all three loggers fall back to `crypto.randomUUID()`. New ┬¦23 rule added.

**PM-168 (`9887b6df`) ŌĆö movement Recent list instant paint.** `renderMovementLog` only ran after the network POST; cold-load boot painted once before Dexie hydrated. Symptom: empty on open, and after a log it showed the PREVIOUS state (one step behind). Fix: render immediately after the optimistic Dexie upsert in both `logMovement` branches (before the fetch); cold-load boot re-renders once `criticalHydrate('workouts'+'cardio')` resolves. movement-history.html also gained `criticalHydrate('cardio')` so walks appear on first paint.

**Build marker.** index.html `vbb-marker` bumped per commit (Update 24 -> 26) so Dean's `?debug=build` banner reflects device build state ŌĆö new memory rule established this session.

**Concurrency note.** This session ran concurrently with the Mind-section session. Multiple sw.js / index.html / nav.js collisions were caught via fresh-SHA-before-commit; no lost updates, but two sessions on vyve-site simultaneously is fragile ŌĆö flagged to Dean.

**Device-verified:** cardio logging confirmed perfect by Dean (PM-166 + PM-167). PM-168 movement instant-paint pending device check.

## 2026-05-17 PM-160ŌåÆ165 ŌĆö Mind section brought fully in line with the portal (colour, header, icons, light/dark)

**Six vyve-site commits continuing the Mind-section work after PM-159.** One campaign: take the seven Mind pages from "functional but not native" to "indistinguishable from the rest of the app."

**Note on PM numbering:** "PM-160" was used by two parallel sessions ŌĆö this Mind-section theme.css fix, AND an "instant on-device achievements" design entry (see backlog top). PM numbers are being reused across concurrent sessions; the commit SHAs below are the unambiguous reference.

### PM-160 ŌĆö `301e4099` ŌĆö Mind pages flat/uncoloured header + bottom nav
nav.js consumes 15 CSS variables (`--nav-bg`, `--nav-bg-mob`, `--nav-border`, `--nav-text`, `--nav-active`, `--more-bg`, etc.) and **defines none** ŌĆö they live in `/theme.css`. The Mind pages never linked theme.css, so every `--nav-*` resolved to nothing and nav.js's injected header + bottom nav fell through to transparent/flat-black. Fix: added `<link rel="stylesheet" href="/theme.css">` to all 7 pages, after the fonts link and before the inline `<style>`. theme.css is pure `:root` variable declarations ŌĆö zero element selectors ŌĆö so it cannot restyle page content.

### PM-160b ŌĆö `bf36166d` ŌĆö sw.js Mind precache restore
The parallel "PM-159 movement-history" commit (`c00458e4`) rebased sw.js off a pre-PM-159 snapshot and silently reverted PM-159's addition of the 7 Mind pages to `urlsToCache`; PM-160 then carried the regression forward. Re-added the block.

### PM-161 ŌĆö `dd51c93c` ŌĆö strip mind.html page-header
Removed the in-page header ŌĆö "Pillar ┬Ę Mental" eyebrow, "Mind" Playfair title, "Train your mind. Build the quiet." subtitle, and the dead-link (`href="#"`) settings cog + their CSS. Page opens straight into "Today's focus". nav.js mobile header still labels the page MIND; settings reachable via the More menu / avatar panel.

### PM-162 ŌĆö `56ec2950` ŌĆö wire 6 tool-tile icons
The six "Your tools" tiles had empty placeholder `.thumb-tool` blocks. Wired each to its icon image (`/mind-*.png`, repo root, uploaded manually by Dean ŌĆö chat-sandboxŌåÆworkbench binary transfer is not viable). New `.thumb-img` class + `.thumb.has-img::after` suppresses the hatch placeholder on image thumbs only. **The 6 icons are AI-generated raster placeholder art for the soft-launch trial ŌĆö NOT a premium vector set.** Same placeholder category as PM-94. A proper vector set (Lucide-family, matching nav.js + exercise.html `ico-*` iconography) is on the post-trial backlog. Body hub (exercise.html) already has the correct vector icons ŌĆö Body needs no equivalent work; the consistency fix is converting Mind UP to vector, never Body down to raster.

### PM-163 ŌĆö `1a3b7995` ŌĆö light mode part 1: remove inline `:root`
All 7 Mind pages carried a single inline `:root` (from the ChatGPT mockups) hardcoding dark values for `--bg`/`--text`/`--surface`/`--border` etc. The inline `<style>` loads after the linked theme.css, so that `:root` WON the cascade and shadowed theme.css ŌĆö theme.js flipping `data-theme` had no effect. Every token was already in theme.css with full dark+light values; fix was to delete the inline `:root` entirely on all 7.

### PM-164 ŌĆö `2d89f96a` ŌĆö light mode part 2: theme-broken colour literals
The shared Mind `<style>` (identical across all 7 pages) had 6 component rules with hardcoded dark literals ŌĆö `.hero-card`/`.thumb`/`.jr-prompt`/`.af-card`/`.vz-hero` gradients faded into `rgba(13,43,43,*)` (near-black, a dark smear in light mode), `.af-act` used an `rgba(0,0,0,0.22)` black inset. Fix: gradient bottom-stops ŌåÆ `var(--bg)` (keeps the teal-into-page-depth fade, correct both themes); `.af-act` ŌåÆ `var(--surface)`. Remaining literals are teal/gold brand accents (theme-safe) or a 2.5%-white hatch texture that fades harmlessly in light ŌĆö `exercise.html` carries the identical hatch, so it is an app-wide pattern, not a Mind defect.

### PM-165 ŌĆö `5acb730f` ŌĆö light mode part 3: theme-init parity
PM-163/164 made the pages theme correctly, but the theme-INIT path still diverged from habits/exercise.html: `<html>` hardcoded `data-theme="dark"`, and theme.js was `defer`'d AND positioned after theme.css. A deferred theme.js runs post-parse ŌĆö the hardcoded attr was masking a flash-of-wrong-theme for light-mode users on first paint. Fix, matching exercise.html exactly: `<html lang="en">` bare (theme.js owns the attr); theme.js moved first in `<head>` right after `<title>`, `defer` dropped so it runs synchronously before theme.css. auth.js stays deferred.

**State after PM-165:** the Mind section is verified in line with the rest of the portal ŌĆö links theme.css, loads theme.js synchronously-first, zero inline `:root`, zero dark-black literals, bare `<html>`. Light/dark flips identically to habits.html / exercise.html. Build banner Update 18ŌåÆ23 across the campaign (PM-161 first introduced the per-commit banner-bump discipline).

**Still open (Mind section):** (a) the 6 tool icons are trial placeholders ŌĆö proper vector set post-trial; (b) Meditation + Sleep tiles both link `mind-library.html` (no dedicated pages) ŌĆö product decision pending (deep-link vs own pages); (c) breathwork is the next real feature build ŌĆö first Mind page to get `mind_sessions` Dexie/Supabase wiring; (d) `mind_sessions` schema still deferred until breathwork's real fields exist.


## 2026-05-17 PM-159 ŌĆö Mind section: strip leftover mockup nav from 7 pages, wire real chrome (+ PM-158 retro-doc)

**vyve-site commit `59a3fff6` ŌĆö 8 files (7 Mind pages + sw.js).** This entry also retro-documents PM-158 (last session), which never reached the brain.

### PM-158 (last session, 16 May 2026, retro-documented here)
Seven Mind-section pages were built from ChatGPT mockups and shipped to vyve-site main:
- `mind.html` (Mind hub landing), `mind-library.html`, `breathwork.html`, `journal.html`, `affirmations.html`, `visualisation.html`, `mind-insights.html`.
- Commit history: the seven pages landed as `ca08f2966` ("Add files via upload" ŌĆö raw mockup HTML, unconverted). nav.js gained the Mind tab as `e1c29a722` ("PM-158: add Mind tab to nav.js").
- nav.js now renders a **5-tab bottom bar: Home / Body / Mind / Sessions / More.** `getActiveTab()` recognises mind/breathwork/journal/affirmations/visualisation paths; `hubPaths` includes `/mind.html`; `hubLabels`/`desktopLinks`/`navItems` carry the Mind tab; `subPageLabels` has all six Mind sub-pages.
- **The Mind pages are scaffold** ŌĆö placeholder content, no Dexie/Supabase wiring. Mind data architecture is decided but not built (see below).
- PM-158 the number was also used by exercise.html-audit commit 5 (`a3b32065`, workout-history) ŌĆö hence sw.js had cache key `pm158-workout-history`. The number is consumed twice; this nav-fix session is PM-159.

### PM-159 ŌĆö the nav bug (this session)
**Symptom (Dean, device screenshots end of last session):** Mind pages rendered a bottom nav of Home / Body / Mind / **Connect** / **Profile** with a brain-pair "Mind" glyph, while the rest of the app (post-PM-158) correctly shows Home / Body / Mind / Sessions / More.

**Root cause ŌĆö worse than the brief's hypothesis, cleaner to fix.** The Mind pages were uploaded raw (`ca08f2966`) with the ChatGPT mockup's own hardcoded `<nav class="bnav">` (Home/Body/Mind/Connect/Profile, dead `href="#"` links, brain-pair glyph) **and its `.bnav*` CSS block intact**. They also had **zero `<script>` tags** ŌĆö no `theme.js`, no `auth.js`, no `nav.js`. So the pages were not rendering nav.js *alongside* a leftover nav; they were rendering **only** the fake mockup nav, with no real nav.js at all, and were **not auth-gated**. nav.js itself was already correct from PM-158.

**Fix ŌĆö uniform across all 7 pages:**
- Stripped the `<nav class="bnav">ŌĆ”</nav>` block + the 5 `.bnav*` CSS rules.
- Injected `theme.js` + `auth.js` into `<head>`; `sw.js` registration + `nav.js` before `</body>` ŌĆö mirroring habits.html ordering.
- **Scope decision (Dean's autonomy standard):** wired the *minimal correct chain* ŌĆö theme.js, auth.js, nav.js, sw-registration ŌĆö NOT the full local-first stack (`db.js`/`sync.js`/`firstPaintHydrate.js`/etc.). The Mind pages are scaffold with no data; dead Dexie scripts on placeholder pages would be noise. The local-first chain lands per page when each Mind page gets real `mind_sessions` wiring ŌĆö breathwork first.
- Chrome now byte-identical to every other portal page: real 5-tab nav, no Connect, no Profile, standard mobile header.

**sw.js (caught in passing ŌĆö a PM-158 gap):** the 7 Mind pages were never added to `urlsToCache`, so they round-tripped to the GitHub Pages CDN every load and failed offline cold-boot ŌĆö the same gap PM-67a/PM-118 fixed for other pages. Added all 7 in the same commit. Cache key bumped `vyve-cache-v2026-05-17-pm158-workout-history-a` ŌåÆ `vyve-cache-v2026-05-17-pm159-mind-nav-fix-a`.

`node --check` passed on every inline JS block (8 pages) and on sw.js. Post-commit verified all 8 files against the pinned commit SHA `59a3fff6` ŌĆö every Mind page byte-equal to the local transform; bnav absent; theme.js/auth.js/nav.js present; sw.js carries pm159 key + 7 precache entries. **Device verification pending Dean.**

### Mind section architecture (decided, recorded here; not all built)
Captured from Dean across PM-77.2 (PF-21/PF-22 plan) and the PM-158/159 sessions:
- **Nav direction is BODY / Mind / Connect**, not the original five-pillar website framing. **Nutrition folds into BODY** (done PM-154 ŌĆö nutrition.html + log-food.html are Body sub-pages; "Body" replaced "Exercise"). Mind is the new section being built out. "Connect" is the future social/community tab ŌĆö not yet a live nav tab (current live nav is Home/Body/Mind/Sessions/More; Sessions occupies the 4th slot until Connect exists).
- **Mind data architecture:** a dedicated `mind_sessions` Supabase table with an `activity_type` discriminator column. Mind activity is **never** written to `daily_habits`. Schema is **deferred** until breathwork's real fields exist (build the first real feature, then derive the schema from it).
- **`mind-insights.html` is Phil-gated** ŌĆö clinical sign-off required before promotion. It is reachable (linked from mind.html) but treated as staged content; correctly absent from nav.js `navItems`/`moreItems` so it never surfaces in the nav bar.
- **Breathwork is the next real feature build** after this nav fix ŌĆö first page to get real `mind_sessions` wiring and the local-first chain.
- **Goal/certificate re-pillaring** (moving goals + certificate tracks off the per-activity Habits/Workouts/Cardio model onto the BODY/Mind/Connect pillars) is **deferred to post-launch**.

**Open:** device verification of the 7 fixed pages; breathwork feature build + `mind_sessions` schema; per-page local-first wiring of Mind pages; Phil sign-off on mind-insights.html.


## 2026-05-16 PM-155 FOLLOW-UP ŌĆö BUG: Recent Movement log list shows empty (source-vocabulary mismatch)

**Found by Dean's device walk same night.** Logged a walk + a stretch on movement.html; the new Recent Movement list still showed "no movement logged yet". cardio.html history showed the walk fine (it filters nothing).

**Root cause.** PM-155's `renderMovementLog` filters rows on `source==='movement_walk'` (cardio) / `source==='movement'` (workouts). But `logMovement`'s PostgREST `payload` writes `source:'manual'` ŌĆö verified against live rows (`test1@test.com`, all tonight's movement logs are `source:'manual'`). The `movement_walk`/`movement` strings only ever existed in the **bus publish vocabulary**, never in the stored `source` column. PM-155 built the reader against the bus namespace, not the column. The optimistic Dexie write spreads `...payload`, so Dexie rows are `source:'manual'` too ŌĆö reader misses everything.

**Why the obvious fix (retag source) is WRONG ŌĆö do not do it.** `cap_cardio` / `cap_workouts` enforce the 2/day activity cap ONLY on rows where `coalesce(source,'manual')='manual'`; non-manual sources are deliberate pass-through (Watch users). Changing movement logs to `source='movement'/'movement_walk'` would silently remove the 2/day cap from every movement-logged walk/stretch ŌĆö breaking activity-cap integrity, the certificate counters, and the charity mechanic. `source:'manual'` on movement quick-log rows is CORRECT and must stay.

**Fix direction (pending Dean's decision).** The movement log list must discriminate its rows WITHOUT touching `source`. The tag is already in the data: movement-logged walks are `cardio` rows with `cardio_type='walking'`; movement-logged non-walk are `workouts` rows with `plan_name='Movement'` (the payload sets this literally). Filter `renderMovementLog` + movement-history.html on those instead. Open question for Dean: a walk logged from cardio.html is also `cardio_type='walking'` ŌĆö accept it appearing in Recent Movement, or keep cardio-page walks out (needs a real discriminator, more work).

**Status: NOT FIXED. Live breakage ŌĆö Recent Movement list non-functional on production.** Must be the first task of the next session, before commits 5ŌĆō7.

Both added to sw.js `urlsToCache` (offline-equivalent, ┬¦23.10 / PM-118).

## 2026-05-16 PM-153 ŌĆö fix PM-151 regression: added habits invisible on habits.html + un-saved on settings reopen

**Context.** Page-by-page paint audit, habits.html. After PM-151 shipped, Dean device-tested the settings habit picker: adding a habit + Save showed "saved" instantly in settings, but (a) the habits page did not show the new habit, and (b) reopening settings showed the habit set un-saved.

**Diagnosis ŌĆö both symptoms read-side; Supabase writes were landing fine.** Live query of `test1@test.com` `member_habits` confirmed the adds persisted server-side correctly (Morning light exposure `active:true assigned_by:self`, Caffeine curfew add+remove both landed). So PM-151's background Supabase path worked. Both failures were the local read layer.

**Bug 1 ŌĆö added habit invisible on habits.html.** PM-151's `_toAddRows` wrote THIN rows to Dexie `member_habits` ŌĆö `id, member_email, habit_id, assigned_at, assigned_by, active` ŌĆö with none of the denormalised `habit_library` join columns. habits.html's `loadHabitsPage` reshape and cache-first paint both `.filter()` on `habit_title` (the PM-110 undefined-card guard) ŌĆö so the new titleless row was silently dropped from the render. The habit was in Dexie; it just had no title. This is the ┬¦23.7.5 / PM-106 fat-row rule ŌĆö PM-151 broke it by writing thin into a fat store.

**Bug 2 ŌĆö reopen settings shows habits un-saved.** `saveHabits` never busted `vyve_settings_cache` (the 10-min settings cache). `savePersona` and `setDisplayNamePref` always have. On nav-back, `populateFromCache` painted the stale cached `memberHabits` synchronously before `loadProfile` refreshed.

**Shipped (vyve-site `deec34f8`, settings.html + sw.js + index.html):**
- `saveHabits` now builds TWO row shapes: `_supaAddRows` (thin ŌĆö FK columns only, what Postgres wants) and `_dexieAddRows` (fat ŌĆö `habit_pot/habit_title/habit_description/habit_prompt/difficulty` joined from `_habitLibrary`, plus `active:1`). The POST sends thin; the Dexie `bulkUpsert` writes fat.
- `habit_prompt` was missing from `_habitLibrary` itself ŌĆö added to both source queries (the Dexie habitLib map and the Supabase `habit_library` select).
- `saveHabits` now rewrites `vyve_settings_cache.memberHabits` to the selected set, same pattern `savePersona` uses.
- sw cache key ŌåÆ `pm153-habit-fatrow-a`. Build banner 17 ŌåÆ 18.

**No EF or schema change.** New ┬¦23 rule: ┬¦23.7.9 ŌĆö optimistic INSERT rows must be fat for fat stores.

**Device-side caveat (not a code issue):** Dean's `test1@test.com` Dexie still holds the one thin Morning light exposure row written by the broken PM-151 code. The fix is correct for all future saves but does not retroactively fatten that row ŌĆö an in-app cache reset (settings long-press footer, PM-104 gesture) re-hydrates it fat. Fresh members never hit this.

Dean chose **Option A** ŌĆö both `habit_description` and `habit_prompt` collapse into the dropdown; resting card shows title + chevron + assignment eyebrow only. Collapsed by default every render; whole title row is the tap target.

**Shipped (vyve-site `4baa445c`, habits.html + sw.js + index.html):**
- `habitCardHTML`: difficulty `<span>` removed; title wrapped in a `.habit-title-row` (role=button, tabindex=0, aria-expanded) with a chevron; `habit_description` + `habit_prompt` moved into a `.habit-collapse` region with no `expanded` class at build time.
- CSS: three `.habit-difficulty` rules removed; `.habit-title-row` / `.habit-chevron` / `.habit-collapse` added; `.habit-prompt` bottom margin dropped (no longer the element above the action buttons).

- The `difficulty` column still flows through member-dashboard + the Dexie reshape ŌĆö simply no longer rendered.
- sw cache key ŌåÆ `pm152-habit-card-a`. Build banner 16 ŌåÆ 17.

## 2026-05-16 PM-151 ŌĆö settings habit/persona/goal saves: local-first critical-path order

**Context.** Page-by-page paint audit, habits.html. Dean reported the settings "manage habits" save taking a couple of seconds and not propagating to habits.html. Diagnosed against ┬¦23.7.6: all three settings save handlers awaited their Supabase writes ON the critical path before any local state moved.

**Correction to an earlier diagnosis.** An earlier read of this called the Dexie `member_habits.upsert` a hang caused by a missing `id` keypath. That was wrong ŌĆö `member_habits` PK is the compound `[member_email+habit_id]`, the upsert override supplies both halves and is correct. The real "couple of seconds" was the awaited sequential network: a 3-habit removal made 3 blocking PATCH round trips before the modal closed. Dean's own read of it ("saving works, it just needs to hit Dexie + bus instantly") was the correct one.

**Shipped (vyve-site `03d2b247`, settings.html + habits.html + sw.js + index.html):**
- `saveHabits`, `savePersona`, `saveGoal` rewritten to the ┬¦23.7.6 critical-path order: Dexie write + bus publish + local UI state first; the Supabase PATCH/POST calls move to a fire-and-forget background block. The member sees the modal close instantly; Supabase catches up after.
- `saveHabits` publishes a NEW `habits:set-changed` event (only per-tick `habit:logged` existed before). Added rows get a client-side `crypto.randomUUID()` so Dexie and Supabase agree on the row id. Failed background writes publish `habits:set-changed:failed`. The `size===0` early-return dropped in favour of a no-change guard (a member can now clear all habits).
- `saveGoal` gained a `specific_goal:changed` publish it never had.
- habits.html: new `habits:set-changed` + `:failed` subscribers + a `reloadHabitSet()` helper that re-reads `member_habits` from Dexie and rebuilds `habitsData`.
- sw cache key ŌåÆ `pm151-settings-localfirst-a`. Build banner 15 ŌåÆ 16.

**Note.** PM-151 introduced a regression (thin Dexie rows) fixed same session by PM-153 ŌĆö see above. **Known parked:** re-adding a previously-removed habit creates a second `member_habits` row (no unique constraint on `member_email,habit_id`); rides with the future locked-habits feature.

## 2026-05-16 PM-150 ŌĆö session_views storage/cap decoupled + 60s dwell threshold

**Context.** Page-by-page paint audit, index.html. Dean reported the home "This week's goals" sessions row stuck at 1/2 while "Your Progress" showed 2. Investigation found a far bigger issue than a paint bug.

**Root cause ŌĆö a schema/trigger mismatch silently destroying data.** The `cap_session_views` / `cap_replay_views` BEFORE INSERT triggers enforced a flat 2/day cap by *rerouting* the 3rd+ session insert of the day into `activity_dedupe` ŌĆö and the cap counted `session_views` + `replay_views` summed, ignoring category. The app's data model (tracking.js, `on_conflict=member_email,category,activity_date`) assumes one row per category per day across 8 categories. So from the 3rd category onward, every session view silently vanished ŌĆö PostgREST returned 201, the API log showed success, tracking.js saw success, but the row was gone. `test1@test.com` had 10 such rows stranded in `activity_dedupe`; 544 total across 5 members historically.

**Compounding finding.** The magic number `2` was hardcoded into FOUR triggers with THREE different counting rules: `cap_*` (cross-table sum Ōēź2 ŌåÆ reroute), `charity_count_*` (per-table <2), `counter_sessions` (per-table <2, bumps cert_sessions_count), `update_cert_sessions_count` (LEAST(count,2) per table per day). `cert_sessions_count` had two writers (`counter_sessions` + `update_cert_sessions_count`) ŌĆö latent divergence bug.

**Decision (Dean).** Keep the 2/day cap, but as a CERTIFICATE COUNTING rule only ŌĆö not a storage rule. Anti-farm moves from the destructive storage cap to a tracking.js dwell threshold. Storage saves everything; the certificate counter keeps LEAST(...,2) unchanged. No uncapping of certificates ŌĆö explicitly declined ("I don't want people watching eight different things and getting eight certificates").

**Shipped ŌĆö migration (4 statements, applied + verified via pg_trigger):**
- Dropped `enforce_cap_session_views` / `enforce_cap_replay_views` triggers + `cap_session_views()` / `cap_replay_views()` functions. Session views no longer rerouted.
- Dropped redundant `counter_sessions` trigger + `increment_session_counter()` ŌĆö `update_cert_sessions_count` is now the single writer for `cert_sessions_count`.
- Rewrote `charity_count_session_views()` ŌĆö kept the per-day <2 sibling guard so charity stays 2/day-capped, matching the certificate rule. `charity_count_replay_views` left unchanged (already per-table <2, correct).
- `update_cert_sessions_count` LEFT UNTOUCHED ŌĆö LEAST(daily,2) intact. The 2/day cap is fully preserved, now living only in the counting layer.
- One-time backfill: recomputed `cert_sessions_count` across all members under the unchanged LEAST(daily,2) rule.

**Shipped ŌĆö tracking.js v9 (vyve-site `9a95ab5c`, 3 files atomic):**
- 60s cumulative dwell threshold. `onVisitStart` no longer inserts on page load ŌĆö it starts the heartbeat timer as a threshold poller. `crossThreshold()` fires the insert + optimistic patch + `session:viewed` bus publish once, when `totalMinutes() >= 1`. Sub-minute open-and-bounce visits never insert (anti-farm).
- `baseMinutes >= 1` fast-paths an already-qualified category straight to heartbeats.
- Failed insert resets the `inserted` flag so a later heartbeat retries (improvement over v8's one-shot give-up).
- PM-43/54/61 bus discipline (recordWrite / recordCanonical / canonical envelope) moved verbatim into `crossThreshold`.
- Build banner Update 14 ŌåÆ 15; sw cache key ŌåÆ `vyve-cache-v2026-05-16-pm150-session-threshold-a`.

**Data repair.** `test1@test.com`'s 10 stranded `activity_dedupe` rows replayed into `session_views`/`replay_views` ŌĆö collapsed per (category, table) with minutes SUMmed, merged into existing live rows via ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE. The 534 historical rows for other members deliberately LEFT in `activity_dedupe` ŌĆö they were legitimately capped under the old rule; replaying them would retroactively rewrite member history (would be a Lewis decision; not done).

**Device-verified.** Yoga 20s ŌåÆ no row (sub-threshold rejected). Workouts two short bounces ŌåÆ no row (each visit resets; neither crossed 60s ŌĆö correct, that is the farm pattern). Podcast 70s uninterrupted ŌåÆ row inserted, `minutes_watched=1.24` (the accumulated value proves the insert fired at threshold-cross, not on load). Ship Unit 1 verified end-to-end.

**Known caveat (not fixed, noted).** The dwell accumulator resets on page unload ŌĆö `visitStartTime` is in-memory, only `baseMinutes` (server `minutes_watched`) survives. Two sub-threshold visits do not sum. Correct for anti-farm; if true cross-visit accumulation is wanted later, the row must be created early-but-unqualified or minutes tracked in Dexie. Post-launch, backlogged.

**New ┬¦23 rule:** ┬¦23.34 ŌĆö a counting cap implemented as a storage-destroying trigger; magic numbers duplicated across triggers with divergent rules.

## 2026-05-16 PM-149 ŌĆö INCIDENT: site-wide login outage ŌĆö `platform-alert` storm drained the nano-tier connection pool; no-op mitigation + project restart

**Shape:** Live production incident, picked up mid-debug from a prior session that had shipped four vyve-site commits (`b1470698`, `b5ee7854`, `207aa1b0`, `9e1d83bb` ŌĆö PM-148/148b) and then hit a site-wide login break. The prior session had ruled the four commits out (correctly) and pinned the symptom: `login.html` hangs on "Signing inŌĆ”", app shows "Preview mode: Auth failed to initialise", browser console `POST /auth/v1/token ŌåÆ net::ERR_FAILED 522`. Not a vyve-site ship ŌĆö one Edge Function deploy (`platform-alert` v8 ŌåÆ v9) plus a Dean-executed project restart.

**Root cause.** The PM-145 `platform-alert` storm, escalated from edge-case to total outage. `platform-alert` v8 has no rate limit and a broken dedup key (dedups on raw `type`, which varies per error). A client error storm produced 40+ concurrent `POST /platform-alert` invocations, each running 90s (500) or 150s (504) ŌĆö confirmed in edge-function logs, storm still live an hour in (most recent OPTIONS preflight minutes before diagnosis). Edge Functions share one compute/connection pool; 40+ stuck 90-150s functions each holding or waiting on a DB connection exhausted the **nano-tier 60-connection ceiling** (`max_connections` confirmed 60 via `pg_settings`). GoTrue could not get a connection ŌĆö auth log decisive line `failed to connect to host=localhost user=supabase_auth_admin database=postgres: dial error`. Every `/auth/v1/token` then 504'd or died at the Cloudflare edge as 522.

**Correcting the prior session's working theory.** The theory was "nano Postgres overwhelmed, pool exhausted." Half right: the pool *was* exhausted, but not by query *work* ŌĆö the box had spare CPU (54%, inconsistent with saturation, a point Dean flagged and was right to). It was exhausted by connection-slot starvation: 40+ stuck Edge Functions squatting on slots. Postgres wasn't overwhelmed; it was starved of slots. `could not accept SSL connection: EOF detected` in the postgres log = clients giving up because the server couldn't fork a backend.

**Diagnostic miss (recorded honestly).** Mid-incident, after deploying the no-op, `select 1` succeeded and this was reported as "Postgres is back / database outage resolved." That was wrong: `execute_sql` runs via the **management API**, a different path from the public API gateway. The gateway was still 522-ing on everything (`/auth/v1/token`, `/rest/v1/*`) ŌĆö confirmed only when the `api` service log was finally pulled. Lesson: `select 1` proves the mgmt-APIŌåÆDB path, NOT the public edge. Verifying the wrong path and reporting the building open. New rule ┬¦23.33.

**Fix ŌĆö two parts.**
1. **`platform-alert` v8 ŌåÆ v9 (no-op).** Deployed via `SUPABASE_DEPLOY_FUNCTION`, `verify_jwt:false` preserved. v9 keeps v8's CORS/allowed-origins exactly, but the handler is an instant `200` ŌĆö no DB read, no Brevo, no VAPID push. Severs the storm: alert POSTs stop holding connections. Post-deploy the DB pool drained 60/60 ŌåÆ 13/60 and `select 1` + Postgres recovered. But the **public API gateway stayed wedged** (522s continued) ŌĆö a layer not fixable from SQL or a function deploy.
2. **Project restart (Dean-executed).** Recycled the whole nano instance including the edge/proxy layer. API log confirms the sequence: 522s until 18:44 ŌåÆ restart 503s on `/auth/v1/health` + `/rest-admin/v1/ready` ~18:52 ŌåÆ clean 200s from 18:53 (`scheduled_pushes`, `watchdog`, RPC all 200, latest 19:05). Edge gateway healthy.

**State at close.** DB healthy, edge gateway healthy, storm dead (`platform-alert` is the no-op v9). Login confirmed working at the infrastructure level; Dean reported "looks like it might be fixed" on a real attempt ŌĆö treated as a confirmed-but-not-hard-verified close. No fresh browser `/auth/v1/token` 200 is in the logs yet because none has occurred post-restart; if the next real sign-in misbehaves, reopen.

**Decisions explicitly left to Dean (not actioned):** project restart was Dean's to execute (he did); compute-tier change was NOT made; the four PM-148 commits were NOT reverted (confirmed irrelevant ŌĆö `login.html` does not load `workouts-session.js`).

**Open, carried forward:**
- **`platform-alert` is a no-op ŌĆö platform error-monitoring is OFF.** Must build a real v10: working dedup key, rate limit, drop the dead `push_subscriptions` write. This is the PM-145 work, now proven P0.
- **nano-tier 60-connection ceiling is the structural fragility** that turned a misbehaving function into a total outage. Backlog P3-1 (`max_connections` 60ŌåÆ200+ / tier lift) now has its evidence. Dean's call.

**New hard rule:** ┬¦23.33 ŌĆö `execute_sql` / `select 1` proves only the management-APIŌåÆPostgres path; it does NOT prove the public API gateway. During an outage, verify the `api` service log (and the layer the user's client actually hits) before declaring recovery.

## 2026-05-16 PM-148b ŌĆö HOTFIX: PM-148 broke workouts.html (duplicate ordinalSuffix declaration)

**The break.** PM-148 (`b1470698`) added `function ordinalSuffix(n)` at the top level of `workouts-session.js`, on the belief ŌĆö stated in a code comment ŌĆö that `completeWorkout`'s reference to `ordinalSuffix()` was a "latent ReferenceError, never defined in this file." That belief was wrong. `ordinalSuffix` is already declared in `workouts-config.js`. workouts.html loads both `workouts-config.js` and `workouts-session.js` as classic deferred `<script>`s ŌĆö they share one global scope. Two top-level `function ordinalSuffix` declarations in the same scope is a hard `SyntaxError: Identifier 'ordinalSuffix' has already been declared`; `workouts-session.js` failed to parse, the Exercise page never ran its init, and it sat permanently on its loading skeleton. Dean reported the broken page within minutes (screenshot).

**Why it slipped through.** The PM-148 pre-commit check ran `node --check workouts-session.js` ŌĆö which validates one file in isolation and *cannot* see a cross-file shared-scope collision. The faulty premise came from grepping `ordinalSuffix` in `workouts-session.js` alone (1 hit = the call site) and concluding it was undefined, without checking the sibling `workouts-*.js` files that share scope. `COMPLETION_MESSAGES` was the same story ŌĆö also defined in `workouts-config.js`; the old `completeWorkout` referenced it fine for the same reason. There was no latent bug; the "fix" manufactured the outage.

**The fix (`207aa1b0`).** Deleted the `ordinalSuffix` block PM-148 added. The `workouts-config.js` definition resolves in shared scope as it always did. Verified: zero top-level name collisions between `workouts-session.js` and `workouts-config.js` (36 vs 34 top-level names, intersection empty). sw.js cache key ŌåÆ `vyve-cache-v2026-05-16-pm148b-hotfix-a`. The PM-148 `completeWorkout` optimistic-first rewrite itself was sound and is unchanged ŌĆö only the bogus helper was removed.

**Rule strengthened:** ┬¦23.32 extended ŌĆö for any file sharing global scope with siblings (the `workouts-*.js` set on workouts.html being the canonical case), `node --check` on one file is not sufficient; a new top-level declaration must be checked against every sibling's top-level names. Never conclude an identifier is undefined from a single-file grep when classic scripts share scope.

## 2026-05-16 PM-148 ŌĆö completeWorkout optimistic-first: the 'Saving...' hang root cause, write-path latency, brain drift corrections

**Session shape:** "Load VYVE brain" continuation from PM-142ŌĆō147. Began as PM-145 (member-dashboard 504s) per Dean's pick; the diagnosis chain moved through three layers and Dean's own observations re-pointed the session twice, landing on a different, higher-value fix. Two vyve-site commits: `b1470698` (PM-148 ŌĆö completeWorkout rewrite + sw.js bump), `b5ee7854` (build banner Update 12 ŌåÆ 13). Both node --check'd, blob-verified byte-equal at the new commit SHA.

**The PM-145 diagnosis (not shipped ŌĆö superseded, but findings recorded).** member-dashboard v69 504s were investigated. Live EF read + edge-function/postgres logs showed it is NOT primarily the achievements payload. The real chain: workout/exercise write errors ŌåÆ client-side JS errors ŌåÆ the portal's `auth.js` global error catcher POSTs `platform-alert` on every error ŌåÆ `platform-alert` v8 has three bugs of its own (dedup keys on raw `type` which varies per-error so nothing dedupes; no rate limit; pushes to the dead `push_subscriptions` table with the pre-`send-push`-v12 `'raw'` VAPID import bug) ŌåÆ an alert storm (20+ calls/90s, 27-64s each) drains the shared Edge Function compute pool ŌåÆ every function queues, member-dashboard (the fattest) 504s, and even 401s take 100s sitting in the queue. Also confirmed: two achievements evaluators query dead columns ŌĆö `workouts_shared` filters `shared_workouts.member_email` (real column is `shared_by`), and a `monthly_checkins.logged_at` query (no such column) throws in the achievements path. **┬¦11A is stale:** it describes member-dashboard v55 / 23-serial-evaluators; live is v69, v60 already parallelised the evaluators via `Promise.all`. Dean's call: `platform-alert` and the dashboard 504s are back-burner (edge-case/reinstall path only; normal users are Dexie-first and unaffected). Findings preserved in backlog for when PM-145 is picked up.

**The pivot ŌĆö Dean's observations re-pointed the session.** Dean clarified: (1) the "5-6 log entries" was NOT a misfire ŌĆö the workout sat on "Complete workout" for a long time, looked dead, so he tapped repeatedly; each tap eventually POSTed. The real bug is the **hanging completion**, not phantom inserts. (2) Cardio "not logging" was a misread ŌĆö the 3 extra cardio sessions correctly did NOT move the engagement score because of the ┬¦23.31 2/day cap (PM-147 working as designed). (3) Engagement page is correct (87); the **home page is stale (74)** ŌĆö same member, two screens, two scores. (4) Weekly check-in + 2 sessions show on engagement but not in "This Week's Goals" on home.

**PM-148 root cause ŌĆö `completeWorkout` awaits the network.** `VYVEData.writeQueued` (vyve-offline.js) is misleadingly named: it is NOT a fire-and-forget outbox. Its body does `await fetch(...)` synchronously and only resolves once the server responds; it only falls back to the localStorage outbox queue on 5xx/network-failure. A **slow-but-eventually-successful** response is the worst case ŌĆö it never queues, it just blocks. `completeWorkout` did `await VYVEData.writeQueued(...)` for the workout POST, again for the `workout_plan_cache` PATCH, and `await getTotalWorkoutCount()` (its own network count) ŌĆö three serial awaited network calls, all on the visible button path. Backend slow (same overload behind the 504s) ŌåÆ button frozen on "Saving..." 75s+. And because `VYVEHomeState.optimisticPatch` + the `workout:logged` bus publish sat *downstream* of the awaited POST, the home page never updated until the round-trip completed ŌĆö the stale-74 home score. The PM-120 "optimistic Dexie write" was bolted in *after* the awaited writeQueued, so it never helped. Engagement reads Dexie directly (PM-119/146/147) so it was always correct.

**PM-148 fix (`b1470698`) ŌĆö `completeWorkout` rewritten optimistic-first.** Mirrors cardio.html `logCardio` (the proven Update-3 pattern Dean confirmed "logs instantly"). New order: generate client_id + payload ŌåÆ Dexie `workouts.upsert` (awaited ŌĆö local, ~ms) ŌåÆ `VYVEHomeState.optimisticPatch` + `recordWrite`/`recordCanonical` + `VYVEBus.publish('workout:logged')` ŌåÆ advance programme counter locally (Dexie merge + localStorage) ŌåÆ tear down the session view ŌåÆ build + show the completion screen (workout count is now a Dexie-only read, no network fallback) ŌåÆ THEN the workouts POST and the plan_cache PATCH fire un-awaited in background `(async function(){...})()` closures. 4xx on the POST ŌåÆ `workout:failed` publish + drop the optimistic Dexie row; 5xx/network ŌåÆ keep the row (reconciles on next sync). The only awaits on the visible path are now local Dexie ops. cardio's pattern uses a raw un-awaited `fetch` rather than `writeQueued`; PM-148 does the same ŌĆö `writeQueued` is left untouched (changing the shared primitive has cardio/habits/set-logging blast radius). Also: `ordinalSuffix()` was referenced by `completeWorkout` but never defined anywhere in the file ŌĆö a latent `ReferenceError` on the completion screen whenever `totalWorkouts > 0`; defined this commit. `getTotalWorkoutCount()` is now dead (left in place, harmless). sw.js cache key ŌåÆ `vyve-cache-v2026-05-16-pm148-workout-optimistic-a`. `b5ee7854` bumped the build banner to Update 13 as the on-device gate.

**Not closed / open:** PM-148 device confirmation pending (Dean to verify: Complete Workout ŌåÆ instant completion screen, no "Saving..." hang; home engagement score moves immediately). The **per-set save path** (`tickSet` / exercise_logs write, workouts-session.js ~L421) has the identical `await writeQueued` blocking shape ŌĆö not reported slow, left untouched, latent. PM-145 / `platform-alert` back-burnered by Dean. The check-in + sessions not appearing in "This Week's Goals" is a separate write-path issue (check-in and session writes, not the workout write) ŌĆö PM-148 does not touch it.

**New hard rule:** ┬¦23.32 ŌĆö `VYVEData.writeQueued` awaits the network internally; it is not a fire-and-forget queue. Never `await` it on a paint/interaction path.

## 2026-05-16 PM-142ŌĆō147 ŌĆö Engagement + cardio local-first: botched-commit recovery, paint-gating, the load-order root cause, 2/day cap

**Session shape:** Debugging continuation from the PM-128ŌĆō133 local-first session, all on the `server.url` dev-loop on Dean's iPhone. Six vyve-site commits ŌĆö `f5675542` (PM-142), `81144bb8` (PM-143), `0d4933b8` (PM-144 probe), `50e7332c` (PM-146), `05fabadb` (PM-147). Build banner ran Update 8 ŌåÆ Update 12. Each commit node --check'd, blob-sha verified, sw.js cache key bumped atomically. The diagnosis arc was long and corrected itself several times ŌĆö two early theories (`!data.member` EF-shape guard; "Dexie empty") were checked against live state and discarded before the real cause was found.

**The symptom chain.** Engagement page skeletoned 20-30s on cold open then painted via the network EF, never from Dexie; on reopen it skeletoned again (cache not surviving). Cardio's running-plan card (`#rp-slot`) was permanently empty ŌĆö not even the no-plan callout.

**PM-142 (`f5675542`) ŌĆö recover the botched PM-135 commit.** PM-135 (`e88c57f1`) was a bad commit: its diff overwrote `getActiveRunningPlan()` with a stray duplicate of `logCardio()`, and shipped a new `renderRunningPlan()` calling `rpKvGet()` and `getActiveRunningPlan()` ŌĆö neither of which existed in the file. `renderRunningPlan` threw `ReferenceError` on its first `await`; the call site was bare/un-awaited so the rejection was silent; `#rp-slot` never painted. Fix, cardio.html: deleted the duplicate `logCardio`; added `rpKvGet`/`rpKvSet` as thin wrappers over `VYVELocalDB._kv` (key `running_plan:<email>`, no Dexie version bump ŌĆö the `_kv` table already existed); restored `getActiveRunningPlan()` as the `member_running_plans` fetch returning `{ok,plan}`; rewrote `renderRunningPlan` to paint the callout synchronously first. Also engagement.html in the same commit: `refreshEngagement` built `score` as a local and rendered it but never assigned `data.score` before caching `data` ŌĆö every cached reopen read `score=undefined` and `renderScoreHero` threw at `score.total`; the throw was swallowed, skeleton fell through to an un-timed `await refreshEngagement`. Fixed: `data.score` persisted; `renderScoreHero` baseline-tolerant; loadPage reveals via a local paint.

**PM-143 (`81144bb8`) ŌĆö read Dexie first, hydrate in background.** Both engagement.html and index.html `await`ed `criticalHydrate(page)` ŌĆö a multi-table network pull, 5s-per-table timeout ŌĆö BEFORE the first Dexie read. The "instant local paint" was gated behind the network. Restructured both: `buildEngagementFromDexie` / `buildHomeFromDexie` called immediately and painted; `criticalHydrate` fired un-awaited in the background with a repaint on resolve.

**PM-144 (`0d4933b8`) ŌĆö on-screen diagnostic probe** added to engagement.html (read-only strip printing VYVELocalDB/VYVESync/VYVEHomeStateLocal state, resolved email, raw Dexie row counts, `computeHomeStateFromDexie` return). Same technique as the PM-132 movement probe. Stripped in PM-147.

**PM-146 (`50e7332c`) ŌĆö THE ROOT CAUSE.** The PM-144 probe reported, on device: `VYVELocalDB present: false`, `VYVESync present: false`, `VYVEHomeStateLocal present: false` ŌĆö at `loadPage()` time. engagement.html's main `<script>` block (opens ~L485) is INLINE ŌĆö no `defer`. It runs during HTML parsing and called `loadPage()` bare at the end. db.js / sync.js / firstPaintHydrate.js / home-state-local.js are all `<script defer>`, which by spec do NOT execute until parsing completes. So `loadPage`'s Dexie-first block ran while all three globals were still `undefined` ŌåÆ `buildEngagementFromDexie` returned null every load ŌåÆ page fell to the PM-142 zero-state, and only the EF could fill it. Every PM-142/143 fix was correct in code and inert on device for this one reason. Fix: `loadPage()` gated on `DOMContentLoaded` (fires after all deferred scripts per spec; calls immediately if parsing already finished). index.html was never affected ŌĆö its loader is gated on the `vyveAuthReady` event, which fires post-parse. Probe confirmed green on device after PM-146: `VYVELocalDB present: true`, `buildEngagementFromDexie(): OBJECT score=74`.

**PM-147 (`05fabadb`) ŌĆö Dexie 2/day cap + probe removal.** `computeHomeStateFromDexie`'s all-time `workoutsTotal`/`cardioTotal` were a raw `.length` ŌĆö the only two counts in the function bypassing `cappedDailyCount` (sessions-total and the monthly workout/cardio counts were already capped). Supabase caps workouts/cardio at 2/day via a BEFORE INSERT trigger (over-cap rows ŌåÆ `activity_dedupe`); Dexie stores every optimistic row uncapped, so a glitched multi-fire inflated the engagement counts vs server. Fix: both totals now use `cappedDailyCount(..., null, null)` ŌĆö ╬Ż days `min(2,n)`. `cappedDailyCount` groups by `row.activity_date`, the same column the server trigger caps on, so the two agree by construction. Raw Dexie rows stay uncapped (the cap is a counting rule, not a storage rule ŌĆö mirrors server-side `activity_dedupe`). PM-144 probe stripped in the same commit.

**Confirmed on device (Dean):** cardio running-plan callout paints instantly; engagement paints progress cards from Dexie instantly (probe green); PM-146 fix verified.

**Not closed / open:** PM-145 ŌĆö `member-dashboard` EF (v69) is 504-ing (150s timeouts in edge-function logs; `member-achievements` 100s+; the inline achievements payload over 23 evaluators is the likely cause). Edge-case/reinstall path, needs its own session. PM-137 ŌĆö the `server.url` WKWebView cache layer cost a variable 2-15min lag on every Update this session; the real dev-loop bottleneck, worth a dedicated fix. The underlying workout-logging multi-fire (one tap ŌåÆ 5-6 inserts) is unfixed ŌĆö PM-147 caps the count but the phantom rows remain; separate investigation.

**New hard rules:** ┬¦23.30 (inline scripts must not call code depending on `defer` scripts ŌĆö gate on `DOMContentLoaded`), ┬¦23.31 (Dexie-derived counts must apply the same caps the server enforces).

## 2026-05-16 PM-128ŌĆō133 (Updates 1ŌĆō6) ŌĆö Local-first paint & write-path fixes: cardio cold-paint + instant logging, build banner, movement root-cause fix

**Session shape:** Debugging session on the vyve-site portal, all via `server.url` dev-loop on Dean's iPhone. Six device-visible commits shipped, labelled "Update 1ŌĆō6" in the home build banner (PM-128 through PM-133 in commit messages/brain). All to VYVEHealth/vyve-site main, each node --check'd + post-commit byte-equal verified, sw.js cache key bumped in the same atomic commit.

**Symptom ŌåÆ diagnosis arc.** Reported: cardio + habits + movement skeleton on cold reopen; cardio logging "stuck on LoggingŌĆ”". The diagnosis moved through several wrong theories (replaceForMember wipe; await-ordering alone; cache TTL) before an on-screen build banner and a movement on-screen probe pinned the real causes. Key correction mid-session: the dev-loop build DOES have a caching layer (updates took 3-4 cold reopens to land, banner showed stale "Update N" ŌĆö `Update 1` on first open, `Update 2` after a page navigation, on the same page) despite the banner's `sw` field reporting `no-sw`. The earlier claim that the `server.url` build has no caching was wrong. Layer not definitively identified (WKWebView HTTP cache vs Capacitor bundle) ŌĆö capacitor.config.json read started, not finished.

**Shipped (vyve-site main):**
- **Update 1 / PM-128** (`3c3730b1`) ŌĆö cache TTL fix: cardio.html 10minŌåÆ24h, movement.html 30minŌåÆ24h. The short TTL meant `readCache()` discarded the localStorage paint-cache on any cold reopen >TTL since last visit, leaving no local tier to paint from ŌåÆ skeleton. 24h matches habits.html.
- **Update 2 / PM-129** (`00519049`) ŌĆö build banner iOS safe-area fix: banner text was rendering behind the Dynamic Island. `padding-top` now adds `env(safe-area-inset-top)`; body padding follows.
- **Update 3 / PM-130** (`06a48f3d`) ŌĆö cardio logCardio() rewritten optimistic-first: Dexie write + bus publish + repaint before the network POST (POST now background/un-awaited). Fixed "stuck on Logging". 4xx reverts the optimistic row; network error keeps it.
- **Update 4 / PM-131** (`f292af3e`) ŌĆö cardio log: removed the residual ~1s "LoggingŌĆ”" pause. Root cause: logCardio step 4 (button flip) sat AFTER step 3 (`await Promise.all([fetchWeek(),fetchHistory()])`), and fetchHistory L598 still `await`ed criticalHydrate ŌĆö a network round-trip gating the button. Fix: button flip + input clear moved BEFORE the repaint; repaint made non-blocking `.then()`; un-awaited the last criticalHydrate. cardio now has zero awaited criticalHydrate.
- **Update 5 / PM-132** (`9067e814`) ŌĆö on-screen diagnostic probe added to movement.html: dark-red strip showing workout_plan_cache Dexie row count, fetchPlan result, localStorage cache hit/miss, reveal source. Read-only instrumentation.
- **Update 6 / PM-133** (`db4156835d713c8d677dc4957f8e6de613ed81bc`) ŌĆö MOVEMENT ROOT FIX: the probe reported `dexie rows:dexie-off` ŌĆö movement.html never loaded db.js / sync.js / firstPaintHydrate.js, so `window.VYVELocalDB` was undefined and EVERY Dexie branch in movement was unreachable dead code; the page was permanently network-bound. Fix: inserted the 3 missing `<script src>` tags after bus.js, before vyve-home-state.js, matching cardio.html's chain. Movement's existing (API-correct) Dexie code now executes. **Shipped + byte-equal verified ŌĆö NOT yet device-confirmed by Dean.**

**Confirmed working on device (Dean):** cardio paints instantly; cardio logging is instant (Update 4); build banner readable + accurate.

**Not yet confirmed / still open:** movement (Update 6) device test pending ŌĆö probe should now show `dexie rows:0/1` not `dexie-off`. Movement probe instrumentation still in movement.html ŌĆö strip in a later cleanup commit once confirmed. Running plan card on cardio (`member_running_plans`, no local tier) still broken ŌĆö separate issue. Delete-a-cardio-session feature designed (swipe-to-delete + undo toast, optimistic, revert-on-failed-server-delete) but NOT built. Caching-layer identity still unresolved.

**Architecture discussion (deferred to its own session):** Dean proposed bundling catalogue data (exercise plan templates, exercise thumbnails) into the app binary + shipping updates via OTA (Capawesome) rather than via Supabase; member data stays on the DexieŌåÆSupabase write loop; bus handles cross-page updates. Confirmed largely correct and consistent with the existing PF-40.6/40.7/40.8 asset-tiering plan (Tier 1 bundled / Tier 2 pre-fetched / Tier 3 on-demand). Refinement: bundle plan TEMPLATES (catalogue); which plan a member is on + their week/session position stays member-data (Dexie+Supabase pointer). 100MB app size not a concern. To be scoped as its own session.

## 2026-05-15 PM-122 ŌĆö Fast-paint: 7 hydrate() ŌåÆ criticalHydrate, defer habits autotick, sync.js 8s timeout (┬¦23.5.1 Layer 5 long tail closed)

**Session shape:** Two vyve-site commits this session ŌĆö `21b603be` (BAD, ship 1, single-file upserts with literal `<PLACEHOLDER_EXERCISE>` content ŌĆö exercise.html replaced with 22-byte placeholder on production for ~90s) and `6911b55a` (CORRECTIVE, ship 2, restored exercise.html + landed the other 9 files in one atomic upsert). Brain commit closes the session, including a new ┬¦23.26 PLACEHOLDER GUARD rule that codifies the ship-discipline failure that produced ship 1.

**The actual PM-122 work ŌĆö closes PM-117 P0-1 (2.11g) carry:** Audit at vyve-site `3ce9c72f` found 7 stale `await VYVESync.hydrate()` call sites across 6 pages still using the 81s sequential 25-table sync.js mass-hydrate. Each missed page was sitting on a blank screen for up to 81s before painting on cold load. Not architectural ŌĆö incomplete PM-112 migration. PM-112 introduced per-page 2-7 table parallel `criticalHydrate` with 5s per-request timeout and wired index + habits + workouts + nutrition + cardio + engagement, but missed: exercise / settings / certificates / monthly-checkin / movement / wellbeing-checkin (6 pages, 7 call sites).

**Ship 2 contents (`6911b55a`, 10 files):**

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| exercise.html L308 | `hydrate()` ŌåÆ `criticalHydrate('workouts')` |
| settings.html L1100 | `hydrate()` ŌåÆ `criticalHydrate('settings')` (new page key) |
| certificates.html L406 | `hydrate()` ŌåÆ `criticalHydrate('engagement')` |
| monthly-checkin.html L673 | `hydrate()` ŌåÆ `criticalHydrate('home')` |
| movement.html L352 | `hydrate()` ŌåÆ `criticalHydrate('workouts')` |
| wellbeing-checkin.html L1014+L1146 | both `hydrate()` ŌåÆ `criticalHydrate('home')` (2 sites, batch replace 2ŌåÆ2) |
| firstPaintHydrate.js | new `settings` page key in `plansForPage(email)` return ŌĆö MEMBERS + HABIT_LIBRARY + MEMBER_HABITS; inline docstring updated |
| habits.html | Fix 3 ŌĆö defer fetchDashboardHabits (autotick EF). `const dashboardPromise = fetchDashboardHabits()` kicked off ONCE before any of the 3 branches; each branch's Promise.all loses fetchDashboardHabits + sets dashboardPayload=null; synchronous render path runs as before (autotickMap empty ŌåÆ cards paint immediately, runAutotickPass no-ops on zero candidates); NEW post-render `dashboardPromise.then(...)` rebuilds autotickMap, applies metadata, runs runAutotickPass (which now finds candidates), re-sorts, re-renders, refreshes localStorage cache. PM-98 write-optimistic autotick pattern preserved. |
| sync.js authedFetch L88-103 | Wrapped in AbortController with 8000ms timeout. Clears timer in .finally. AbortError ŌåÆ labelled rejection. Pure backstop ŌĆö happy path unchanged. |
| sw.js | Cache key `vyve-cache-v2026-05-15-pm120-workouts-session-optimistic-a` ŌåÆ `vyve-cache-v2026-05-15-pm122-fast-paint-a`. |

**Pre-flight:** `node --check` rc=0 on all 3 pure-JS files (firstPaintHydrate.js, sync.js, sw.js); inline-script extract + `node --check` rc=0 on all 7 patched HTMLs. All `str_replace` targets asserted unique-match single-occurrence before patch; wellbeing-checkin's 2-occurrence needle was an intentional 2ŌåÆ2 batch verified post-patch. vyve-site main HEAD `3ce9c72f` re-verified unchanged immediately pre-ship-1 (┬¦23.14). Post-commit byte-equal verification at `6911b55a`: all 10 files local-string == Contents-API-base64-decode, no length mismatches, no char-level diffs (┬¦23.15).

**Ship 1 ŌĆö `21b603be` ŌĆö the placeholder ship-fail:** Constructed the `COMPOSIO_MULTI_EXECUTE_TOOL` args inline with a JSON `upserts: [{path: "exercise.html", content: "<PLACEHOLDER_EXERCISE>", sha: ...}]` array as a "template I'll populate". Fired the call without populating. GitHub accepted it (the call was well-formed, the placeholder is valid UTF-8) and committed exercise.html as a 22-byte file. Discovered immediately when the commit response came back with `changed_paths: ['exercise.html']` (only one file ŌĆö should have been ten). Re-fetched the new exercise.html SHA `7abc10a9` (post-placeholder), built a complete 10-file upserts dict in the workbench using `patched[path]` references rather than inline JSON, fired the corrective commit via `run_composio_tool`. Window of corrupted state on production: ~90 seconds.

**┬¦23.26 codified:** PLACEHOLDER GUARD ŌĆö never build an MCP tool call with a literal placeholder string as a field meant to carry real content. The rule, the failure mode that surfaced it, the workbench-first pre-flight assertions to prevent recurrence, and the recovery posture if it happens again are all in master.md ┬¦23.26. Key escape-clause: any time you find yourself writing `"content": "<...>"` or similar in a tool-call JSON args block, STOP. Build the payload to a variable first; assert each entry against the source-of-truth dict; THEN fire.

**What this ship explicitly does NOT do:**
- Does NOT touch backend EF latency (member-dashboard EF still 10-30s cold). Fix 3 just unbinds habits.html paint from the slow EF; the EF itself is unchanged. ┬¦23.5.1 backend EF latency campaign is the next session's stated P0.
- Does NOT retire System B (vyve_*_cache_* localStorage prewarmers). Open decision deferred.
- Does NOT touch activity.html ŌĆö EF-only by design, out of scope.
- Does NOT address first-ever-cold-load (Dexie empty by definition ŌĆö must populate once).

**┬¦23.5.1 Layer accounting:** Layer 1 bus migration (DONE PM-30..PM-44). Layer 2 Realtime bridge (PM-45 deferred). Layer 3-4 Premium Feel local-first (PM-77+). Layer 5 criticalHydrate migration (STARTED PM-112, FINISHED PM-122 except activity.html ŌĆö EF-only-by-design). Layer 6 SPA shell (permanently dropped). Layer 5 long tail closed.

Expected: (a) exercise / settings / certificates / monthly-checkin / movement / wellbeing-checkin paint in 5-15s on cold (was 30-81s same network), (b) habits.html paints card list immediately (cache-first already worked), autotick badges land second 10-30s later (EF still slow but no longer gates paint). ## 2026-05-15 PM-121 ŌĆö Tool discovery brain commit: `tool_search` does not surface Composio toolkits, route via `COMPOSIO_SEARCH_TOOLS`

**Session shape:** Not a vyve-site ship. Brain-only commit, no portal changes. Codifies a workflow gotcha that cost the first ~3 minutes of this session and would have cost every subsequent session the same amount until written down. Dean asked for real brainstorming on a separate topic after this commit lands; this is the housekeeping pass that closes the tool-discovery loop first.

**The symptom:** Session opened with Dean's standard "Load VYVE brain". I called `tool_search(query="github get repository content")` expecting to load `GITHUB_GET_REPOSITORY_CONTENT` directly ŌĆö the way it worked in PM-114 through PM-120. It returned only Google Drive write tools and one Supabase first-party tool. Three more `tool_search` variants ("github file content fetch", "GITHUB_GET_REPOSITORY_CONTENT" as literal slug, etc.) returned the same set. I told Dean I couldn't reach GitHub.

**Dean's response was correct:** "this always just normally works. It's just worked in the last chat, and it's worked for the last six months." He was right ŌĆö the underlying connection was active the whole time. The failure was in the discovery layer, not the connection.

**Root cause:** `tool_search(query="composio github repository")` ŌĆö adding the literal word "composio" ŌĆö returned the seven Composio meta-tools (`COMPOSIO_SEARCH_TOOLS`, `COMPOSIO_MULTI_EXECUTE_TOOL`, `COMPOSIO_REMOTE_WORKBENCH`, etc.). From there the working pattern is: `COMPOSIO_SEARCH_TOOLS` to get the plan + tool schemas, `COMPOSIO_MULTI_EXECUTE_TOOL` to actually invoke `GITHUB_GET_REPOSITORY_CONTENT` / `GITHUB_COMMIT_MULTIPLE_FILES` / etc. The host generation in this session routes Composio-mediated toolkits through Composio's own meta-tools rather than surfacing the underlying GitHub MCP slugs directly through `tool_search`.

**Why this surfaced now:** likely a host-side change between the last session and this one. Probably won't revert. Whatever the cause, codifying the routing pattern is cheaper than re-discovering it every time, and the symptom (host claims it can't reach a connected service) is exactly the shape that destroys session trust in the way Dean experienced today.

**Pre-flight discipline:**

- VYVEBrain main HEAD pinned at session start: `068266becmd0407792ef54c9b3978d3b73211a763c3` (PM-120 session-handoff commit).
- master.md size 450,443 chars; ┬¦23 tail is ┬¦23.24 (PM-116 ship); next rule number is ┬¦23.25.
- changelog.md size 1,374,669 chars ŌĆö confirmed >1MB Contents API limit; fetched via raw-at-branch (acceptable for read-only load, current rule ┬¦23.15 stale-by-minutes caveat applies but we are not mutating these reads).
- Confirmed via `COMPOSIO_SEARCH_TOOLS` that the GitHub Composio connection is `ACTIVE` under account `github_peste-herse`, VYVEHealth user, account_type `PRIVATE`. Connection health is not the issue ŌĆö discovery is.

**Brain changes shipped this commit:**

1. **`brain/master.md` ┬¦23.25 appended** ŌĆö new hard rule codifying the tool-discovery routing pattern, response-handling quirks (1MB Contents API limit + raw-at-SHA workaround, base64 newline stripping), connection-status check via `toolkit_connection_statuses`, and explicit generality clause covering all Composio-mediated toolkits (not just GitHub). Sits below ┬¦23.24.

2. **`brain/active.md` ┬¦4 patched** ŌĆö appended a one-line pointer to ┬¦23.25 under "Commit discipline" so the rule is visible from the working-set load. The full rule lives in master.md.

3. **`brain/changelog.md` PM-121 prepended** ŌĆö this entry.

**Open hygiene carried forward (unchanged from PM-120 session handoff):**

- Device walk for PM-118 + PM-119 + PM-120 still pending. Dean to cold-boot iPhone in airplane mode, walk workouts.html and engagement.html, report back.
- Dual-cache (System B) retirement decision still Dean's call, still untouched.
- `home` page key in firstPaintHydrate.js still missing `WELLBEING_CHECKINS_30D` + `WEEKLY_GOALS` ŌĆö P1 ~10 min follow-up in backlog.
- Severity-drift ┬¦23 codification candidate (audit JSON severity ŌēĀ narrative priority ŌĆö narrative wins for scheduling, JSON severity for accounting) ŌĆö still INFO in backlog, still awaiting a co-rule landing to avoid a lone-rule brain commit. PM-121 itself is a lone rule, but it is acutely operational (every future session is affected) rather than a tactical pattern (severity drift only matters during audit-fix sessions), so the bar for promoting it earlier is met.

**No new ┬¦23 rules beyond ┬¦23.25 itself.** No vyve-site changes. No PM-117 audit findings affected.

**Next:** Dean has flagged "real brainstorming about fixing this" as the next topic ŌĆö scope unclear from the brief, will surface when he opens the conversation. Likely either (a) further tooling robustness work, (b) the deferred device walks, or (c) something else entirely. Brain is up to date and ready for either direction.

## 2026-05-15 PM-120 ŌĆö PM-117 audit P0 #4 SHIPPED: workouts-session.js Dexie-first writes + criticalHydrate wire (atomic 2-file commit `3ce9c72f`)

**Session shape:** Direct execution against PM-117's prioritised fix list item #4. Closes the root cause of PF-31 ("workouts page re-entry ŌĆö green check has DISAPPEARED") at the source: 3 writeQueued sites with no synchronous Dexie write. ~50 min from "go" to shipped + brain committed.

**Pre-flight discipline:**

- vyve-site main HEAD pinned at session start: `17318f12d0c737dc8df8095beb16a4737ea867de` (PM-119 ship from this session).
- workouts-session.js blob `db76ea28` (50251 utf-8 bytes) ŌĆö matches the PM-117 audit's pinned blob SHA exactly, zero drift.
- Verified `workouts.html` script chain (L562-572) loads `db.js` ŌåÆ `sync.js` ŌåÆ `firstPaintHydrate.js` ŌåÆ `workouts-config.js` ŌåÆ `workouts-programme.js` ŌåÆ `workouts-session.js`. So `VYVELocalDB` and `VYVESync` globals are guaranteed defined before workouts-session.js runs ŌĆö no script-tag changes needed in workouts.html.
- Verified `VYVEData.writeQueued` (vyve-offline.js L260) has ZERO Dexie integration ŌĆö only writes to localStorage outbox + network. This is what makes every writeQueued site a read-after-write hazard from Dexie's perspective. The PM-117 audit's diagnosis "writeQueued without preceding synchronous VYVELocalDB write ŌĆö read-after-write hazard" is correct: writeQueued does not shadow-write to Dexie at all.
- Verified all 3 target Dexie tables exist with appropriate keyPaths: `workouts` (keyPath `id`, secondary index `[member_email+activity_date]`), `exercise_logs` (keyPath `id`, secondary index `[member_email+activity_date]`), `workout_plan_cache` (keyPath `member_email` ŌĆö singleton row per member). Confirms the merge-vs-replace distinction for site 3.
- Cardio.html PF-9 read as the reference pattern (L792-819): optimistic Dexie upsert post-network-call, catch+warn non-fatal, comment block noting future PF-15 shadow-drainer retirement.

**Severity drift:**

The audit JSON tagged all 3 write_bypass findings on workouts-session.js as P1; the audit narrative + Dean's brief named them as the next P0 (priority list item #4). PM-120 treated as P0 closures per the narrative source-of-truth principle. New backlog INFO item surfaced: codify in ┬¦23 that when audit JSON severity ŌēĀ narrative priority, narrative wins for scheduling, JSON severity stays for audit-progress accounting.

**Part A ŌĆö L548 sync.hydrate ŌåÆ criticalHydrate('workouts'):**

This was a bonus fix outside the original priority list ŌĆö PM-118 fixed the four `await VYVESync.hydrate()` sites in workouts-programme.js (L82/L263/L368/L409 of that file). workouts-session.js has its OWN sync.hydrate call at L548 that PM-118 didn't catch because the PM-118 brief and the PM-117 audit narrative both scoped the fix to workouts-programme.js only. The audit JSON `per_file_audit` entry for workouts-session.js listed `sync_hydrate_calls: [{line: 548}]` and `localdb_refs: [{line: 550, table: 'workouts', op: 'allFor', kind: 'read'}]` ŌĆö a Dexie read-after-hydrate that was on the slow 81s mass-hydrate path. Wired to criticalHydrate('workouts') here so the workouts page key (workout_plan_cache + workouts[30d]) hydrates in parallel with 5s timeout instead of awaiting the full mass-hydrate. Same shape as PM-118.

**Part B ŌĆö L422 exercise_logs POST optimistic upsert:**

Inserted after `await VYVEData.writeQueued({...})` and before `_publishSetLogged()` so bus subscribers (`set:logged`) see the row in Dexie. Full payload `{id: payload.client_id, member_email, exercise_name, activity_date, sets_completed, reps_completed, weight_kg, logged_at, client_id}`. Wrapped in defensive `try { ŌĆ” upsert(...).catch(warn) ŌĆ” } catch(warn) {}` so any failure is non-fatal. Fire-and-forget ŌĆö no `await`, the write returns a Promise but we don't gate the function on it.

**Part C ŌĆö L605 workouts POST optimistic upsert:**

Same shape as Part B, with the additional discipline of `!_workoutWriteResult.dead` gating: PM-63 Layer 4 added a `dead` flag to writeQueued return values for 4xx terminal errors (vs queued 5xx/network) so the caller can fire `workout:failed` eagerly and `VYVEHomeState.revertPatch` can undo the optimistic patch within one paint frame. The Dexie upsert is gated on the same dead-check ŌĆö if we know the row will never reach the server (4xx), we don't want it in local Dexie either, otherwise revertPatch and Dexie would disagree. Queued (5xx/network) and ok (2xx) paths both upsert.

**Part D ŌĆö L707 workout_plan_cache PATCH merged upsert:**

The interesting one. workout_plan_cache is keyed by member_email (singleton row per member) and stores the programme JSONB, plan_duration_weeks, generated_at, plus the two counters `current_session` and `current_week`. The PATCH only sends `{current_session, current_week}` ŌĆö those are the only fields the user mutated. A plain `VYVELocalDB.workout_plan_cache.upsert({member_email, current_session, current_week})` would `db[table].put(stamp(row))` and **replace** the entire Dexie row, dropping programme + plan_duration_weeks + generated_at + everything else.

This is the same hazard codified for `member_habits` and `members` partial-upsert merges in db.js (PM-97 PF-15 ┬¦23.7.5). Those tables have custom `upsert` overrides that read-modify-write inside a transaction. `workout_plan_cache` doesn't have such an override (it uses the default `makeTable` factory), so PM-120 implements the merge inline via `VYVELocalDB.raw()` ŌåÆ `db.transaction('rw', db.workout_plan_cache, function () { ... })` ŌĆö open a R/W transaction, get existing row, Object.assign merge, put. Touched_at stamped explicitly. Fire-and-forget per the pattern.

Alternative considered: add a custom `upsert` override to db.js workout_plan_cache like the existing member_habits / members overrides. Rejected as out-of-scope for PM-120 ŌĆö that would be a db.js schema-API change affecting every caller of workout_plan_cache.upsert (currently zero callers, but any future caller would inherit the merge semantic). Adding it inline at the call site keeps the change contained. Backlog item: if a second workout_plan_cache partial-upsert appears, promote the merge override into db.js then. Until then, inline is fine.

**Part E ŌĆö sw.js cache key bump:**

`vyve-cache-v2026-05-15-pm119-engagement-dexie-a` ŌåÆ `vyve-cache-v2026-05-15-pm120-workouts-session-optimistic-a`. No precache additions (workouts-session.js was added to precache by PM-118).

**Pre-commit verification:**

- `node --check workouts-session.patched.js` rc=0 Ō£ō.
- `node --check sw.patched.pm120.js` rc=0 Ō£ō.
- Structural verification: 1├Ś `VYVESync.criticalHydrate('workouts')`, 0├Ś legacy `VYVESync.hydrate()`, 1├Ś `VYVELocalDB.exercise_logs.upsert({`, 1├Ś `VYVELocalDB.workouts.upsert({`, 1├Ś `db.workout_plan_cache.put(merged)`, 6├Ś `[PM-120]` markers (3 sites ├Ś {warn, throw}). Each optimistic upsert verified to land after its corresponding writeQueued (deltas +12, +12, +22 lines respectively ŌĆö no transposition).
- vyve-site main HEAD re-verified `17318f12` immediately before commit per ┬¦23.14 ŌĆö unchanged from session start.

**Ship:**

- Atomic 2-file commit via `GITHUB_COMMIT_MULTIPLE_FILES` from Composio workbench (plain UTF-8 in upserts[].content per PM-86.1/87 rule).
- Result: `3ce9c72f255bcb4aab666971ec5acbb16c96dbe8`. New tree `8bc4c0e5`. retry_count 0. Blobs: workouts-session.js `7ffe1f21`, sw.js `5ddca97b`.

**Post-commit byte-equal verification (┬¦23.15, Contents API only):**

- `workouts-session.js` at `ref=3ce9c72f`: 54538 utf-8 bytes Ō£ō. SHA-256 `cb22ef56ŌĆ”77b5355d` matches local Ō£ō.
- `sw.js` at `ref=3ce9c72f`: 8957 utf-8 bytes Ō£ō. SHA-256 `07197ab9ŌĆ”84fcab60` matches local Ō£ō.
- Spot-checks against remote: 1├Ś criticalHydrate('workouts'), 0├Ś legacy hydrate(), 1├Ś exercise_logs.upsert, 1├Ś workouts.upsert, 1├Ś workout_plan_cache merge put, new cache key present + old absent.

**Closed by this ship (from PM-117 audit findings):**

- L422 write_bypass on workouts-session.js (audit JSON: P1; narrative: P0 #4 part 1).
- L605 write_bypass on workouts-session.js (audit JSON: P1; narrative: P0 #4 part 2).
- L707 write_bypass on workouts-session.js (audit JSON: P1; narrative: P0 #4 part 3).
- Bonus: L548 sync.hydrate ŌåÆ criticalHydrate('workouts') wire (not flagged as a separate finding but visible in per-file `sync_hydrate_calls`; closes the workouts page on the slow mass-hydrate path).

**Cumulative across PM-118 + PM-119 + PM-120:** 16 of 46 audit findings closed (11 by PM-118 ŌĆö 10├Ś sw_precache_gap P0s + 1├Ś workouts critical_hydrate_missing P0; 2 by PM-119 ŌĆö both engagement.html P0s; 3 by PM-120 ŌĆö three write_bypass P1s, plus L548 hydrate wire as bonus). Per audit JSON severities: 13 P0 + 3 P1 = 16. Per narrative priority: items #1, #2 (PM-118), #3 (PM-119), #4 (PM-120) = first 4 of 5 priorities.

Per narrative priority: next is item #5 ŌĆö log-food.html 4 writes bypass Dexie via in-memory `diaryLogs[]` JS array + `saveDiaryCache()` localStorage (~2 hr). Read-after-write hazard: log food ŌåÆ close app ŌåÆ reopen ŌåÆ Dexie has no record until queue drains.

The new Dexie merge runs in addition, not in replacement.

## 2026-05-15 PM-119 ŌĆö PM-117 audit P0 #3 SHIPPED: engagement.html Dexie-first wire (atomic 3-file commit `17318f12`)

**Session shape:** Direct execution against PM-117's prioritised fix list item #3. Closes the master.md ┬¦3 violation flagged by the audit: engagement.html had ZERO VYVELocalDB refs, ZERO criticalHydrate, ZERO sync.hydrate ŌĆö score ring entirely server-bound via member-dashboard EF. ~75 min from "go" to shipped + brain committed.

**Approach decision ŌĆö option (a) over option (b):**

The backlog entry written by PM-117 recommended option (b): reuse the existing `home` page key by calling `criticalHydrate('home')` from engagement.html, on the grounds that engagement is largely a subset of home. I went with option (a): create a separate `engagement` page key. Reasons:

- A separate page key is more explicit about what data engagement.html actually needs. Coupling to `home`'s key would mean every future home-view evolution affects engagement and vice-versa.
- The new key let me add `WELLBEING_CHECKINS_30D` to the critical hydrate set specifically ŌĆö closing a real gap. The engagement score has a wellbeing component (12.5 of 100 pts) computed from latest wellbeing_checkin; without WELLBEING_CHECKINS_30D in critical hydrate, that component lands as 0 on first paint until lazy mass-hydrate populates. With (b) the gap stays.
- Cost is trivial: one extra page-key entry in a return block.

**Pre-flight discipline:**

- Brain pre-load: `brain/active.md` + `brain/changelog.md` (head only via raw S3) + `tasks/backlog.md` + audit narrative `.md` + audit structured `.json`. Audit JSON cross-referenced for engagement findings (3├Ś P0 directly relevant: `hydrate_missing_page_key`, `no_dexie_wiring`, `architectural_drift`).
- vyve-site main HEAD pinned: `e8df0dbd6a0336fb2d18f3b1232cc1301f59f0de` (PM-118 ship from earlier this session).
- Live file fetches at pinned SHA: `engagement.html` blob `d6b5e99f` (90695 bytes; matches the audit's pinned blob SHA ŌĆö no drift); `firstPaintHydrate.js` blob `1ff55523` (13838 bytes ŌĆö same blob as pre-PM-118 since PM-118 didn't touch it); `sw.js` blob `99483e78` (8938 bytes ŌĆö the PM-118 file with `vyve-cache-v2026-05-15-pm118-precache-a` cache key); `home-state-local.js` blob `8c3200f9` (25232 bytes); plus `index.html` and `db.js` for pattern reference.
- Confirmed via grep that engagement.html had 0 matches each for `VYVELocalDB`, `criticalHydrate`, `VYVESync`, `/db.js`, `/sync.js`, `/firstPaintHydrate.js` ŌĆö audit's "0 of everything" classification accurate at HEAD.
- Confirmed `home-state-local.js` already in sw.js precache (L47 of post-PM-118 sw.js ŌĆö was added pre-PM-118). No precache additions needed for PM-119.
- Confirmed `wellbeing_checkins` + `weekly_goals` both first-class Dexie tables in db.js (L209-210 schema; L464/L466 makeTable; L124-125 in the source-of-truth `vyve_*` table list).
- Confirmed `VYVEHomeStateLocal.computeHomeStateFromDexie` is the public API on `window.VYVEHomeStateLocal` (home-state-local.js L522-530).

**Part A ŌĆö firstPaintHydrate.js:**

Superset of `home` (7 tables) ŌĆö `home` page key remains missing wellbeing_checkins + weekly_goals (known gap, added to backlog as P1 follow-up).

**Part B ŌĆö engagement.html:**

- 4 new `<script src>` tags injected after `<script src="/achievements.js" defer></script>` (between the achievements load and `</head>`): `/db.js`, `/sync.js`, `/firstPaintHydrate.js`, `/home-state-local.js`. All `defer`. Order mirrors index.html L306-309 (PF-11b reference pattern). Inline HTML comment block documents the PM-119 wire-in.
- New `buildEngagementFromDexie(email)` async helper function (~70 LOC) injected just above `async function loadPage() {` (anchored to the unique `// ---- Main load ----` comment). Returns `{counts, streaks, checkinStreak, score, activityLog, __pm119_dexie_source: true, __computed_at}` ŌĆö the same shape that `loadPage`'s EF translation block (L856 onwards in the original) produces, so the existing render functions (`renderScoreHero`, `renderStreaksFromPrecomputed`, `renderActivityGridFromPrecomputed`, `renderLogFromPrecomputed`) consume it without modification. Returns `null` on any Dexie unavailability (no email, no LocalDB, no Sync, no compute module, no state row yet) so caller falls through to EF-only path. The function calls `VYVEHomeStateLocal.computeHomeStateFromDexie(email)` (same call index.html uses), then reshapes (streaks naming: home returns `workouts_*` keys, engagement renderer expects `workout` singular ŌĆö mapped explicitly). activityLog is rebuilt from raw rows via parallel `VYVELocalDB.*.allFor(email)` reads (same logic the EF uses server-side).
- Dexie-first paint block injected into `loadPage()` between the localStorage cache early-paint (`} catch(_) {}` at the cache-first try end) and the EF fetch try (`try { // Network refresh path`). Block fires `VYVESync.criticalHydrate('engagement').catch(() => null).then(() => buildEngagementFromDexie(email))`; on success renders all four panels and sets `_renderedFromDexie = true`; dispatches `vyvePaintDone { source: 'dexie' }` for telemetry alignment with index.html PF-11b.
